In Semitic languages (including Arabic) all words are derived from tri-literal roots.
2 is the first number in Arabic. From 2 came myriad forms. From 1 came 2 (1+1), and then 3 is the beginning of multiplicity.  This is why language, which is inexhaustible in nature, is built upon 3. A constellation of meanings emerges out of primitive root structures based on 3 letters.
 
Quran comes from ق ر ی ..... ….. Which means city. And city creates civilization. Quran is the image of civilization.
The image is that within the walls of the city you have civilization and outside is jungle. It civilized barbaric people.
 
Civilization are normally built on books. Interestingly Arabs even though they are an ancient civilization, they did not have the concept of books until the Quran. They only had oral poetry. So Quran was a radical departure from previous concepts of knowledge.
If you do not have oral tradition you cannot have a literal tradition. Orality is the process of humanization. Quran is oral before it is a book. QARAA means to recite as well as to read.
 
Book KITAAB….. Comes from the root word KATABA….. Which means to join together
 
The Arabs would send their children to these buddoos for eloquence training. Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) until he turned 40 we don’t know much about his life.
He was kind to orphans and poor. He was reflective and contemplative. He was very quiet person. He was sadiq and ameen.
He belonged to an aristocratic clan that was in bad times
He used to go to جبل النور..... ….. Mountain of lights….. He was engaged in تَحَنُّث….. Which means to avoid idols (polythesiams)
 
In the 40th year in the month of Ramadan he had an angelic experience. A angel came in human form and said اقرأ X3. He said I don't know how to read/recite. ما أنا بقاری….. So Jibraeel squeezed him until he thought his sides would burst.
He said….. Read in the name of your master who created you from a clinging tissue; taught you with the pen; taught you that which you did not know
When this revelation came on the prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) he became concerned that he might have been possessed. So he went to his wife Hazrat Khadeeja Kubra (older women), and narrated the incident to her. She then went and spoke with her uncle Warqa bin Naufal, who was familiar with revelation. After hearing the words he said this is NAMOOS….. Which comes from the greed word Nomus which means "LAW". He said this is similar to what was given to Moosa AS. He warned the prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) that once you start delivering this message people will become your enemies
 
After that the revelations started coming fairly regularly. He described it like the vibrations of a ringing bell. If you read the مقطعات such as ALIF LAAAM MEEM….. They have a vibration to them. This is because revelation was moving into physicality from a different realm. Divine meanings were getting embodied in words.
Quran is the uncreated speech of God. It is his eternal meanings that move into the vehicle of language
The next revelation was یا ایھا المزمل….. The one who is wrapped. Because Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) would wrap himself in a blanket when he would meditate.
 
Rasul Allah was asked to invite his family to the word of Allah. Maulatina Khadeeja was the first to accept Islam followed by Maulana Ali. After that he invited his family members who were Quraish. He asked them, "What if I said there is an army on the other side of this mountain Qubais (جبل القبیس). Would you believe me?". They all replied that they would believe him because he was SAADIQ and AMEEN. So he said "What if I tell you that I have been chosen as a messenger of God?". They said we don’t believe you. And they rejected him, and they started opposing him.
 
And when he started preaching his message, one of the complaints the Quraish had is that….. یُسَفِّہُ اَحلامَنا….. He makes us look stupid. Because he would say "You worship these pieces of wood and stone. How can they benefit you?". And this was remarkable similar to the way Ibraheem AS deconstructed the idea of idolism. When he was 13 years old, there was a wedding in the neighbouring village. The entire village left to attend that wedding. He went into the main temple there and smashed down all the idols with his axe. And then hung the axe on the shoulder of the biggest idol. When they returned and saw the state of their idols there were very upset, and started investigating who could have done such a thing. Someone said, we heard Ibraheem speak of such things. So they called him and he said why don't you ask this big idol, because he's the one holding the axe. They said "You think we're stupid? How can this idol speak?", so he replied" Then why do you worship him?
 
The purpose of the Quran is to reflect on the throne-ness. To question why are we doing certain thing? We do not reflect on the actions we do. We do not question them. We simply do them because of culture, which is super imposed upon us.
Why are we burying our daughters? Quran came to question things. And this is why Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) faced so much opposition because he started saying "This is wrong".
Quran is categorized as having two major parts
Meccan Quran
Madani Quran
85 Surahs
39 Surahs
11 Para
19 para
Smaller
Larger
 
 
Main topic of Meccan Quran was TAWHEED, which linguistically means "To Make One" or "Making One". It means to worship Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) without associations. There is no Anthropomorphism in Islam. There is no association of God creations….. لیس کمثلہ شئی….. There is no THING (creation) like him. Therefore whatever occurs to the intellect, Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) is not like that. Maulana Ali said….. العجز عن درک ادراکہ اداک . The inabilityt to comprehend God is the comprehension of God.
 
The Christian solution to the unknowability of God is Christ, in which god becomes man, so that God can understand man. Incarnation means "becoming of flesh". But for Muslims, the know ability of God is ONLY through creation. We explore creation to understand God. Because creation is the theater of divine manifestation. But Muslims do not believe that god can become a thing. There is no embodiment.
 
Quran is not a linear book. The book asks its reader to surrender the desire for linearity. And if someone cannot do that they will have a huge problem following the Quran. Because it does not begin at the beginning and it does not end at the end.
 
Quran talks about the concept of AAKHIRA, which means the end or the hereafter. The Arabs did not believe in the concept of a hereafter. Their world comprised of manhood, chivalry, poetry, wine and women. Most of their poems was about wine and women. They loved those two things. Quran RADICALLY challenged that world view.
They asked the messenger, "How can decayed bones be brought back to life after they have become dust?". Quran responds that the one who brought you the first time is capable of doing it again. Because anything done the second time is easier than the first.
 
Quran talks about a ساعۃ….. Which mean the end of time. Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) was the first sign of the beginning of the end. On a individual level, it is the last moment of our lives which is imminent death. All previous traditions (Jewdism, Christianty, Hinduism, Budhism) indicate that at some point the human experience will come to an end. They all have a concept of an apocalyptic vision.
 
The descriptions of the after life are very descriptive in the Quran on either side. Heaven as well as hell. And these descriptions are very sensual in nature. In paradise it talks about gardens with flowing rivers, fruits, beautiful maidens, youths. But according to a hadees, when Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) was asked about heaven, he said….. ما لا عین رأت ولا اذن سمعت ولا قطر علی قلب بشر. It is something that no eye has seen, its sounds no ear has heard  and it has not occurred on any heart. So the concept of heaven and hell are descriptive so that we can gain some imagination of what it is. Because after death there is no body. All that exists is the soul, and so it’s a sensual experience rather than a physcial experience.
For example Aristotle asked his student who was thirsty and was reaching out for a drink. He asked, is it he drink that you seek or the quench of your thirst that you find pleasurable. And the student replied the quench of the thirst. So it is that human experience which has been described for jannah, and the physicality is only a symbol to indicate to the underlying experience.
 
The Meccan surahs with verses of "Oh humanity or mankind", whereas Medeni surahs begin with "Oh you have believed". Where Meccan Quran was geared more towards the individual, Madani Quran was about social interactions and behavior. So if you believe in Allah, how does that change your behavior towards his other creations?. Meccan Quran was aimed a building individual personalities, whereas Madani Quran was aimed at building a society. It was creating an environment in which the Quranic teachings will manifest at a societal level, and not simply at an individual level.
 
How the Quran was compiled
 
Arab culture necessitated these people to have a phenomenal memory. If they could not memorize poems or stories perfectly, they could not be a storyteller. And this is why Quran is primarily an oral tradition and not a written tradition. As the chapters and verses were being revealed on Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam), those blocks of revelation were being written down in his presence on gazelle and camel leather. But the are not being compiled in any order.
 
In 632 Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) died. At that point many of the companions had memorized the Quran perfectly. ابی ابن کعب  was considered the best Haafiz among the companions. In 633 the battle of Yamama was fought against people in Najd. One of them had declared himself to be a prophet. In that battle many of the Huffaz were killed.
 
At that time the need of compiling the Quran in a book form was realized. But since the messenger himself never put revelation in the form of a book, there was a great amount of concern among the companions. They didn’t want to introduce something that the messenger himself did not do.
Once this need is realized, Zain Ibn Saabit (زید ابن ثابت) was given the resonsibility to compile the Quran. The criteria for this compilation was that every piece of Quran that was written in the life of the prophet in front of him, be brought forward along with 2 witnesses. This is how the entire Quran was put together.
They found 2 witnesses for every single piece of revelation except the last 2 verses of Surah Tawbah. At that time Khuzaima was used as a witness because Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) had said during his life, that the witness of khuzaima was equivalent to two witnesses. They compiled all these blocks of revelation into a MUSHAF or a SAHEEFA.
In this version of Quran, there were no dots or اعراب  on the letters. Over time this resulted in people reciting the Quran in different ways, because there was no standardized writing. So for example کتب  could be pronounced کَتَبَ  or کِتَبٌ. There were no diacritical marks on the letters. Quraish would pronounce مومن, where as Bani Tameem would pronounce مؤمن .
 
Usman ordered a standardization of the MASHAF. Zain and 3 other companions, including Maulana Ali took on the responsibility of adding the dots and Eraab on the entire Quran. This standardized version of the Quran was then sent over to all different cities and Usman commanded all other copies to be burnt.
 
But even then, much like today, written Quran is NOT the primary source in which Quran is protected. It is protected through oral transmissions. Written Quran is only used as a crutch in the absence of oral transmission. Even today when a new copy of Quran is printed, it is sent to the HUFFAZ for verification. It is not checked against other existing copies of the Quran.
 
For all previous books THEY themselves took on the responsibility of protecting the book. But because Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) was the last prophet, and there was no one coming after him who could point out the mistakes in the book (similar to previous nations), Allah took it upon himself to protect this book.
نحن نزلنا الذکر و نحن لہ لحفظون
 
That original copy of the Quran is in Tashkant. One copy was sent to Egypt. The one that Maulana Ali put together in the Kufi script is in Kufa
 
 



And this is why before we begin I want us to understand the context for revelation. Because content without context is a disaster! If you want to clearly understand any content, you have to study it within its proper context! There are countless examples to illustrate this, but I'll give you one simple example.
First, let's count to 10: one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine and 10.
Now I'm going to count to 10 again, but this time I will place one of the numbers out of context, and then you tell me which number is outer of context: one, two, three, five, six, four, seven, eight, nine and 10.
It is easy to see that the number four is out of context. What told you that the number is out of context? The numbers around it, right? The sequence or the environment surrounding it revealed to you that the number 4 is out of context. Because if I counted: one, three, five seven, nine, two, four, six, eight, 10, then the number 4 is not out of context. Because it falls in place with the surrounding numbers!

So in order to understand the miracle of Quran, we *must* understand it within the context of Arabia, where it was being revealed. One of the greatest problems of us not being able to experience the miracle of Quran, is that we try to experience it in the context of 2016. The Quran is a timeless miracle; but what we need to understand is that it was a different kind of miracle for the people at the time of the prophet, and it is a different kind of miracle for us. There are amazing things about the Quran that the ancient arabs were able to see and experience which are completely lost on us. And there are amazing things about the Quran that you and I would find absolutely miraculous, which the ancient arabs would not be able to experience at all. This is what makes the Quran a timeless miracle. It continuously adapts to the changing times.



As I said in our last session... the best introduction is when the person gives it himself. In order for us to know what Allah wants, the best source for that information would be from Allah himself. He gives that information in his book, the Holy Quran. The ultimate miracle that he bestowed upon his beloved prophet. When you favor someone, you give them more than you give the rest of the world. So by that logic, the Quran must be the BEST of all miracles, the most powerful of all miracles, the most amazing of all miracles.

Yet we as muslims, completely fail to see that. So that's why I wanted to start with, why this book is a miracle, because this revelation is what Rasool Allah used as his proof of prophethood. Now we already believe this book to be a miracle by faith, which is a thing of the heart. But let's see if we can find a way to believe this intellectually as well.

And as I said previously, there are only two things that matter. The heart, which is the source of all emotions, and the intellect, which is the source of all reasoning. If you just have information in the brain, it does not naturally translate into action. You have to use integrity to force the action. And if you only have faith, then even though it will result in action, the action will be weak, and you will not be able to sustain it for long. Faith, that stands on the ground of doubt, cannot stand for long. Therefore, ONLY when we establish a balance between the heart and the intellect, actions can become perfect! They becomes ever lasting, and they becomes exteremely powerful.

The miracle of Quran is a vast topic. InshaAllah, I'm going to attempt to cover it in 3 sessions. I'll show you the actual miracle in the 3rd session, but I need to lay the ground work in these 2 sessions, so that we are actually able to see the miracle in the third. Because what I want to show you requires a complete paradigm shift. So please do not miss the next 2 sessions. I promise you it will be truly amazing InshaAllah! I especially want you kids to see this, and that's why I'm going to attempt to do these 3 sessions in English. Because I don't want you guys to miss a single thing. And this is why, I asked you to take notes if you can. Unfortunately we have a 7 day gap in between two sessions, that's why it is really important, that when you come to the next session, you should remember everything I say today, like you just heard it from me. Because I will pickup where I left off in the previous session.

So let's start by asking ourselves a couple of  basic questions:
My first question is, Is the Quran, the word of Allah or is it the words of the prophet (PBUH)? Did Allah give his messenger the actual Arabic words, or was it his own creation?
My second Question: Is Quran for the heart or the intellect?
What do you think? Just call it out! Anybody!

As I said in my previous session, the way I want to do this is pretend like I'm talking to just a couple of people so we can make this interactive.
Choose Yasmeen Hussain

Well let's look at what the Quran says:

وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَنزِيلُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ۔ نَزَلَ بِهِ ٱلرُّوحُ ٱلْأَمِينُ۔ عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ۔ بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
This (Quran) has been revealed to you by the lord of the universes. Revealed through the trustworthy spirit (Jibraeel). On your heart so that you can warn mankind in clear Arabic language - 2X

So the Ayat says (عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ), that it was revealed on the heart... Hmmmm Interesting! Not the intellect, but the heart! Isn't that strange? Intellect is the actual tool of reasoning, but Allah is saying we revealed it on your heart, which is the source of emotions. Why is that?
Because you see... the heart is the fountainhead and the source of all emotions and feelings, and it is the intellect that converts those emotions into words. Let's say you feel thirsty and you're sitting in someone else's house. What do you do? You ask for water.... So you're converting your feeling into what? words!
That feeling, that desire to ask for water, emerges first in your heart, then the brain converts it into language, or words "Please can I have a glass of water?" You understand?
So the ayat says, we revealed this Quran on your heart. Heart deals with what, meaning or words? Meaning! But then it says in the next ayat (بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّۢ مُّبِينٍۢ), in clear Arabic language. What converts meaning into language? The heart or the intellect? The intellect! So what this ayat is saying, is that we revealed the essence of the Quran on your heart, and now it is your job to convert it into the Arabic language in order to warn mankind!  So the words of the Quran are actually the words of Rasool Allah (PBUH) not Allah Himself. Allah revealed the meaning on his heart, Rasul Allah formulated the words. So the words of Quran are actually Rasool Allah's words, and that's the first thing we need to understand in order to begin appreciating this miracle.

And if you logically think about it, if Allah just gave the words to Rasul ALlah, and all Rasool Allah did was simply narrate it, then where would be the honor of Rasul Allah in that? In that case Allah would just be using Rasool Allah as a medium. He could have chosen anyone instead of him! No!!! Instead Allah Himself testifies...
لَوْ أَنزَلْنَا هَذَا ٱلْقُرْآنَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍۢ لَّرَأَيْتَهُۥ خَشِعًۭا مُّتَصَدِّعًۭا مِّنْ خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ
Had i revealed this Quran on a mountain, it would have exploded into dust out of fear of Allah.

Fear of what? Fear of their incapacity to convert this revelation into the perfect words! It was ONLY Rasul Allah (PBUH) during that time, whose heart had the strength to bear this revelation, and then convert it so accurately into words.

Now it is said, that Rasool Allah didn't know how to read and write... that's a separate discourse and I'm not going to get into that here right now. But our belief is that the concept is absolutely incorrect. He of course knew how to read and write, and there is a whole majlis of Hazrat Zia Ali Sahab on this

Ok, Now I want you to remember these ayats... cause we'll come back to them in a bit.
Thank you kids!

Now let's talk about Why Quran is a miracle?
What are some of the way in which we believe Quran to be a miracle?

1. Predictions: These could be miracles for us, but couldn't possibly have been a miracle to the Arabs?
2. It has been untampered for over 1400 years
3. Linguistic excellence:

This is all great and wonderful however, if the power of quran is:
Predictions!, then these could be miracles for us, but couldn't possibly have been a miracle to the Arabs? Cause we can appreciate these predictions, but there is no way they could have appreciated all this in those times. But Quran was not revealed to us! It was revealed to the Arabs of that time. So what was the miracle in it for them?
If it is a miracle because the content has been untampered, then in my opinion that's pretty cool! I'd put it more in the pretty cool category, rather than the miracle category. It's certainly not as amazing as seeing a dead man come to life, or a staff turning into a serpent.
Now if this is a linguistic miracle, It could certainly be a miracle to the Arabs, but then it's not a miracle for us... at least not until we learn Arabic.

I would hear people tell me... OMG Quran is so awesome! Its arabic is amazing, No poets of that time could come up with anything in comparison to it. And I would get all excited and say "WOW! Quran is so awesome!!!! Wait.... Why is it awesome?" Oh because it's Arabic is awesome! Oh WOW! But wait... I don't know Arabic :( Well too bad for you!!  It's still awesome!! And you'll never know how awesome because you don't know Arabic!
It's like I show you an opaque glass door and I say, you know behind this door is something REALLY amazing! It's SOOO incredible! OMG, I can't even begin to tell you how amazing it is!! And then you ask me, can I see it? And I say NOOOO!!! You can't! Go learn Arabic first!

But unfortunately that's the attitude today. But the problem with that approach is, that who's going to learn arabic for 5 years just so that they can see the miracle of Quran? Most people will not do that. Certainly not without enough motivation that drives them in that direction. I mean, all adults here are professionals. Everyone of us chose a certain career path, and we struggled for it, and worked hard for it. But we didn't just pick our careers out of a hat. There was something that inspired us to choose that path. right? So either give me the inspiration to want to learn Arabic, or at least show me something! Don't just throw your hands up in the air and say ... you need to know Arabic in order to apprecitate the Quran!

Now look! of course you need to understand Arabic in order to understand the Quran. That's a given. Because that's the language of the Quran. We can't deny that. And the more I learn Quranic Arabic the more I understand why that is so important. And InshaAllah, in a few sessions, you will too. But that doesn't mean that if we don't know Arabic we cannot atleast get a small taste of this miracle!! We may not get the entire 100% of it. But we could at least enjoy 2% of it. It's better than nothing. And then who knows, maybe we may get so inspired by this 2% that we might decide to strive towards understanding the remaining 98%.

Every book in the world has an audience, and a subject the book addresses. The audience of the Quran, the one it speaks to is man! (الانسان). And the subject of Quran, the topic it addresses for man is piety (التقوی). So this is the first thing to remember. It talks about how a man can acquire what? piety

In Quran, there are only 2 types of conversations. It is either giving information, or it is expressing emotion! That's it! There isn't a third kind. When it talks about shareeat, rules of halaal haram, jannat, jahannum, akhirat, angels what is that information or emotion? Information!
When it talks about prayers, supplications, love, desires, prophetic stories and events, what is that? information or emotion? Emotion!

Do you know why there are only 2 types of conversations in the Quran? Because there are the only two kinds of conversations we humans can have .... Think about it... Think about any conversation you've had in the past, or any conversation you will have in the future, isn't it either giving information, or expressing emotion?

Let's now return to the ayah

وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَنزِيلُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ۔ نَزَلَ بِهِ ٱلرُّوحُ ٱلْأَمِينُ۔ عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ۔ بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
This (Quran) has been revealed to you by the lord of the universes. Revealed through the trustworthy spirit (Jibraeel). On your heart so that you can warn mankind in clear Arabic language -

Where did Allah reveal this miracle, the heart or the intellect? the heart! And immediately after that he says, so that you can warn mankind in clear Arabic language. What converts meaning into language or words? the heart or the intellect? the intellect! So you see... Quran is a miracle for the heart as well as a miracle of the intellect, because those are the only 2 kinds of conversations we humans can have!!! SubhanAllah!

Now here's the most important part...
Ask Audience: What does the word miracle mean? In common language, not in the context of religion, but in our day to day life, when do we use or hear the word miracle?
Call it out, anybody
Doctors use the word miracle when patient unexpectedly recovers
It's used in sports, in cricket for example, when they hit a six on the last ball
You tank an exam, and then find out you got a 97!
You see an amazing dream;
So the word miracle is thrown around all the time in common speech.


Now let's take a deeper look at the word miracle. What meaning does that word hold for us. When I tell you that I'm going to show you a miracle...  in your mind.... how would you separate that from a magic trick or an illusion? So for example Moosa (AS) turns his staff into a serpent, is that a miracle or a magic trick? And how would you differentiate it? Eisa (AS), brings a dead man back to life! Miracle or magic?
Ask Audience: So what is the difference between magic and a miracle?


In general speech, miracle is used for something that is highly unlikely, but not impossible. Something that is what? Highly unlikely, but not impossible! But it also has a spiritual sense, pretty much among all religions in general. In this spiritual sense, a miracle is something that you experience. It is something that you feel in your heart. It is not something I can give to you, becuase it is a very personal experience.

In other words, it is not something that can be proven by scientific facts, or guaged in scientific terms. In the scientific context, a miracle is irrelevant. Why? Because the scientific community in general, when they study a phenomenon... what is their attempt? Are they trying to figure out if this is a miracle, or are they trying to figure out if there is a scientific explanation behind it? What is their attempt always?  To find a scientific explanation! So scientifically speaking there is no such thing as a miracle. It has to have some scientific explanation. That's the post modern thought.

Therefore, miracle could be a common language thing, or it could be a religious thing. But what it certainly cannot be, is a scientific thing. You understand? Why? Because science is something you can sense with the 5 senses! And when you're talking about a miracle, you're definitely asking people to go beyond the 5 senses. right?
So whenever science doesn't have an answer, their response is that we don't know why this happend. We don't have an answer yet, but we're sure there is a scientific explanation behind it. There is no leap of faith in science. So, science can tell us how the big bang happened. But what science cannot tell is why it happened! And if you ask them why? They'll say that is not a scientific quesiton, that is a philosophical question or religious question.

But If you and I, today, in this day and age, were to believe in a miracle, then we have to see something beyond science. Something that just cannot be explained otherwise. Only something like that will make a believer out of me and you. Because we're living in a highly intellectual, very scientific age. In reality we are much bigger believers in science, than religion, even if we are religious people. Because we live in an age of questioning, and challenging, and asking questions and seeking intellectual answers.

With me so far? Any questions?

OK, now let's agree on a common definition of what a miracle is supposed to be. Religiously, for something to be a miracle, it needs to meet certain criteria:

1. A miracle should be impossible for human beings to perform. It is not something that can be performed. It is not a skill. It is not a trick, it is not technology. It cannot originate from a human being. For example if you see a human being floating in air... that is not something a human being can do without a device. Or a new born baby speaking... and not just speaking, but speaking eloquently. That's not something human beings can do. Historically, from the beginning of time, there is no evidence of such a thing happening, except through miracle. Yet we have Eisa (AS) addressing the people in the most eloquent language, when he's one day old.
2. It breaks the laws of nature. There are certain patterns and laws of nature which we observe on a daily basis, and we take for granted. Like the fire burns for example. We know this historically. Yet here we have Ibrahim (AS) thrown in the midst of fire and it doesn't burn him. Why? Fire is supposed to burn! So that's the second one. It breaks the laws of nature.
3. It cannot be duplicated: Why is magic, or an amazing trick, the most amazing illusions done by hoodeeni or David Copperfield not be considered a miracle in the islamic sense? Because it can be duplicated. Somebody else can perform the same illusion, the same magic, the same trick. And more than one person can do it.
4. It can ONLY be done by somone claiming to be the messanger of Allah. Musailema (AlKazzab) saw that Rasul ALlah (PBUH) gained so much popularity by claiming to be a prophet, so he thought, if he did the same, he could be pretty popular as well. So he too declared that he was a messanger of Allah. Now he lived in a separate town from the prophet. One day the prophet was taken to a well that had completely dried up. He was requested to pray to Allah that the well recovers, because the town was suffering because of it. Rasul Allah (PBUH) prayed and spit into the well, and it started overflowing with water as the narration goes. In Musaliema AlKazzab's town, there too was a well that was half dry. The supply had become short. So the people of his town told him "You do the same thing. You're Allah's prophet too... don't worry you got this!". So he prayed and spit in the well, and it completely dried up. So a miracle can only be done by a true prophet. If someone else tries to do it he will fail.
5. The last criteria, and the greatest of all, is that the entire purpose of a miracle is to challenge mankind 3x. Since the beginning of time, the point of giving a miracle to any prophet has always been, to challenge the people of their time. A challenge that if you think I'm lying, if you think I'm crazy, If you think I'm insane, then you do what I'm showing you. And if you cannot do it, and no one amonst you can do it; if you as in individual cannot do it, and if you collectively as a group cannot do it, then what I have been given can only be from Allah. Therefore when I tell you I'm the prophet, I speak the truth.
Nooh (AS) challenged his ummah with the flood. Ibrahim (AS) challenged his Umma with the fire. Moosa (AS) challenged Firaon with the staff turning into a snake, by parting the water. Eisa (AS) challenged his umma by bring the dead back to life! By curing the leapor. So the purpose of a miracle is always to challenge mankind.
Suleman (AS) could speak with Jinns and animals, right? But that's not considered his miracle. Why? Because he did not challenge the poeple of his time with it! His miracle was what? the thrown of queen Sheebah (بلقیس کا تخت). He transported it from her kingdom to his. That was his miracle, because he challenged someone with that.

Now let's talk about what the word miracle means!

The word for miracle in arabic is معجزۃ۔ . It is a derivative of the word أعجاز, which in turn is from the root wordعجز. And what it means, is to bring someone to his knees, such that he cannot rise again. It means to incapacitates or overpowers the opponent, to make them incapable of any kind of response.

So imagine, consumed in my thoughts, I'm about to cross a street. There is a truck speeding towards me, and I don't look at it. As I begin crossing the street, I see the truck when it is 1 feet away from me. In that one second, that feeling of being completely incapacitated, that feeling of helpless, that feeling of being on my knees, that feeling of being overpowered, to the point that I am not even going to attempt to save myself. The feeling, where the truck driver will not even attempt to hit the brake, because it is so pointless at this time.
So in that 1 second, just before I turned into pancake... what've I've experienced is أعجاز.

In Quran, however the word معجزۃ is not used. Instead of the word معجزۃ, what's used is the word "Ayah". Ayah in arabic means two things. It means a miracle, and it also means a sign. So a good translation that would allude to both definitions, would be a miraculous sign. So what is an ayah? It is a miraculous sign.

So now let's start talking about why this book is a miracle. One of the reasons why we find it so hard to appreciate the Quran as a miracle, is because we try to do so in our present times. You know why? Because the Quran was not revealed in 2015. It's teachings may certainly apply in our the time period, but it was not revealed in our time.
Same holds true for Islam. The Islam we see around us today, is not the Islam that existed in the times of the prophet. That's why we find Islam so confusing today.
That's why we need to begin, by first understanding why the Quran was a miracle in the prophet's time. What was Islam in the prophet's time. So, what we need to do is go back in time, more than 1400 years, to this piece of land called Arabia. But before we take this journey, we need to prepare for it. Just like when we plan a vacation to a country, we first try to gain some basic knowledge about that place, right? The culture, the language, etc. So let's understand a few things about that time before we make this trip.

Get energy level check...
Volunteer
For our discussion today, the first thing you need to know, is that there are 3 types of Arabic. How many types?
And you need to remember them in this order I tell you...

Spoken Arabic: Another word for spoken Arabic is busted arabic, ugly arabic, slang, or twisted, demented, mutated Arabic. Also called عمِّیَّۃ which means dialectic arabic. Meaning Egyptians have their own dialect, Algerians have their own dialect, Kuwaits have their own dialect and Saudis have their own dialects. Arabic is similar but the dialect varies from country to country.

Proper Arabic: Any Arabic newspaper that you read, or any Arabic news program that you watch on TV is all in proper Arabic. It's also called الفصحۃ . Also, if you ever take an Arabic course in college, they call it Modern Standard Arabic. What they mean by that is فصحۃ. It is correct arabic, it is enunciated correctly. It uses the grammatical rules of Arabic correctly. So when you're learning proper Arabic, you're learning correct Arabic. There is nothing wrong with it.

But what we really need to understand for our purpose is this third type of Arabic, which is what? Ancient Arabic!

Ancient Arabic: Ancient Arabic is the original, pure arabic spoken in the land of Arabia. It was far more sophisticated, far more eloquent, far more complex, far more advanced, far more involved, far more intricate, far more powerful than proper modern standard arabic. This is something you really need to understand in order to appreciate the Quran!!
If you go to Egypt today, and you study arabic for 5 years, and you return speaking the most perfect arabic, you're still speaking which Arabic? Proper Arabic!  That still doesn't mean you have an understanding of what? Ancient arabic! They are two completely different things.
The subtelties, and the minute details that the ancient Arabs could detect in a sentence, the proper arabs cannot. That's why even when the proper Arabs today, hear the Quran, they do not hear what the ancient Arabs heard when they heard the exact same ayat. So first let's understand how come these Arabs reached such linguistic heights!!

Every country in this world today, has something called nationalism, or patriotism. You take something and say, we're the best at it. For example, one country makes the best baseball bats, or cricket bats. That's their national pride. They unite as a nation in the production of that one particular thing. Today we even have something called state pride. Every state has something they're very proud of, right? I'm of NJ, we have the highest murder rate in the country or something.

Ok Good! So let's go back in time. The year is 610 BC. Rasool Allah (PBUH) has been born and he is 40 years old. And since we're from the future, we know that any day now the Quran is going to be revealed to him. But we're not going to meet Rasool Allah (PBUH) on this trip. The people we really want to meet are these arabs of this time. So now what do we see?

We see a bunch of Arabs hanging out in the desert. There are no buildings, no agriculture, not even much of a farming community. They haven't even discovered oil yet. Their neighbour is the Persian empire, and another is the Roman empire. Now, if you know anything about empires, they are always trying to increase the size of their backyards. They always want to expand. But we see something very strange. Do we see them marching into Arabia? No! Why? because they're thinking, why should we send our soldiers to get BBQ is the heat of the deserts? What are we going to get out of them anyway? They're useless to us! They have nothing! There is nothing we can take over. They have no monuments, no resources, no buildings, they have absolutely nothing. So there's no point going after them. So they just leave them alone.

So now we see these Arabs living by themselves in isolation, and we see that they actually enjoy this isolation. And when a bunch of people live in isoltation with no external influence, what happens to their language? It gets refined! It becomes more and more pure; It becomes perfect! Because they're only talking among one another. There is no external influence on their language. Are you with me?
So what do we see these Arabs taking pride in? IS IT their beautiful architecure, the amazing structures that they built? ; the beautiful gardens they have? the amazing armies they command? No! So then what was the only thing they had that they could possibly take pride in? Language! That's the ONLY thing they had. So when you take pride in the ONLY thing you have, you REALLY REALLY REALLY take care of it. So Arabic was their national identiy! It became a matter of Arab identity to be focused on language! To excel in it, to drop everything else and focus on just this one thing. So much, that language for them was the greatest of all things to know!

Do you know what the opposite of Arab is? العرب وال ؟۔. Ajam! Do you know what ajam means? Dumb! Mute! One who cannot speak. So if someone could not communicate with these arabs, at the same level of sophistication that they spoke Arabic with, that's what they would call them! Mute! Meaning, Dude! you may as well have been born dumb and mute, because you're not even worthy of speaking with us. This was how crazy they were about eloquence! And what is the height of any language? Poetry! So the Arabs of this time can recite poems as naturally as you and I have a conversation.  They would converse using the most eloquent poetry.

It was so crazy that their sports were developed around poetry! They used to have this ancient form of dissing competitions. You know how there is freestyle rapping today? They had their own version of it, except it wasn't slang. It was poetry in it's highest and purest form. Two tribes would select one man, the BEST man from their tribe, and they would then battle each other in poetry before a huge crowd. And the one who wins, has successfully disgraced the other tribe for generations to come. And the one who lost, in all likelihood, lose his head. Because he did not just lose the competition, he lost the dignity of his tribe, for how long? a few days? a month? a year? No, for generations to come!!!!! So these guys took their poetry and their literature, VERY VERY VERY seriously. This was an extremely serious thing to them.

Why am I telling you all this? How is this relevant? I'm telling you all this because you really need to understand, how important language was to these Arabs, and to what linguistic heights they had perfected their language. Because realize, that these are the people on which Quran is about to be revealed.

Let me try to explain what was so special about their poetry... They spoke in riddles. They spoke in images.

When an Arab looked outside his window, what did he see? Nothing .. He just saw the empty dry desert. When you see nothing, you develop a very vivid imagination. Today we don't have much of an imagination. You know why? Because everything that we can think of has been visualized in sci fi movies. So if we are asked to image, we'll just relate a different version of what we've seen before. But a good imagination is when one can imagine things they haven't seen before. And this was the arab imagination. They would draw pictures with their words. They used words that were very picturesqe. Their words were full of imagery.

So there is this poet. He's making poetry about how generous he is and how much he gives in charity. So his wife comes to him and says.... Are you crazy? We don't have even have food to eat. What are you talking about? We're bankrupt and you're making poetry about how generous you are? He turns to her and responds to her in poetry. He says:
السَّیلُ حَربٌ عَلی مَکانِ العَالِی
Heavy rain does not get along with a house on top of a hill! 2X
What does that have to do with being charitable, or being generous? But do you know what he was talking about?
In your head imagine a house on top of a hill. And what's going on? Heavy rain! When it rains, does the water stay on top of the hill, or does it come to the bottom of the hill? So he says, RIZQ, or provision comes from the sky. And there are two kinds of people in the world. The lofty ones and the lowly ones. People that are closer to God and those that are worldly. If your house is on top of the hill, aren't you close to the sky? Close to God? So the water doesn't stay with me, meaning the provision doesn't stay with me, I keep giving it to the lowly people. And where does the water accumulate? Where does it create a puddle? So the people that are rich are actually lowly, and I'm the lofty one.
And he says all this by saying what?
السَّیلُ حَربٌ عَلی مَکانِ العَالِی
5 words. Heavy rain does not get along with a house on top of a hill!
So they spoke in these riddles. They wanted you to imagine what is being said. They wouldn't just say it. And if you didn't get it, they would say... hah! Ajam (mute)!!! So this was the kind of sensitivity to language that they enjoyed.
 

Now think about this. Imagine you are about to fight an opponent with naked swords. You are facing death right now. Can you imagine what you mental state would be? I know I would not even remember my name if you asked me! But these Arabs, they would recite poetry! And their poetry was so powerful, their words would shake their opponents to the core!

المَوتُ أَھْلاَ عِندَناَ مِنَ العَصَلِ
نَحنُ بَنُو ضَبَّۃَ أَصحَابُ الجَمَلِ
نحن بَنُو المَوتِ اِذَا المَوتُ نَزَلِ 
This is the poem that people of this tribe Banu Dabba would recite when facing their opponent. Death to us is sweeter than the taste of honey. We are the people of Banu Dabbah. We are the sons of death, and where we arrive, assume your death has arrived!

In the battle of Khyber, Maulana Ali is facing Amr Ibn Abd Wud. This man was a monster! It is said that he alone was equivalent to 1000 soldiers. He challenged the prophet saying "Don't you have any men left amonst you to face me? Arent' you the one's exited to go to heaven? Face me in battle and I'll send you to the destination you desire!". Nobody answers his call. Maulana Ali with Rasul Allah (PBUH) permission, goes against him, reciting....


أنا الَّذِی سَمَّتْنِی أُمِّی حَیدَرَا
کَلَی صِغَابَاطِلْ کَرِیہِ المَنظَرَا
وَفِیھِمِی بِصَّاعِ کَیلِ سَّندَرَا
I am the one whose mother named him Hyder! Evil trembles when it see me standing before it. My sword breaks open skulls with one strike!

یاَ حَبَّ ذَالَجنَّۃُ وَقْتِرَابُھَا ،  طَیِّبَۃٌ وَ بَارِدٌ شَرَابُھَا
وَ الرُّومُ رَومُ قَدَّنیٰ عَذَابُھَا ، عَلَیَّ اِلاَّ قَیطُھَا ضَرَابُھا
These are the verses of Hazrat Jaffar Al-Tayyar when he was fighting in the battle of Moota

أقْسَمتُ یَا نَفسُ لَتَنْزِلِنَّا  ،  کارِھَۃً أَولیٰ تُطَابِعِنَّا 
These are the verses of Hazrat Abdullah iBn Rawaha when he was fighting in the battle of Moota


طلع البد علینا

A man comes to Naseeruddin Tusi... who was an scholar, and asks him Will the love of Ali matter on the day of judgment? Now If someone asks you or me this question. What would we say? Sure! Absolutely! Of course! Right? That's the best we would come up with.... This is what he replies.. I'm going to recite the poem first, and then I'll explain what it says, OK?

لو أن عـبداً أتى بالصـالحـات غداً
وود كـــلّ نـّــبي مـرســـــل وولي
وصام مـا صام صـوّاماً بلا ضجرِ
وقام ما قـام قـــوّامـاً بـــــلا مـــلل
وحجَ ما حجَ من فـرضٍ ومن سننِ
وطاف ما طاف حـافٍ غير منتعلِ
یلبسو اليتــامى من الديباج كلهّمُ
ويُطعم الجائعين البـّــر بالعســـــلِ
وعاش في النــاس آلافــاً مـــؤلفـةً
عارٍ من الذنب معصوماً من الزللِ
ما كان يوم الحشـر عند الله منتفعاً
إلَّا بحـــب أميــر المـــؤمنين عليّ

 


 

The Quran comes to these people through the messenger of Allah (PBUH) in this environment. Where they take a word and they rip it apart! When you say a sentence, they will rip it apart and criticize each word to the nth degree. They will shred it to pieces. They were EXTREMELY critical. They would try to out do each other. Of course the best way to beat a poet is to attack his what? Not to attack his personality, but to attack what? His poetry!. You attack his poetry and now you've actually got something. The Quran is revealed and what are some of the things that the disbelievers say about the Quran? It is the word of a poet! Word of magic! Word of fortune teller!


So they said he was a شاعر. But when their best poets went to challenge the messenger of Allah (PBUH), they came back swearing, Wallahi! This is not what? It's not poetry!
Then they said he was a mind reader (کاہن). In those days when fortune tellers spoke they talked in mumbo jumbo. They spoke this gibberish and it was supposed to have some sort of a magical effects. Words nobody could understand. And they would say this guy is casting a spell. But did the words of Quran make sense? They did! So it was clearly not the words of a fortune teller.
Then they called him insane. They said these were the words of insanity. That he is posessed! And that these are magical words. First of all as soon as you say this is magic, you are acknowledging that this is not normal, it is para-normal. Even by alleging that the Quran is magic, they accepted its power.
And to say that he is insane, it was basically that they couldnt' come with any allegation against the Quran. If you can't defeat the Quran, might as well accuse who? The messenger! You know when you lose a debate of ideas, you then start attacking personality! Right? It's very common. If there is a debate on an issue, and your opponent cannot beat you on the issue, what does he do? He starts attacking your personality.... Your mama! It's called character assasination. This is what they tried!
But in all their criticisms, they acknowledged its victory! SubhanAllah!




 


AlHamdulillah! We are studying the miracle of Quran. In the previous session we talked about some of the ways in which we normally consider the Quran to be a miracle. How we've extremely underrated the majesty of this revelation. At best we base its miracle on the various predictions it makes, or it's being untampered over centuries etc. All of which is true, but very single dimensional.
We talked about how the Quran is a miracle of the heart as well as of the intellect
I showed you from the ayats of the Quran, that the language of the Quran is actually Raool Allah's words. Allah did not give Rasool Allah the words. Words are His own creation and that's why this Arabic of Quran is the miracle of Rasool Allah (PBUH)
Then we discussed in detail the difference between miracle and magic, and how we completely miss the miracle of Quran, because we're searching for the wrong thing. If we see a miracle, as pulling a rabbit out of a hat, then we are definitely never going be able to experience the miracle of Quran.
Then I defined the word miracle for you. The word in Arabic is mojiza, that comes from the word Ejaaz, which means to incapacitate, or to make someone incapable of response, or to bring someone down on his knees so that he cannot rise again.
Then I explained 5 primary characteristics of a miracle:
1. A miracle should be impossible for human beings to perform.
2. It breaks the laws of nature.
3. It cannot be duplicated:
4. It can ONLY be done by somone claiming to be the messanger of Allah.
5. And the most important of all... that the entire purpose of a miracle is to challenge mankind. If the prophet does not use it to challenge mankind, then it's not really a miracle. It then falls into a different category of Khurafaat, Tasarrufaat, Karamaat, but not of Ejaaz
In Quran, the word معجزۃ is not used. Instead the word "Ayah". Ayah in arabic means two things. It means a miracle, and it also means a sign. So an Ayah means a miraculous sign.
Then we looked at 3 different types of Arabics and their differences: 1. Spoken Arabic, 2. Proper Arabic, 3. Ancient Arabic


And then finally we went back in time to Ancient Arabia to understand what level of sophistication the Arabs enjoyed when it came to language. Why Arabic had evolved to such heights, and how it meant EVERYTHING to them. I gave you a couple of examples of how their language was full for imagery, how powerful their imagination was, and how they used very picturesque words and how they painted their imagination with words. And poetry came as naturally to them, as conversation comes to us...
In Pakistan... kids prepare
After the people of mecca rejected Rasool Allah, the news of his arrival reached medina. Everyone arrived on the outskirts of medina to welcome him. All the little girls that were 10/11 years old they gathered right in front. I swear to you nobody prepared these little girls in advance. As soon as they saw Rasool Allah's face rising in the distant horizon, they all started singing together...
طلع البدر علینا من ثنیات الوداع
Here rises the moon from the valley of Wada

They did not rehearse this poem before. They made this up as they went along. Have you ever been so in sync with a friend, that you can complete each other's thoughts, finish each others sentences, say the same things at the same time? This is how in sync they were with Arabic. They were creating and singing this song all together, completing each others verses... and these are 10/11 year old little girls. SubhanAllah!
وجب الشکر علینا ما دعا لی اللہ دعا
Gratitude has become incumbent on us, for the beautiful call by which you've called us to Allah
أیھا المبعوث فینا جئتا بی الامر المطاعۃ
Ya Rasool Allah this is not mecca where the people rejected you! This is medina, every child, every soul here will be obedient to you
جئتا شرفتا المدینۃ مرحباً بک یا خیر داع
You have honored Medina with your presence! We welcome you with open arms ya Rasool Allah
 

So, with all these examples, if I was able to give you, even a small idea, of their eloquence, their sophistication, the precision of the words they used, then today I'd like to move forward and talk about the frame of mind the Arabs were in when the message of Allah was revealed to them by Rasool Allah (PBUH)

Volunteer
I want you to imagine something with me. Imagine you're sitting in your living room doing your thing, and there is a knock on the door. You open the door and you see your neighbour standing outside. After you exchange greetings, he says to you... Last night an angel came to me and told me that Allah has chosen me as his messenger. And Allah has commanded me to deliver His message to mankind. I have already invited my family but they have all rejected me. Since you're my neighbour, and you have a right on me as your neighbour, I'm now delivering the message to you. I am Allah's messenger, and everything that I say is not from me, but from Allah himself. So obeying what i say is obeying Allah, and disobeying me is actually disobeying Allah.
So this is what your neighbour just hit you with. What's going through your mind right now? What are you going to say to him? Are you OK? What did you have for dinner last night? right? You're my neighbour for so many years, I've seen you all my life, and now all of a sudden you want me to believe that an angel comes to you and Allah speaks to you through the angel? And then on top of that you're telling me that your own family has rejected you, they're calling you insane, but you want me to listen to you? follow you? obey you? Change my life based on what you're saying? Everybody already thinks you're insane! What's going to happen if I follow you? What will they call me?
Aaah... I'll think about it! Thanks! And you shut the door.

Think about this for a minute... we so easily condemn the disbelievers, oh these kuffar! They rejected the prophet, they called him insane! They are going to hell! right? But put yourself in their shoes for a moment. What Rasul Allah was asking them, was not an easy thing to believe in. This bridge from disbelief to faith, was not an easy bridge to cross. You see... today it's very easy for us to talk about Islam, and preach Islam, because it's been going on for thousands of years.
I said I'm going to start these sessions on Islam, and you all just came. I didn't have to make any effort to invite you to these sessions. Why? Because you've already bought into the concepts of Islam. But not for the Arabs of that time! This was the first time it was happening. It was a very difficult task that was being asked of them. Do you understand?

If your neighbour came to your doorstep and claimed to be a prophet, of course you will think he is insane!!! Why wouldn't you believe him? Do you know why? Statistics! Because you have never seen that happen before in your life. Believing in one God is not that far fetched. Believing in a hereafter - possible. But what is absolutely impossible to believe? What you just cannot see happening is what? You just cannot see a man, who you've known all your life, suddenly being spoken to by an angel. that just goes beyond your wildest imagination. Right? Why you! Why not me? What's so special about you?

So I want you to appreciate the state of mind, the Arabs at that time were in, when Rasul Allah approached them with the message? They were not bad people, they were not evil people. What was being asked of them was EXTREMELY difficult. When in the entire city, you are the only one holding a particular idea, a particular faith, that is different from everyone else's faith, what does that makes you? Crazy! It makes you insane! And what does it make the next person in line believing you? Just as insane! So before we write these people off as evil, and kafir, put yourself in their shoes for a second.  How easy is it to accept when no one around you believes? The message of Rasul Allah (PBUH) was a VERY VERY VERY difficult thing to believe for the people in the city. It was not easy. Faith was an EXTREMELY expensive, extremely dangeous commodity at that time. Do you understand?

OK, if you can appreciate that, I now want you to imagine, that the person that the angel brought the message of prophethood to, is no one else, but YOU! Now it is YOU who has to go and knock on your neighbours door, and deliver the message to him. What is the state of your heart now? If you tell this to your own parents, they will drag you to a psychiatrist. Imagine having to stand before a group of strangers, and promoting this novel idea, that goes, not only against all their existing beliefs, but also the beliefs of their forefathers before them. How would you feel about delivering this message? What would be some of the things you'd be afraid of?
Would you be afraid of ridicule? Of being called insane? Would you accept this task? Of course not! It's just too difficult. But Rasool Allah did it. He accepted the responsibility! Why? Was this his ticket to Jannah? No! He was already from the Jannah! Jannah was created for him! He already owned it! Then why would he accept such a difficult and painful responsibility?? You know why? He did it for me! He did it for you! So that you and I could be rescued from the slavery of our souls. So that you and I could have an actual shot at entering jannah. He said, if I don't take this step, how will the people of my ummah be free? How will they be guided? He did this for me! He did you this for you! He did this for all of us. Now that is love! That is mercy! That is a true blessing!

So hopefully having appreciated, how difficult this task was for the prophets, Allah gave his prophets certain miracles. This was in order to facilitate the decision of the people they were calling. The purpose was, that if you think I'm crazy! then let me show you, let me challenge you with what Allah gave me, and then you tell me if anyone besides Allah could have given this to me. If not, then this miracle is my proof that I am speaking the truth. And now if you're still not going to believe me, it's only because you're arrogant and obstinate; but you can no longer deny that I am Allah's messenger. When they saw the water parting, the staff turning into a serpent, the dead man coming to life, birds of clay coming to life and flying, they were incapacitated. All their arguments were defeated. It brought them to their knees. Once you're on your knees, you have two choices. You either surrender and fall into sajda, or you become arrogant and stand back up and disbelief. At that point it becomes a personal choice.

Any quesitons so far?

Now having understood all this, let's start talking about the Quran itself as a miracle. First of all let's understand what does it mean to feel a miracle. Because remember! miracle is something you experience. It's not just something you see. You see a magic trick, but never experience a magic trick. You can only experience a miracle!

We have really under estimated this word miracle. We have no clue what a mircale feels like. The best we know of it is, as I said last time, is when someone recovers from an unexpected disease, or hits an impossible home run, or passes an exam, or survives a very serious accident, we consider these things to be miracles. So we don't really have a true point of reference to compare it to when we hear the Quran. Because in order to experience a miracle, we first need to have some idea of what it feels like. Right?

So let's once again take a journey back in time, 2000 years before the time of the prophet this time.
Imagine we are among a group of people, that are all running for their lives. We learn that the army of Firaon is chasing them. And being from the future, we know that if he catches us, that means certain death for us. So we start running with the group until we reach the bank of this huge river, and we find ourselves completely trapped. We see Moosa (AS) standing at the bank of the river holding his staff. We see some people blaming him for the situation they're in. They're saying Moosa! we are now trapped because of you! You led us to our doom!
But then! we see Musa (AS) lift his staff and strike it on the water, and before our very eyes, we see the water splitting. We see the water rising on both sides, standing like huge walls, creating a pathway in between for everyone to walk through. As the Quran states:

فَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْبَحْرَ ۖ فَٱنفَلَقَ فَكَانَ كُلُّ فِرْقٍۢ كَٱلطَّوْدِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ

Imagine walking through this river watching the walls made of water on both sides. You can touch it, you can feel it, you can hear it. It's still water, and you're experiencing this miracle first hand. What is the state of your heart right now? Would there be the slightest doubt in your heart that Musa (AS) is in fact Allah's messenger? You see, what you're feeling right now, this is the feeling of experiencing a miracle. Would you ever be able to describe it in words even if you tried?

Now think about this... Is this the state of your heart when you recite the Quran? or hear it being recited to you? It's not! Because we see the Quran at best as a religious text, but not as a miracle. But you know what the ancient Arabs felt when they heard these verses recited to them? They felt what you just experienced standing with Musa (AS). Quran was no doubt, a religious text to them as well, but in addition to that they would experience the miracle everytime they heard the verses recited to them. That's why it had such a strong impact on their hearts.

Another thing I want you to appreciate about the Quran. Are all the previous miracles alive today? Is the water still separated or has it joined back? Are dead men still coming to life? Does fire still turn into a bed of flowers? no! They occurred in a particular instance, and then it was all over. But is Quran still alive? If we were able to experience the miracle of Quran, then could that experience be repeated as many times as would like. Could we experience its miracle over and over and over again?


When the people standing on the bank of the river, saw it split in two, they experienced a miracle. Then they narrated the incident to their children. Look this is what happened! I was standing right there. I saw it with my own eyes. Then the child narrates it to his child, that my dad was there when it happened. Then that child narrates it to his children, that my grandfather was right there. And this goes on until the story reaches us. When we narrate the story of Musa (AS) to our kids, it's more like (yawn) yeah there was something about moosa and the river parting or something. Why? Because to us it is just a story! A story that we have accepted on faith. But for that man who narrated this story the first time, was it just a story? No, it was a miracle to him! You understand?
What created this difference? Why is it a miracle to that man and a story to us? Because the miracle is over! It happened once in the history of time, and never again. The river never split again. The bird made of clay did not come to life ever again and fly off. Anything that is seen with the eye, has a short life span. You see it and then it's over and it becomes a story. So all these miracles that took place in the times of the previous prophets, could have been miracles to them. But for us, they're just stories that we accept on faith.

And this is why, when an athiest comes to Jews or Christians, and ask... Hey! Is it true that Jesus, brough the dead back to life. They say of course! He asks, how do you know that? The Christian says, because it's in the bible. He asks, did you actually see that miracle with your own eyes? Were you there? No! So then how do you know it happened? Did anybody record it? Is it on youtube? The Christian says because I believe in it! So the athiest says, then don't tell me that story, because that's just your belief! And anybody can believe anything.

Now when the same athiest comes to us Muslims and asks... Is Quran the message of Allah? We say of course! He asks... did you see Jibraeel (AS) descend fromt he heavens with the message? We say No! So then how do you know this is the message of Allah. We say... let me show you! Let's study this book, and let me show you why this can't be from a human. You see?

What we just said to the athiest, that "Let me show you", this is the part we all need to really understand and take seriously. Because if we don't see the miracle ourselves, how are we ever gong to show it to someone else? This is why Quran is not changing our lives! Because it's just a book of religious text to us, we are not able to experience it as a miracle. We are not mesmerized by it. Although each an everyone of us has access to this book. If we so desired, we could experience this miracle any minute of the day. We can experience it 5 times a day in namaaz... Wouldn't that just be amazing?? Wouldn't it be an incredible experience to be able to feel this miracle every single day?

Now let's see if there is a difference between what was given to the previous prophets, and what was given to our Raool Allah (PBUH)? Their miracles were for which sense? Ibrahim (AS) being thrown in the fire and it turning into a bed of flowers. People are experiencing this with which sense? What sense is involved? Moosa (AS) throws the staff on the ground, and it turning into a serpent. What sense is involved? Eisa (AS) brings the dead back to life. What sense is involved? All these miracles are for the eyes to see! Right?

But what is Quran for? Was is revealed as something to see, or something to hear? It was something to hear, not see! It was for the ears, not the eyes.

قُلْ أُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ أَنَّهُ ٱسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا قُرْآنًا عَجَبًۭا
وَأَنَّا لَمَّا سَمِعْنَا ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ آمَنَّا بِهِ
قَالُوا۟ يَقَوْمَنَآ إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا كِتَبًا أُنزِلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مُوسَىٰ
وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَتُنَا قَالُوا۟ قَدْ سَمِعْنَا
What is Allah saying?
يَٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ضُرِبَ مَثَلٌۭ فَٱسْتَمِعُوا۟ لَهُ

Listen! Listen! Listen! Oh people! I'm giving you examples, so you can listen! Why did Allah all of a sudden change His format? Why did he switch from video to audio? I mean doesn't that sound like taking a step down?

I was reading the story of prophets to my daughter Erum when she was 6 years old. I went through all the prophets, and when I reached Rasul Allah and told her about hte miracle of Quran, she got so upset! She said that's not fair Babu! Everyone else got such a cool miracle, and all our prophet got was a book! Now that sounds blasphemous, but she has a point. How come every other prophet got such cool miracles? Do you know why she felt it was cool? Because it was for the eyes to see. But Quran was not for the eyes to see, it was for listening. And what happens when you can't see something? You imagine! Is imagination more powerful or is sight more powerful? Isn't this why movies are such a disappointment when you've read the book first? Because the movie cannot match up to your imagination.
I read this amazing book divergent, and then later saw the movie. I swore I'm never going to read a book again, cause it destroys the movies.

So the question is, why did Allah change the format?
You know why he changed the format? because when you cannot see something, what are you forced to do? You are forced to imagine! What were the Arabs so big on? What did they take so much pride in? Their ability to imagine and paint that imagination in words! Quran with its mesmerizing words, created the ultimate imagination for them. An imagination that was so superior to the Arab imagination; using words that were so precise, so perfect, so eloquent, so mesmerizing, that it brought them down on their knees. It incapacitated them. It was therefore a miracle to them!


 

This book is so powerful!


AlHamdulillah! We continue on our journey to experience the Quran as a miracle. Just a quick recap of what we've covered so far...
The Arabic word for miracle is Mojiza. What does it mean?
What is the main purpose of any Miracle for any prophet, in any given time?
Who does the words of the Quran belong to?
Who does the message of the Quran belong to?
Who does the Quran speak to? Who is the audience of Quran
What is the subject of Quran?
What two types of conversations does the Quran have? Why?
Is Quran a miracle for the intellect or for the heart?

How many types of Arabic are there? What are they?
In Arabia, during the time of the prophet, what was the most important thing for Arabs? What did they take pride in? What did they identify themselves with?

In the last session we talked about how difficult it was for the people of Arabia to accept Islam. Once we appreciated that, we talked about how difficult it was for Rasool Allah to take on this responsibility. [Example of changing dinner location - change Gods]. Why did he do it? Why did he accept this responsibility? What emotion was at play for him to accep this responsibility?
Were the past miracles for the eyes or the ears? What is Quran for? Why did Allah change the format?

So Hamdulillah, we've now understood the context, the environment, the circumstances, and the people on which the Quran was revealed. These were people with incredible imagination! people of supreme eloquence! people that painted pictures with words. Who when battled, intimidated their enemies with words. People for whom, the ultimate defeat was not death, but defeat in a poetry contest. If their opponent used one word incorrectly, or did not place each word in it's proper place, they would detect that, and use that to attack his poetry, and rip it to shreds. They would criticise it to the nth degree. And mind you, they would do all of this extemporaneously. They would not prepare for this beforehand. In short, what we would consider incredible poetry today, was simply normal conversation to them. 


Yasmeen, Hussain, you guys are very talented tennis players MashaAllah. Tell me what level of opponents would you enjoy playing against? Someone your own level or someone very junior? Why? Wouldn't it be easy to win against an weak opponent?
Now what if you stand against a player that completely destroys you in the game! I mean he beats you 15-love. I mean he makes you literally run to your mommy crying. Would you play with him again? And if you do, and he beats you the same way again, and again and again. Will you want to keep playing with him?

And since language was the ONLY thing they cared about, literally more than life itself; and language is something you hear, not see; that's why the ultimate miracle, the living miracle, the miracle of all miracles, the Quran, was revealed to them primarily as a linguistic miracle.

Ultimately the Quran, is a conversation. It is a conversation for all minkind - not just for believers, or disbelievers but all mankind in general. Allah says...

هَذَا بَلَغٌۭ لِّلنَّاسِ وَلِيُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِ وَلِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَهٌۭ وَحِدٌۭ وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَبِ
This one ayat explains everything. What is the Quran? هَذَا بَلَغٌۭ It is a message..  Who is it for? لِّلنَّاسِ  for people! Why? وَلِيُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِ so they could be warned ? Of what? وَلِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَهٌۭ وَحِدٌۭ so that they become aware that their lord is only one? And so what should they do? وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ  Constantly remember him!
But then in the last part, He changes His tone. Eveything He said so far was generic, for all mankind (لِّلنَّاسِ  ) but then he identifies a group from among mankind, and calls them أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَبِ people of intellect! So even though this message is for everyone, only people of intellect will actually believe and accept!
 

So today I want to talk to you about the Quran in light of it being a message, a conversation...
When we communiate , there are essentially 3 components, that we must keep in mind. There are 3 aspects to communiating:
First is the speaker - Who is saying it
Second is the speech - What is being said
Third is the style - How it is being said
Ask kids to Repeat

So now let's talk about these components individually:

Speaker
The first is the speaker . When we see someone speaking, before the person opens his mouth, by just the way he is dressed, the way he looks, his age, his body language, based on these things, we've already made a judgment about this person. We've already formed an opinion. Think about it this way...

When you have an important question related to shareeat, that you're seeking religious guidance for. You walk into the jamaat khana. You see hazratain sitting, mulla sahibaan sitting, some other people of knowledge in there, and you also see your not so religious, not so knowledgeable friend sitting in the corner. After the musafaha, you sit down and present your question to the elders. As soon as your finish, your friend starts answering your question. Would you take the answer seriously? Or would you prefer Hazrat sahab to answer your questions? Why? Now what if they both gave you the exact same answer? Would you still prefer the answer from Hazrat sahab? Why? they're both giving you the same answers.
The reason is not that your friends answer is wrong, but because it's coming from a source that you don't really acknowledge for that content. But when Hazrat sahab gives you the exact same answer, it holds lot more value to you. So who did you take into consideration there? The speaker!
Another example, you're not feeling well. You go to your friends house, you see a relative there who is a doctor. You tell him about your illness... would you prefer your uncle prescribed a medicine for you, or would you prefer your friend suggested something? Why?
Again, who did you take into consideration there? The speaker! The speaker is a big deal!

Speech
The next component is speech. The content that is being delivered.
You come to a khutba, a sermon. You go to the masjid to hear the kutba. You get there a little late so you did not get a chance to enter the main hall, so you just sat outside. You didn't get a chance to see the khateeb (The person delivering the sermon). The khateeb is a young kid. Let's say it's Saleem here! Shadia's son Saleem. He is the khateeb. He comes to the minbar wearing  jeans and a T-Shirt and a base ball cap truned backwards. Now remember you can't see him... And he gives the most killer khutba you've EVER heard! Now, not having seen the speaker, what are you thinking? You're thinking, with who did this Sheikh, this Maulana sahab study with? So what did you take into consideration this time? Not the speaker, but the what? the speech! So the speaker is important, but the speech, the content is just as important. you understand?

Style
The third thing that is important is style.
It's time for namaaz, you're busy playing outside the masjid. A mulla sahab on his way to the masjid walks by, and starts yelling at you. Why are you playing outside? Why aren't you in the masjid praying? Is this your gratitude to Allah for all his blessings? Shame on you! Stop this nonsense immediately and go to the masjid right now! And then he walks away. What do you do? You wait for a few seconds, look at each other, share a laugh and get back to playing. A few moments later, another mulla sahab walks by and very nicely, very kindly, reminds you that it's time for namaaz... says something like... kids! you might not have realized that it's time for namaaz. I know you guys are having fun, but our lord, our creator is calling us! Let's show Him our gratitude for all his blessings, by hastening towards his call.  I'm on my way to majid right now. Would you like to give me company? Would you like to walk with me? You immediately stop playing and start walking with him.
Now both are saying the same thing! Both are asking you to stop playing, and go for namaaz. But one's words inspire, you, the others turn you off. They're both mulla so speaker is the same, they're both asking you to pray, so the content is the same. So then what's different this time? The style!  So style - the way in which something is said, is extremely important too! Both mullas were telling you the same thing. But the way in which they delivered the content, either made it effective, or ineffective. So not only are the speaker and the speech important, but the way in which you present the content has to be effective, otherwise the communication is lost.

In the end the point of communication is to influence the audience. I'll say that 3 times!
The purpose of communication is not just to have someone listen to some things or see some things , but to influence them! Influence a change in them. And you cannot do that until you speak effectively, even if you have something good to say.

Let me give you another example. You listen to a majlis - incredible content ! but the way in which it is delievered is extremely monotone. It's extremely monotone! Imagine this, you're in a majlis and the speaker goes....

When Allah the Most High decided to create the earth, sun, moon, stars, galaxies--- He commanded them: "Be!" and they were. Almighty Allah said: Verily, His Command, when He intends a thing, is only that He says to it, Be!" and it is!

He just recited to you the translation of an ayah. Now this content is profound! Ayat of the Quran is being being quoted. A really important statement is being made about the creation of the universes, but the way in which it's being stated is SO BORGIN, that it will knock you out! It's verbal anesthesia! It'll put you to sleep! And you see... there is nothing wrong with the content, because the content is divine! And there may be nothing wrong with the speaker. He may be a very knowledgable man. But what's the problem this time? The mode of speech! The style of speech! How something is being presented! You understand?
 

So these are the 3 dimensions of communication. And quran is powerful in all 3 dimensions of communication.
It is powerful because it's speaker is incredible
It is powerful because it's content is incredible
It is powerful because it's style is incredible

When all three components are incredible, what does it make the conversation? Incredible!


But what I want to focus on with you today is not the first dimesnion, which is the speaker; not the second which is the content, but the third! Not that Allah says it, or what Allah says, but HOW Allah says it. This is where the Quran is SO unique in the way it communicates. The way in which Allah says stuff is so powerful and so incredible!!!... you see... the Quran when it's translated.... you already know who the message is from.
