Now maybe... maybe... you get the content, of what he says... maybe... But what is it that you will DEFINITELY not get?? How he said it! That's completely, 100% lost in translation.
What he said, maybe you get out of translation... maybe. But how He said it is completely gone! Because how he said it exists in which language? in Arabic. Even if you explain it to someone in Arabic, in your own Arabic , it will still not be how He said it. So the creative beauty, the unique beauty, the unparallel majesty of Allah's words, cannot be translated. Something from the meaning can be translated. But the impact that the original words have, nothing else can have. This is what I want us to understand and appreciate today. That translating the words of Allah, into our words, first of all, cannot capture the originally intended content, but it certainly destroys the style of communication. It creates a gap. And in order to understand the distance of this gap, think of the distance between Allah and his creation... That should give you an idea of the distance between Allah's words and man's translations. Because Quran is from Allah, and the translation is from his creation. Man's translation can NEVER be a substitue for Allah's words. It just can't be.
This is not a debate on what's lost in the conent. We could certainly talk about that too, and I can give you numerous examples of that. But what I want to talk to you about today, is what's lost in the beauty, in the style...

So now I want you to think about this...
We as Muslims... who do we believe to be the ultimate speaker of this communication? Who is this message from? Allah! We as Muslims believe this. But when a non-muslim or Kaafir , hears this communication, who does he see delivering the message? Who does he believe to be the speaker? Rasool Allah! This is very important to understand. To you and I, this is the message of Allah, but to the disbeliever, it's not the message of Allah, because he does not believe in Allah. If he did, that would make him what? A muslim! So a kafir believes this is message of Rasool Allah.

What are the 3 components of communication? So logically speaking, this Quran, based on the components of speaker and content , could be a miracle to a Muslim, but could never be a miracle to the disbeliever. You understand? Because they do not believe in the speaker. And when you don't believe in the speaker, you don't even want to listen to the content.

So then, the question we have to ask is, what was it that made this communication a miracle to the disbelievers?? What was it that mesmerized these people? What was it that everytime the messenger of Allah (PBUH) opened his mouth, they would be dumbfound. They didn't know what to say...  It was it's incredible style! It was the style, the beautiful way in which it was being said, that was the miracle for the disbelievers. SubhanAllah! I'm going to share with you 3 stories to highligh the effectiveness of Quran's style, and then I'm done InshaAllah Ta'ala!

Tufail bin A'amir Al-Dausi
He was the leader of his tribe. The best man in the group would be chosen a leader. And what was the measuring stick of virtue to the Arabs of that time? Language! So if Tufail bin Amir was a leader, it's implied that he was above the rest in what? Language! He was the best poet of his tribe.  Now imagine a person in his position... Is this someone who would be easily impressed by another poet?

He goes to Makka to perform tawaaf of the Ka'aba. The leaders of Quraish find out that he's coming. They get worried that he might hear the prophet reciting Quran. And if that happens, he might get enchanted. so they meet him as he enters the city, and informs him about the prophet. They say to him... There is a man here who says these words, and puts a spell on people. He turns sons against their fathers, brother against brother, husband against wife.It is causing disunity in our tribe. These people are being posessed by deamons when he speaks to them. We think you're an important figure for your tribe. We think your integrity is going to be compromised if he is able to cast his spells on you. And as they're saying this they hand him ear plugs. They say, in order to be safe, just plug your ears so that you do not listen to what he says... Now why did they do that? Because what happens when a leader becomes muslim? The tribe members follow! So they had to take care of business!

Tufail Ibn A'amir goes to the Ka'ab and does his tawaaf. The messanger of Allah is sitting there reciting Quran. He hears the sweetness of the words, and realizes this is the man they were talking about. So what does he do? He plug his ears and starts running away. Half way in his jog he stops and thinks to himself. I am the leader of my tribe! I am the pride of my people! There is not a single poet who can stand against me in eloquence. I am one of the most skillful poets in all of arabia. Some words are going to overpower me? I can take this! He unplugs his ears and goes back to the messenger of Allah, sits next to him and asks: یا محمد! ما عندک؟ Oh Muhammad show me what you have!

Now this is where I want you to pay close attention. I'm going to recite what Rasul Allah tells him. You know who the speaker is! You will not understand the content, but what I want you to focus on is the beauty of the words. I want you to pay attention to how the words sound. I want you to feel how each words fits like a jewel in a ring. Rasul Allah turns to him and says
 

أِنَّ الحَمدَ لِی اللہَ أحمَدَہُ وَ أستَعِینَہُ وَ أُومِنُ بِہِ وَ أتَوَکَّلُ عَلَیہِ ۔ مَن یَّھدِہِ اللہُ فَلاَ مُضِلَّ لَہ وَ مَن یَّضِلَّ لَہُ فَلاَ ھَادِیَ لَہ

He says... All praise is for Allah! I praise Him, and I seek his help, and I believe in Him, and I put my trust in Him, for the one He guide can never stray, and the one he misleads, can never be guided

Tufail Ibn Amir hears this one sentence and he's stunned! He is mesmerized! He says...

أُعِد أُعِد! فقد بلغنا قاموس البحر

Repeat it ! Repeat it! Indeed your once sentence has sucked up the 7 seas!

Now this time you know all 3 components of this communication. See if you can feel the power of this communication now. The messenger of Allah (PBUH) again says

أِنَّ الحَمدَ لِی اللہَ أحمَدَہُ وَ أستَعِینَہُ وَ أُومِنُ بِہِ وَ أتَوَکَّلُ عَلَیہِ ۔ مَن یَّھدِہِ اللہُ فَلاَ مُضِلَّ لَہ وَ مَن یَّضِلَّ لَہُ فَلاَ ھَادِیَ لَہ


Tufail bin Amir, with tears in his eyes, he puts his hands in the hand of the prophet and says... I submit! Make me a muslim! He recites the sahadah (لا الہ الا اللہ) right there and then. One Ayah! One Ayah! SubhanAllah! He takes the shahadah and then narrates his story. This is how we know about it.

3 Kuffar

Abu Jahal, Abu Sufiyan, Akhnas bin Suraiq, are the three leaders of Quraish. The greatest haters of Rasul Allah (PBUH). They were the seniors, the elders of the Quraish! They were the think tank. And they would sneak in the middle of the night and put their ears on the walls of the prophets (PBUH) home, and would listen to the Quran being recited. And then they would sneak back before the morning, and what happened the first night was they ran into each other. Each of them didn't know the other was there. So the first guys goes, what are you doing here? and he says what are you doing here? haha
They knew what they were doing there. So they swore they would never ever return. They caught each other the next night! They again swore they won't come back. They caught each other the third night. This time they swear by their mothers, their fathers their gods, their lives that they will never come back again. So they don't come back.

But Akhnas is curious. he goes to Abu Sufyan and he says to him. We have been listening for 3 days. what do you think? is this the truth? Abu sufiyan says to him "Ofcourse it's the truth. What else can it be?". OK! Let's talk to Abu Jahal, he's been listening too right? So they both go to Abu Jahal and they ask him.... What do you think? Ofcourse it's the truth he says!

Abu Jahal says Ofcourse it's the truth? The greatest enemy of Islam, the greatest hater of Rasul Allah says it's the truth??

But then he explains... You see we're Banu Aa'mir, and Muhammad is Banu Hashim. Right there! Before I tell you anymore, what did he take into consideration? The speech or the speaker? The rejection of the disbeliever came at the fact that these words, as powerful as they are, as true as they are, as flawless as the content is, as mesmerizing as the style is... we are not willing to accept this person at any cost! Because when Banu Hashim gives charity, we match them! When Banu Hashim recites poetry, we match them! When Banu Hashim  show bravery, we match them! But when one of them comes up with these words, we can NEVER match them! So they have won forever! We cannot let that happen! That was the reason!
SubhanAllah! It's a completely different take on history. Even those who hated the Quran were mesmerized by the Quran. Even they were mesmerized by the Quran.

Last stroy

عتبی ابن ربیعۃ
  • 01-21- Day 11B.mp3 [36:21]
     


Uthbah Ibn Rabeea'ah! Think of him as the mafia boss when it came to language. Expert debator! Very insulting to those who opposed him in public discourse! He was a negotiator on behalf of conflicting tribes. You can think of him as a consultant. He was hired for these kinds of jobs. When somebody needed to be destroyed through language, Uthbah was the go to man. He would go in, make demands, and get the job done.

Quraish came to him and said, we're lose this war of words! We have somebody who's really good at words! Somebody really good at debates, at discussion and negotiation. So they hire Utbah. They say Utbah! You go to Muhammad (PBUH). He is sitting there.  You talk to him! Make some demands of him, figure out what he wants. Humiliate him! Destroy him! We'll watch from a distance. Today we watch cricket or basketball games, this was their sport. They enjoyed such debates. So they're excited about this match that's about to take place.

Utbah goes to Rasul Allah. Exteremely rude, extermely condascending. Ya Muhammad!

فرقت دیننا، و شطنتا آلھتنا، و عبت دیننا، و فرقت أِخواننا

You've torn apart our religions, made our lords out to be devils, you've disgraced our religion, and separated brothers from brothers.
From an Arab standpoint, his tone was basicall What the hell is your problem (نعوذباللہ)? What is it that you want from us?


أِنَّما أِن کُنتَ تُرِیدُ بِہِ مُلکاً، مَلَّکناَ عَلی رُؤُسِنَا
Is it authority that you desire? Fine! We make you our leader. We give you complete control of our posessions
أِن کُنتَ تُرِیدُ بِہِ الشَّرَف، لا نَقطَعُ أَمراً دُونَک
Is it honor that you want? Fine! We will not make a single action without your permission
أِن کُنتَ تُرِیدُ بِہِ البآءَ، جَمَعنَا لَکَ نِساَءَ قُرَیشً فَخطَ أَیُّ نِساَءَ قُرَیشً فَنُزَوِّجْکَ عَشَرا
Is it women you desire? Fine! We'll stand all the women of Quraish before you. Any and every woman that you point to, we will give to you in marriage
أِن کُنتَ تُرِیدُ بِہِ المال، جمعنا لک أَموَالَناَ حَتّٰی تَکُونُ أَغنٰی قُرِیشً مالا

Is it wealth you desire? Fine we will collect all our wealth and put it before you. We will make you the richest man of Quraish
Tell us what you want? Make your demands!

He barks at the messenger like a dog! And Rasul Allah is listening quietly, patiently. And when he's done... in a very soft tone, the messenger says to him.... أَفَرغْتَ یا ابا ولید؟ Are you done?
Utbah says, fine! Your turn! Rasul Allah says nothing to him, but recites the Quran. He starts reciting the Ayats of Surah Fussilat.. He says
 

بسم اللہ الرحمن الرحیم
حمٓ۔ تَنزِيلٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحْمَنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ۔

The first thing he says... rough translation... Of course I cannot match what Allah says...
It is something that has been sent down from the exceedingly merciful, the constantly merciful.
You see, when the attack came from Utba, the accusation was... this is your message with a hidden agenda! and you're just trying to get smething out of it. The first thing Quran says in response is what? This is not the prophet's message. Where are they from? It's from Allah! You are not negotiating with my prophet! Who are you debating right now? You're debating Allah. You're not fighting the prophet, you're fighting Allah. I have sent down this message to my prophet

‏كِتَبٌۭ فُصِّلَتْ آيَتُهُۥ قُرْآنًا عَرَبِيًّۭا لِّقَوْمٍۢ يَعْلَمُونَ۔
It's a message, whose demands have been made absolutely clear in the Arabic language (your language)
Remember, he asked for demand? Allah, is responding to his demands.
Allah says, I'll tell you what those demands are! And I'll tell you in plain Arabic so all of you can clearly understand! You wanna know what my demands are? Here they are...
‏بَشِيرًۭا وَنَذِيرًۭا فَأَعْرَضَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ۔
This Quran has 2 functions. Give good news and a warning, a threat! But most of you ignore it on purpose. اعراض  is to ignore on purpose! You are deliberately not listening to what I'm telling you
So Allah is revealing his demands through the Ayat of the Quran to Utbah. And as the ayats are being revealed, people are watching in the distance.
The prophet continues
وَقَالُوا۟ قُلُوبُنَا فِىٓ أَكِنَّةٍۢ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَآ إِلَيْهِ وَفِىٓ آذَانِنَا وَقْرٌۭ ۔   وَمِنۢ بَيْنِنَا وَبَيْنِكَ حِجَابٌۭ ۔ فَٱعْمَلْ إِنَّنَا عَمِلُونَ۔ ‏
قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُكُمْ ۔  يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَهُكُمْ إِلَهٌۭ وَحِدٌۭ ۔ فَٱسْتَقِيمُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ۗ
وَيْلٌۭ لِّلْمُشْرِكِينَ ‏۔ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَهُم بِٱلْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كَفِرُونَ۔ ‏إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّلِحَتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍۢ۔
قُلْ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَكْفُرُونَ بِٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ لَهُ أَندَادًۭا ۚ۔ ذَلِكَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ۔

Now as these ayats are being recited, people see Utbah's expressions start changing. He was comfortably and proudly sitting, and now his face is changing as Allah is presenting his demands..
Then what they notice is he's crying! Utbah started crying! Their expert negotiator, Kaafir, hater of Islam, insultor of Rasul Allah (PBUH), لعنۃ اللہ علیہ
He is listening to the Quran, and he starts crying. As the ayats go on, his emotions start becoming stronger and stronger and his crying starts becoming louder

‏وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَسِىَ مِن فَوْقِهَا وَبَرَكَ فِيهَا وَقَدَّرَ فِيهَآ أَقْوَتَهَا فِىٓ أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍۢ سَوَآءًۭ لِّلسَّآئِلِينَ۔
ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَهِىَ دُخَانٌۭ فَقَالَ لَهَا وَلِلْأَرْضِ ٱئْتِيَا طَوْعًا أَوْ كَرْهًۭا قَالَتَآ أَتَيْنَا طَآئِعِينَ۔
‏فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَوَاتٍۢ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَأَوْحَىٰ فِى كُلِّ سَمَآءٍ أَمْرَهَا ۚ وَزَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِمَصَبِيحَ وَحِفْظًۭا ۚ ذَلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ۔

أَعْرَضُوا۟ فَقُلْ أَنذَرْتُكُمْ صَعِقَةًۭ مِّثْلَ صَعِقَةِ عَادٍۢ وَثَمُودَ

I'll just translate this last verse for you... Allah says, after listing his demands... If you still ignore what I'm saying, I am threatening you with the torment that destroyed the tribes of Aa'd and Samud.
As soon as Utbah hears this, he jumps forwards and puts both his hands on the mouth of the prophet! and says! Stop! Please Stop! Please do not recite another word!
The messenger of Allah continues till the ayats of Sajdah and then falls in Sajdah.

Utbah gets up and returns to the people who sent them. This expert debator they had hired to quiet the messenger of Allah. Who their hopes were lying in. When they see his face, they say... The Utbah returning is not the one who left from here! This is not the face you went with. Utbah says that whatever this man says will one day become an enormous event. I don't want to go into each one of them, but I'll talk a bit about the last one

أَعْرَضُوا۟ فَقُلْ أَنذَرْتُكُمْ صَعِقَةًۭ مِّثْلَ صَعِقَةِ عَادٍۢ وَثَمُودَ
And if they continue to ignore deliberately, then you tell them! Now who's you? Muhammad (PBUH). By the words he realizes, that he is not talking to Rasul Allah, he is conversation with Allah right now. Allah is not dignifying Utbah, Allah is telling the messenger to tell him. Allah is not telling him directly. Allah could have said أَنذَرْتُكُمْ I am warning you! But he said  فَقُلْ أَنذَرْتُكُمْ you warn them! Warn them of what? Explosions! صَعِقَةًۭ مِّثْلَ صَعِقَةِ عَادٍۢ وَثَمُودَ Just like the explosions that occurred with the tribes of Aa'd and Samud. This was something the Arabs knew. They knew this had happened in the past. And now Utbah sitting realizes, that he has been issued a direct threat from Allah himself. He was c0mpletely overpowered. He could not hear another word. He tries to stop the messenger by putting his hands on his mouth.

He comes back and tells his people, that this is what the messenger said. I suggest you take him seriously, because you all know that Muhammad has never ever lied.

سیکون نبأ عظیما He says... His words are going to become a huge event. They say to him سحَّرَک He has done his magic on you too! Utbah says say what you will, it's not magic, but I'm not talking to him anymore. I'm done! He came down on his knees

I wanted to share these stories with you because I wanted to show you a tragedy. The tragedy is that the disbelievers at the time of the prophet (PBUH) and the believers shared one thing. They had one thing in common. They were both over powered by what? The Quran! They were both overpowered by the Quran. But we, the believers, the muslims and the momineen of this time... not to mention the disbelievers. I'm talking about the believers, we don't appreciate the power of the Quran. We are not stunned, mesmerized, over whelmed, by the words of Allah, even in comparison to the way that the disbelievers used to be mesmerized back then. And that's a shame! That's a real tragedy. That affect of the Quran, is something that is lost.
And it's lost to the point, where poeple actually can criticise the Quran. Why does it say this, why does it say that. They're not over powered.
 

Any form of communication or literature is subjective. You like a book I don't. You like a conversation, I don't. It's based on personal opinion. So then how do we judge a good conversation? What is the measuring stick? What influence does it have on the people. What impact it has on the world. What change does it bring? How does it change people? How does it influence behavior? How does it command over people? That would be the judge! That is a practical judge! Of the power of any speech, any literature! You could say it's wonderful, but I can say it's just fiction. Or you could say it's wonderful and it completely changed my life.

If you study this fact historically, no society went through such transformation based on a single text, the way in which the muslims went through with what? Quran. No other society in history. And in how many years did that society get transformed? 23 years! Even orientalists agree to this! First Ayat is revealed at the age of 40, and it stops at the age of 63. Just 23 years.


 

has there ever been a change in the world.....  that happened in the course of 23 33 53 63  years.....  I don't care let's talk about 500 years.....  I change that changed the way people think.....  what people love.....  what people hate.....  how people dress.....  how different the government how they run their economics.....  how they get married how they get divorced.....  what they eat what they don't eat.....  what they say what they don't say.....  how they do business.....  how to take a loan.....  it changed every aspect of their life in 23 years..... 

 those of you who are in high school you take global history.....  and you learn about revolutions.....  you learn about the French Revolution in Europe.....  these revolutions were either political or economic.....  they did not change the ethics of the people.....  they did not change how people eat sleep and drink.....  they did not change what people love and hate.....  they did not change how people dress.....  nothing else changed.....  there was just one big change over the top and that's it.....  and even those changes were brought about by people.....  who read the works of philosophers.....  and those philosophers left decades before.....  I'll give you an example.....  the Russian Revolution.....  the communist revolution.....  you know who the author of  of the communist.....  revolution is? It's Karl Marx..... .....  you know where Karl Marx is from?.....  He's from Germany.....  and he died 60 years before the Russian Revolution.....  the guy was a librarian.....  he was a writer.....  philosopher.....  60 years back he sitting in a room writing about all of this stuff.....  it's not even in his wildest dreams that people will be killing each other over this.....  and 60 years after his death.....  there are people revolutionizing  a country based on his works..... right?.....  you see there is this historical formula.....  the philosopher of an idea.....  is never on the battlefield.....  never.....  and the Philosopher's of an idea is not himself a revolutionary he is simply a philosopher
he's not going out there to convince people to make a change.....  he's just writing.....  in the case of Muhammad (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam)  who's delivering the message? and who is on the battlefield?.....  and not just in the battlefield who is in the front of the battlefield?.....  who's delivering the message and who is being boycotted and being forced to live in a cave.....  who is being expelled from his house?.....  he's not just standing behind some podium and telling the people to march forward.....  he is the first to march forward when there's nobody standing behind him..... this has never before happened in history.....  ever.....  ask your political science professor.....  ask your history professor.....







In 23 years, this man starts, single handedly mind you, by the end of these 23 years what change have these words, and this speaker , and this speech and this style of speech... What change have they influenced?

You see... the world has seen many revolutions to bring about change. But whenever you have a quote unquote revolution, those changes are either political or economic in nature, or the military chagned. But the way people live on a day to day basis, that does not change. If a new government comes into power, it doesn't change children interact with their parents, or how husbands talk to their wives. That's still the same. But look at the change the Quran brought! Unparallel in human history!

The way people eat, did that change? The way people sleep, did that change? Did the way people deal with their families change? Did the way husbands treat their wives change? Did how people schedule their day change? did the way people speak to each other change? Did the way they greet one another change? Did the way they do business change? Did the way they dress change? Did the way they go to the bathroom change?
The question isn't what changed! You know what the right question is? What didn't change? Everything changed! Everything changed!

What exactly changed wasn't just societal, wasn't just on the macro leve. It also changed the way people think. It changed everything! everything! from the very individual level to the collective level and every spectrum in between. This itself is the evidence of the unparallel power of the Quran. The unparallel power of the Quran. SubhanAllah

Now relate this to the definition of a miracle - this is the first miracle.
InshaAll in next session... selecting 7 out of 350 miracles..
Screen share -
 

 

I have a monumental task before me today. InshaAllah, I'm going to start showing you the miracles of Quran today. I'm nervous, as much as I'm excited. I'm nervous becuase I feel this is a very difficult task for an amature like me. But I'm excited, because by the blessings of my elders, Allah has give me this opportunity to share this incredible stuff with you.

InshaAllah, I'm going to try my best to explain these to you as clearly as I can. But if you don't understand, please stop me and ask me to explain it again. Because if you don't, then it will defeat the whole purpose and everything we've learnt so far will be of no use. This is the goal we've been working towards in the previous sessions.

Now before we start experiencing these miracles, you have to keep in mind everything that I've stated so far. Like I said before, we can't experience these miracles in these times. We have to think of the Arabs of  Rasul Allah's time that spoke Ancient Arabic. In fact, not just think, we need to BECOME, we need to BE the Arabs of that time in order to truly appreciate these miracles.

Just a quick recap... How many types of Arabic did we discuss previously? What are they? Spoken, Proper and Ancient
So when the Quran was revealed, it created a category of it's own. It was not just one step ahead of ancient arabic, it was miles ahead.  Quranic Arabic reached, where Ancient Arabic never reached. So the pinnacle of Ancient Arabic is the Quranic Arabic. This is why even today, the ultimate standard of Arabic is the Quran. To judge whether the Arabic of  some content, is good or bad, the standard is Quranic arabic. this is understood and appreciated even by non arab literaries.
Rather than doing a full recap of all previous sessions, I'll just get into the details, as and when they're applicable to a particular miracle... As I mentioned previously, Quran is not a miracle in a single dimension. It's a miracle in various dimensions. So InshAllah, I'll show you a miracle or two from each dimension. I'm in no rush over this. We'll go at whatever pace we need to. So once again, if you don't understand, please let me know, and I will explain it as many times as you need me to. OK?

Now you may experience a miracle in each and every example that I show you; or you may experience it in some particular dimension. But I'm confident that InshaAllah you will get a taste of it. And that's all we're after. I want you to remember the feeling that you had, when you were standing next to the bank of the river as Moosa (AS) parted the water with his staff. If you get the same feeling today, in your heart, then you'll know that you've experienced a miracle.

Let's just become aware of what a miracle is... but remembering what it's not. It's not a magic trick. It's not something for the eyes to see. Because remember, a miracle is something you experience in your heart, not something you understand with your intellect. It has to do primarily and ultimately with the heart. Just having the information about the miracle is not enough. And I'll support that statement from the Quran

In the Quran when we think of ایمان , one of the prophets that stands out is Ibrahim (AS). In the Quran we see his prayer:

وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَهِیمُ رَبِّ أَرِنِى كَيْفَ تُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ
And Ibrahim says, o my lord! show me how you bring the dead back to life. Allah says..
قَالَ أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِن؟
Why Ibrahim? Don't you believe? Don't you have faith? Why does Allah ask that? Because if one has faith, then why would one ask for proof?  Ibrahim (AS) says
قَالَ بَلَىٰ وَلَكِن لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِى
Of course I have faith! No doubt about it. But I ask you to show me so that it eases my heart. Notice he did not say لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ عقلى So that it eases my intellect! He said, لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِى so that it eases my heart. Allah through this incident teaches us that despite having faith, the heart can be uneasy. And the purpose of miracles, are not to ease the intellect, it is to ease the heart, even though it affects both. See... we normally think, that we need to understand the miracle intellectually, but the Quran is teaching us that we need to experience the miracle of Quran emotionally... from the heart.
This is why I started these sessions with the miracle of Quran so that it puts your heart to ease. Once your heart is at ease, the message will automatically sink in InshAllah.

That was the greatest road block for me on my journey as well. That's why I feel gaining all this knowledge wasn't helping me at all. Because it didn't enter my heart. It was like for the longest time I sat next to a swimming pool, and tried to read and study about swimming. But only when I jumped into the water, I was able to learn how to swim.

We discussed a few miracles in the previous session ... I showed you the miracle of "Style" right?... How the style affected the non believers of the time. How Tufail Ibnu Amar AlDausi, an eloquent poet, leader of his tribe, turns Muslim just by listening to a single Ayat of the Quran. It took this poet, just one single ayat to get mesmerized! Then I told you about Utba Ibn Rabeeah, the most eloquent debator of that time. How he challenges Rasool Allah, and when the prophet (AS) recites the ayats of Surah Fussilat, how he gets over powered... starts crying.. and physically tries to stop the prophet by placing both hands on his mouth.

Then we discussed how the purpose of any communication is to influence the audience. And the success of any communication is based on how it changed lives. And how this Quran, as a communication as بلاغ المبین influenced the people of that time. How it brought about an unprecedented change at the very micro level! No other message, no other messenger, brought such a change in the history of time.

So today, let's actually start looking at some of these miracles. I told you that the arabic word for a "miraculous sign" is "Ayah". Every ayah is a miracle of the Quran! But not just the ayaats.... the book itself, it's structure, it's ability to be memorized like any other book, it's eloquence, the imagination it potrays, it's literary perfection, it's linquistic excellence, it's perfection in the choice of words... There are so just many dimensions in which this book is a miracle. So let's take an outside-in approach with this Quran. Let's look at the Quran from outside and then reach into its ayats...

I have selected 2 miracles that we can experience, looking  at the Quran from outside....

Miracle of Rememberance

The first miracle I'm going to start with, is what I call the miracle of Rememberence...

Volunteer

Is Quran an oral tradition or a written tradition? Oral! This is the first thing that fascinates me about this content. It's a spoken text. Now you tell me... is it harder to preserve an oral tradition, or a written tradition? Oral! It is exteremely difficult to preserve an oral tradition. I'll give you an example... Do you know what chinese whisper is?
Let's say that we as a group play this game of Chinese whisper... I start with you, whisper in your ear "Aslam bumped into Faisal".... OK? Now you whisper it Yasmeen, she whispers it to Hussain, he whispers it to Nadia, she whispers it to Saleem and we go on... What was the original sentence? By the time it reaches the last member of this group... it has turned into "Aslam, burned Faisal's house down, destroyed his car, and killed his whole family"... this is something that happens in oral tradition. Nobody wanted to intentionally changed the original message. Somebody just decided to say the same sentence in their own way... they added or took away 1% of the sentence. Somebody else another half percent. Somebody else another 2%. Now 1 to 2% is not a major change. Is it? But when it passes through multiple people, the original message becomes completely unrecognizable. These kinds of experiments are done in communicaiton psychology all the time. Any kind of oral tradition is impossible to preserve. And the more it travels, the more people it passes through the more it changes... the more it varies. It's only logical.
Now by comparison, written tradition is safe. But not entirely safe. In the time of the prophet (AS), it's not like they had image scans, back up hard drives, .. so even if you have a written book, a line could get smudged, a paragraph could become illegible, it could get torn. So if the text reaches down generation, that could create skepticism about the integrity of the original content, right?

Now the Quran is an oral expression. Now when it's compiled in the form of a book, it's not compiled in the order of the revelation. What was the style of recording teh Quran in those days? Rasul Allah would recite a passage as the divine inspiration (wahi) would come to him. The people of memorize it. Now we normally think, and the argument against the Quran is  how could they memorize it so accurately...

I'm going to say a sentence to you... and I want you to recite it back to me verbatim.. ok? In my last session, I explained how the Quran as a conversation was a miracle of speaker, speech and style... Now repeat it... you see you were able to repeat it back to me accurately. was it hard? No! Do you know why? Becuase I stated it in a language that you're very familiar with. You have comamnd over. So the Quran was not revealed to us desis... it was revealed to Arabs... who were masters of what? the arabic language! So memorizing Quran to us may be a very difficult task, but for them it was a piece of cake. you understand?

So as the Quran is being revealed, people around the Rasool Allah, are listening to them, and memorizing it down to the syllable by heart. Then they would travel far and wide and recite the Quran to the Arabs of other cities... they would listen to it, memorize it and then teach others.

Now add to this the challenge of the change in order of the Quran. Maulana ALi in the time of Hazrat Usman, compiled the Quran in it's form today. The Quran is not compiled in its revelation form, it's compiled in a different form. The first ayat that was revealed was
ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ‎
and the last ayat revealed was
ٱلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِى وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ ٱلْإِسْلَمَ دِينًۭا
but this is not the order we find in the Quran. The first ayat is
بسم اللہ الرحمن الرحیم
of surah fateha and the last ayat is
من الجنۃ و الناس
of Surah Al-Naas

OK, so these Arabs during the time of Rasool Allah, memorized the Quran, left Arabia and travelled to different countries and started spreading the Quran. Now understand that once they left their land, they are cut off from the main land. It's not like they had phones or email.. and they're calling each other to confirm the ayats... Hey was it AlHamdu Lillahi or AlHamda Lillahi... right? Once you're cut off you're cut off.

So these people are memorizing the Quran and spreading it, so the logical assumption should be that within a year there should have been a 100 versions of the Quran. Right? An uncountable variations of the original tradition. That's what should have happened.

Now fast forward to 2015.

Today, almost every mosque in the world has a Quran memorization program. hundreds of thousands of kids memorize the Quran each year. We all have too.. atleast some surahs right.

Have you ever been to Indonesia? Do you speak Indonesean? OK! So let's say you travel to Indonesia which is a muslim country.... You're among a bunch of local girls who don't speak English or Urdu. And you don't speak Indonesian. You start reciting Surah Al-Fateha... You say one syllable wrong... just one syllable. You say... AlHamda Lillahi... 3 girls will immediately stop you. they'll say no it's AlHamdu lillahi! Isn't that incredible? You don't know their language, they don't know yours, you've never met before... but as soon as you recite the Quran, you're unified in one language.

If I'm at the airport, it's time for namaaz... a man stands up and starts praying. Other muslims join him in prayer. He starts reciting the Quran. Complete strangers... never met each other. if he recites one word, one letter, one syllable of one word wrong, everyone will correct him with the right syllable. By the syllable - SubhanAllah!

Which city do you live in Karachi? Ok let's assume for a second... All the Qurans of the world have been destroyed.. Every book, every digital copy, every form of it is destroyed. It's no longer available anywhere... not on the internet, not on computers, not on your phone, not in any book shelf... completely gone. Not just in (city) but everywhere in the world. OK?
The only copy left is the original text that has been preserved in some library in Russia let's say. There is no other form of Quran available any where in the entire world. You understand?

I can guarantee you that within 24 hours you will have the original Quran back... in your city. Not even another city, just your city. How? Because of the kids in (city) who have memorized it. You give each kid a few pages to write and within 24 hours the entire book will be back. And when they reproduce it in writing and you compare it to the libary copy of the Quran.. guess what? it will match down the last syllable! SubhanAllah! Tell me of one book in the entire world with which something like this is possible. SubhanAllah! This is how ingrained Quran is in the hearts of the Muslims. And this book is over 600 pages. It's insane. This stuff fascinates me! How can the world be so unified when it comes to this one single book? So many people are memorizing the Quran, logically we should have so many versions of the Quran. But AllahuAkbar!

OVer time documents become less viable..... less valid..... This is the only text that becomes more and more valid over time..... You kow the biblical studies..... most Bible scholars that are doing PhD from universities here.....  Bible scholars.....  they no longer believe that the Bible is historically accurate.....  they themselves don't believe it.....  listen to the comments by Professor Gerald Derks.....  listen to his interview.....  he has a Masters Degree in divinity.....  and he does not believe in the Bible.....  you could not get rid of OIUR message if you tried.....


This is incredible stuff. When I learnt this for the first time, I was blown away... How is this possible? You know... The first time I read the Quran, I read its translation. Then I don't know what crawled into my brain... I got motivated to learn Arabic. My goal became to learn enough Arabic to be able to read and understand this book without translation. I did not want to speak in Arabic, I didn't want to order a filafil in Arabic, I did not want to make Arab friends... I just wanted to understand this content without the filter of translation. Hamdulillah I've been studying Arabic since... And the more I study, the more I realize that this book is truly amazing. It is unique in every form. To this day this book mesmerizes me. The more I sit and study it, the more I'm enchanted by it. And when I learnt this... I just felt people need to know about this... it's just too amazing to not be recognized.

My intention is not to shove this down anybody's throat. I do this for my own fascination. See... Islam is a huge subject. There are many categories of Islam, both in it's zaahir and baatin... in it's exoteric form as well as its  esoteric form. There's shareeah, seerat, fiqah, hadeeth, theology studies... But to me, personally, the only thing I want to study is this book. Because to me this book contains Islam in its most complete form.

Anyway... moving on...

Structural Miracle

The next miracle I want to talk about is its unique strucutre. For this I'd like to speak with someone who is aware of the structure of the Quran.

First of all what is the Quran? It's a message, in the form of what? A book! Even the Quran calls it a kitaab.

ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ نَزَّلَ ٱلْكِتَبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ
آمَنَ ٱلرَّسُولُ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِۦ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۚ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَلَٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِ

Let's look at the structure of this book. You've read books haven't you? So you know something about the organization of a book right? What does a book normally have? It has a table of contents, chapters, paragraphs, lines, index at the end, right?

Now let's look at the Quran's structure

The Quran is made up of how many surahs? 114 surahs. What is the word "Surah" translated into in English? Chapter! Now you tell me! Chapter 1, Ch2, Ch3, Ch4... 
In any book, is Ch4 based on Ch 3? Yes it is.
Now what's stated in in Chapter 3...  is it again repeated in Chapter 4? and then again in Chapter 5 and then again in Chapter 6? No it's not! There is no repetition. There is some sort of logical and sequential progression across all chapters.
But does the Quran repeat itself across multiple chapters? Yes! It has already stated the story of Moosa (AS), in chapter 2, it does it again in 3, in 4, in 5. There are 70 passages just related to Moosa (AS), and they are all over the place, in almost every single chapter. And they too are not in chronological order. They are in all kinds of order! So then these are not really chapters, are they?
And then if you examine the individual chapters of the Quran, numerous topics are covered with sudden switches from one topic to another and then back again. Because what WE call chapter is something else. A story book has chapters too. But what happens is that the chronology moves forward. So you cannot compare the surah to a chapter. Surah is Quran's own proprietary term. There is no such thing as a Surah in any other book.

Now what does a Surah contain? Ayaats! What is the word "Ayah" translated as? Verse! Where have you heard the word verse before? Not in religion but ouside of Islam, where have you heard the word verse before? What comes to mind when you hear the word verse. In poetry, songs, or biblical contexts. So when you hear the word verse, you're thinking of a song, a poem or the bible. Now the Quran is very adamant in letting us know that this content is not poetry:
وما علمنہ الشعر و ما ھو بقول شاعر
We did not teach you poetry! This Quran is not poetry. The last thing we should be thinking as Muslims, is that this is poetry. And the last thing we should be thinking is that this content of the Quran has been plagurized from the bible. But the 2 things that come to mind when you hear the word verse, is poem and bible. Maybe not to our mind because we're so conditioned to this, but to the average non muslim, that's the first thing that comes to their mind. The word ayat is unique. No other book has ayats. It's unique! It's a proprietary term of the Quran. Both Surah and ayats are proprietary. Copyrights of Allah!

You can't even think of an ayah as a sentence. What's the definition of a sentence?
a grammatical unit of one or more words that expresses an independent statement, and that typically has a subject as well as a predicate

Now in the Quran, can you think of one word that is a complete ayah? Al-Rehman! Just one word that is a complete Ayah! Have you every read a sentence that just said "The Magnificent" "The Benevolent". But in the Quran, just one word can be an ayah.

Then, I can think of 3 ayats that go together to make one whole sentence.
الحمد للہ رب العالمین، الرحمن الرحیم مالک یوم الدین
These are three ayats but grammatically one sentence. The previous was one word but a complete ayah. Now there are 3 ayats but grammatically one single sentence. You don't have this in any other language. Then there are entire passages, entire paragraphs dedicated to explaining an entire subject, but just one ayah

مُّحَمَّدٌۭ رَّسُولُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥٓ أَشِدَّآءُ عَلَى ٱلْكُفَّارِ رُحَمَآءُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ تَرَىٰهُمْ رُكَّعًۭا سُجَّدًۭا يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًۭا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِضْوَنًۭا ۖ سِيمَاهُمْ فِى وُجُوهِهِم مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلسُّجُودِ ۚ ذَلِكَ مَثَلُهُمْ فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ ۚ وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِى ٱلْإِنجِيلِ كَزَرْعٍ أَخْرَجَ شَطْـَٔهُۥ فَـَٔازَرَهُۥ فَٱسْتَغْلَظَ فَٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَىٰ سُوقِهِۦ يُعْجِبُ ٱلزُّرَّاعَ لِيَغِيظَ بِهِمُ ٱلْكُفَّارَ ۗ وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّلِحَتِ مِنْهُم مَّغْفِرَةًۭ وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًۢا

A whole paragraph in Surah Al-Fatah! But one single ayah! You cannot confine it to any literary form. There is ONLY one word you can use to describe an ayah and that is an ayah. This makes the structure of the Quran unique. Unlike any other book in the world.

Then you can't categorize the contents of the Quran in any chronological order. Are the Meccan surahs first and then the Madani Surahs? No! They're all mixed in together. Is the shortest Surah first and the longest surah last? Or is the longest Surah first and the shortest Surah last? No! It's not going by size order, it's not going by subject order, it's not going by chronological order. Every other book in the world has an order, a sequence, a progression, but not the Quran. It has its own unique order unlike every other book. 

Even the bibles, the old and new testaments have an order... The old testament is a compilation of many books.

The first 17 are historical books. They gave the history of civilization from creation, to the time of the Persian Empire.  They record the history of the Jewish nation from its inception, through its days of glory and deportation, and finally, to its days of survival as a small, insignificant nation surrounded by enemies intent on destroying it.

Then comes a different set of books, which are known as the poetic books of the Bible: Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and the Song of Solomon. They books don't relate historical experiences.  Rather they relate the experiences of the human heart through poetry. Similar to our munajaat. They delve into the questions of suffering, wisdom, life, love, and most importantly, the character and nature of God.

The next 17 books of the Bible comprise the last division in the Old Testament—the Prophets. Isaiah, Jeremiah, Lamentations, Ezekiel, Daniel, Hosea, Joel, Amos, Obadiah, Jonah, Micah, Nahum, Habakkuk, Zephaniah, Haggai, Zechariah, Malachi.

But the Quran has no such order. This is so evident that orientalists who criticise the Quran have concluded that there is no order to the Quran! It's chaotic (نعوذ باللہ).

There is absolutely nothing comparable to the Quran! SubhanAllah!

If I give you time would you be able to find me a book of a similar structure? So does this make you incapable of a response? So you see even the structure of this book is a miracle!

 














We're experiencing the miracles of Quran. We have a couple of new people joining in, so I'm going to start by doing a quick recap of the previous sessions... And those who are joining in today, if you have trouble understanding these concepts, then we can have a separate session, and i can go over these concepts with you in more detail.

Faizah

Who is the message of the Quran from?
The words of the Quran.... who do they belong to?
So the message was revealed as "noor" on the heart of Rasul Allah, and he converted into it words for us.

We've discsussed that Quran is essentially a conversation. A conversation that Allah is having through through the medium of his beloved prophet Muhammd (AS)
Who is the audience of Quran? What is the subject of Quran?
What are the two forms, in which the conversation of Quran takes place? Either giving information or expressing emotion. And why is that?  because human beings are capable of only having the same two kinds of conversations. All human conversations can only fall into these 2 categories. Giving information or expressing emotion.
Information is related to which body part? Heart or Intellect? Emotion is related to which body part? Heart or intellect?
So the Quran is a message for both, the heart or intellect!

Who was the Quran originally revealed to? The Arabs
What was the most important thing for an Arab during the time of the prophet? Language
What language did they speak?
We discussed that Arabs had a very powerful imagination! An imagination that you and I are incapable of. We discussed what were the factors that developed their superior imagination. And then on top of that they had this powerful language. So they would draw out their imagination with words in poetry. And I gave you a few examples of that as well.

So the language they spoke was Arabic. How many types of Arabic did we discuss? Which Arabic did the people during the time of the prophet speak?

When a tribe would choose its leader, what was the criteria for selection? Who would be chosen to lead a tribe?
When they would have poetry competitions, what would happen to the person who lost?

So that's the context in which the Quran was revealed. Now let's go over the miracle itself...

What is the arabic word for a miracle? Ejaaz
What does the word mean literally? To bring someone down to their knees, to incapacitate someone, to make someone incapable of a response
What was the example I gave for experiencing Ejaaz?

We talked about the difference between miracle and magic. A miracle is not a magic trick. It's not pulling out a rabbit from a hat....
What is the essential purpose of a miracle?
Is miracle for the heart or for the intellect? Do you see a miracle or do you experience a miracle?


Perfect! Then we started discussing the miracles of the Quran from an outside in approach. Quran is not a miracle in a single dimension. It's a miracle in multiple dimensions. Let's quickly go over some of the dimensions in which we saw the Quran as a miracle.

Quran essentially is a conversation. What is the measuring stick of a good conversation? How it influences its audience; how it influences change in the audience. So the first miracle of Quran we saw was how it changed it's audience at the very micro level. And we discussed how such a change is unprescedented in history.
Then we talked about the 3 components of a conversation... What are they? speaker, speech, style.  - So the Quran is powerful because it's speaker is powerful, its speech is powerful... but the miracle lies in which component? the style. And I shared 3 stories with you to illustrate the power of style, and the effect it had on the disbelievers

Then in the previous session, we discussed how Quran is a structural miracle.... How even though it is a book, it does not conform to the rules of any book. I also explained how it is a miracle of rememberance, and how and why this book can never ever be destroyed... And if all the Qurans of the world were destroyed, how it could be reproduced accurately within 24 hours. Not possible with any other text in the world.
Allah himself challenges mankind.. He says

وَإِن كُنتُمْ فِى رَيْبٍۢ مِّمَّا نَزَّلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍۢ مِّن مِّثْلِهِۦ وَٱدْعُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَدِقِينَ

SO now that we've looked at the Quran from the outside, let's start taking a look at the inside. Let's start examining the ayahs... What is the meaning of the arabic word ayah? Sign and miracle - a miraculous sign. So every ayah of the Quran is a miracle.

Thank you Faizah!

Before we begin, I want everyone to become aware of what a miracle feels like.. remember the exercise we did in order to experience a miracle? So I want you all to remember that feeling. We all know what the heart feels when it experiences a miracle. So become aware of that feeling, so that you recoginize it when you experience it.
 

Scientific Miracle

Today I want to talk about the scientific dimension of the Quran as a miracle... Now as we discussed earlier... that to science, there is no such thing as a miracle. Miracle is an extremely unscientific concept. There is no emperical evidence to prove the existence of heaven, hell, angels, jinn , everlasting life, and all this sort of stuff. We believe in jannah we can eat anything we like, and we never need to go to the bathroom, and we sweat perfume... All very unscientific for someone who doesn't believe. right?

But despite this fact we see that in recent times, this subject - science and Quran - is really picking up momentum. And the reason for it is because the Quran speaks of Allahs creation with one specific intention - the intention of inspiring reflection - inspiring reflection on the beauty of his creation.

أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ ۗ مَا بِصَاحِبِهِم مِّن جِنَّةٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌۭ مُّبِينٌ
إِنَّ فِى ذَلِكَ لَآيَتٍۢ لِّقَوْمٍۢ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ

Allah invites us to reflect! Reflect on what? The subtelties, the design of His creation, the technology that he used in His creation... The technology behind a single leaf, the symmetry involved.... the advanced technology in a single cell... in an atom.. Allah wants us to reflect on it and see how advanced it is. .. With where science is today, which such spectacular advancements... we cannot even create a fly, or a mosquito... Allah wants us to reflect on the technology used to create man.

And I think that's why to the general Muslim population, these scientific phenomenon are so amazing. But the truth is, we may be realizing these facts after 1400 years, but it has been true all along... But something to really appreciate about this dimension, is that today when we read certain ayahs and say, this doesn't seem right, or how is this possible... well it's possible because what was said 1400 years back, is being proven today. Therefore what is being said today, even if it seems impossible today, may be possible 1400 years from now.



So I want to quickly touch upon a few ayahs from this dimension. One interesting example is Surah Al-Hadeed... Hadeed in arabic means "Iron". Allah says..

وَأَنزَلْنَا ٱلْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌۭ شَدِيدٌۭ وَمَنَفِعُ لِلنَّاسِ

And we have sent down iron, which is very powerful, and contains many benefits for mankind

Now Allah talks a lot about His creation in the Quran... but when he does so, he uses the word  خلق.
يَٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ
هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًۭا
إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ لَآيَتٍۢ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَبِ

But when he speaks of iron, he did not use the word خلق. Instead what did he say? وَأَنزَلْنَا .We sent down!
Scholors of the past had been struggling with this concept. Because the words of the Quran are very precise. Muslims would argue that oh by sent it down he actually meant that he created it.... And the argument to that would be .. No! If he wanted to say he created it, he would have used the word خلق. He specifically used the word أَنزَلْنَا  sent it down.
And so we find in the 20th century, geologists coming to agreements about the beginnings of the earth. One of them being that iron is not part of the original earth's substance... It came to the earth in the form of meteor showers. And it got burried very deep into the core of the earth... So the word used أَنزَلْنَا"We sent down" becomes a very accurate depiction of the reality of iron coming into this world. Because it was literally sent down.

Another example is in surah Furqan, Allah say

تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًۭا وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا سِرَجًۭا وَقَمَرًۭا مُّنِيرًۭا
Allah speaks about things in the heavens that are at his disposal..
تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًۭا Blessed is the one who placed stars in the sky.. وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا سِرَجًۭا And then he says... in the sky he place for us a lamp. He calls the sun a lamp in this ayah... and then he says وَقَمَرًۭا مُّنِيرًۭا   . The word qamar means moon.., but the word "muneer" is what is so scientific. THe word muneer means something that is given light. It doesn't have light of its own, it is given light from an external source. Something illuminated... Something that does not give out light, but something that is lit by something else. For example the room you're sitting in is muneer, because of the light. The bulb itself is siraaj, but the room is muneer.
So when Allah spoke of the sun he called it a lamp. But when he spoke of the moon, he called it something that reflects light. So at a time when this is not a known phenomenon, Rasul Allah is speaking of a moon as a body in space that does not give off its own light... I mean today this is common knowlege. But imagine yourself 1400 years ago, its not a very obvious concept. It looks like its giving off its own light. right?

Then another amazingly scientific miracle is Allah says in Surah Al-Ambiya.. describing the heavens and the earth, which is the arabic expression for the universe.

أَوَلَمْ يَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ كَانَتَا رَتْقًۭا فَفَتَقْنَهُمَا
Do the disbelievers not see that the heavens and the earth was a single fused material and we separated them...
The word ratq means something that is fused and inseparable. fused and inseprable. The word ratq is used when a mother is carrying  a child because the mother and child are inseparable. And when she would start delivering, the word used is fataqa. Fataqa is the time for her to start parting. Literally her body is parting up and she is parting from her child.
So the ayah says that the heavens and the earth used to be fused into one body, they were inseparable and then we caused them to come apart. Meaning the universe in its origin was a fused, united body... one matter and then it parted and spread out far and wide. So the big bang theory is interestingly very close to this ayah...
 


Another one is where Allah says...

وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ حَىٍّ

we made the water a source of life for all living things.. Again something stated way ahead of its time...
 





 

So we're talking about the Quran as a miracle... Just a quick recap...
What is Quran? It is a conversation as Allah says: ھذا بلاغ لی الناس. This message is from Allah, but the words are the creation of Rasul Allah (SA). The words of the Quran are not from Allah as the common Muslims believe. So it is a conversation that Allah has with its creation through the words of Rasul Allah (SA)...  Every conversation needs an audience. The audience of Quran is Al-Insaan (Man)! And the subject that the Quran talks about to its audience is: Taqwa (Piety)!

The word miracle معجزۃ comes from the Arabic word أعجاز, which literally means to bring someone down on his knees. It also means to incapacitate someone, or leave someone incapable of a response. A miracle is something we experience with the heart, as opposed to a magic trick or magic that we see with the eyes...

When we hear people talk about the miracles of Quran, at best we hear its miracles in a single dimension of "predictions". Quran speaks about future events that are coming true today. But as I said earlier, the predictions could be a miracle for us, but it could not possibly be a miracle for the Arabs of that time on whom the Quran was being revealed.

Quran is a miracle in various dimensions, not just one dimension....  Some of the dimensions in which we've seen the Quran as a miracle so far are:
As a conversation بلاغ مبین .... We learnt that every conversation has 3 components.... Speaker, Speech (or the content) and Style... We saw that the Quran is powerful in all 3 dimensions, as in its Speaker is powerful; the speech (the content) is powerful, but the true miracle is in it's style. The way in which the content is delivered. That in itself was the true miracle....  Because we know that the disbelievers did not believe in Allah.... And when you don't believe the Speaker, his speech... what he wants to say... holds no importance either .... But then why when they heard Rasul Allah (SA) recite a verse, they immediately accepted Islam? Because of its mesmerizing style... So the style in itself became the miracle.

Then we saw how the Quran is a miracle in terms of its influence. The measuring stick of any conversation, of any communication, is the influence it has on its audience. And we saw how the Quran influenced such a violent and barbaric community, and completely transformed them at the very micro level..... Everything about them changed!

Then we looked at how in the dimension of rememberance, the Quran is a miracle... and how and why this message can never be destroyed as long as man exists.

We also looked at it as a structural miracle! It is a book because the Quran calls it kitaab. And it has the external apperarance of a book, but it in no way shape or form, conforms to the format of any book. It's surahs are called chapters, but they're not really chapters... it's ayats are called verses, but they're not really verses.... There is no apparent order in the way the surahs are organized... It's not in chronological order, it's not in size order, it's not in revelation order, it's not even in the order of the place where it was revealved...

Then I showled you how Surah Fateha is the complete index of the Quran. A book that spans over 600 pages, is fully indexed in 7 beautiful Ayats... something that is impossible with any other book. Then I also showed you how its first ayah BismillahAlRehmanAlRaheem is itself the index of Surah Fateha. SubhanAllah! A book with an index of 7 ayaats, that has it's own index in the form of BismillahAlRehmanAlRaheem... So basically the entire Quran is indexed in its first ayah... BismillahAlRehmanAlRaheem...

Then in the last session we saw the scientific dimension of the Quran, in which Allah speaks of scientific realities that are way ahead of its time.


So we see that the Quran is a miracle in multiple dimensions.... and it is a living miracle... it has remained a miracle for over 1400 years for its audience no matter what period they were born in.
There are certain dimensions in which Quran is a miracl exclusively to us, and not the Arabs
There are certain dimensions in which Quran is a miracle exclusively to the Arabs, and not to us
And then there are certain dimensions in which Quran is a miracle to both, us and the arabs as well.

For example, in the scientific dimension, Quran is a miracle exclusively for us
In the style and influence dimensions, the Quran is a miracle to us and the Arabs as well
In the linguistic dimension, the Quran is a miracle exclusively to the Arabs and not for us, unless we study Ancient Arabic


Today I want us to experience another dimension of Quran's miracle... the dimensions which truly fascinates me... This is the linguistic dimension of Quran. Because remember, the Quran was revealed to a group of people who excelled in language!

And what always fascinated me, and motivated me to learn Ancient Arabic was to understand exactly this...  what was it that these Arabs saw in these ayaats, that a disbeliever would hear the prophet recite one single ayah, or say one single sentence, and they would instantly recite the shahadah.

I remembered this incident when I was doing the session yesterday...

This Christian priest comes to Rasul Allah, with the intention of debate... with the intention to argue... He wanted to show Rasul Allah (SA) that how wrong he was about Islam...
Now when the intention is to debate, or just argue... a person never really listens to what is being said... the intention is never to understand or learn... right? the only reason one listens, is to come up with a response against it... If you say "The sky is blue...",  I'll say "Well! Not when it's sunset! Not when its midnight...  Therefore you're wrong!"  Because my entire goal is NOT to agree with you...


So this Christian priest appreaches Rasul Allah (SA) and starts off his conversation by saying: یا محمد! ما الاسلام؟ Oh Muhammad! What is Islam?
Rasul Allah looks at him, and in a very soft tone replies:
طيب الكلام وَأَفْشُوا السَّلامَ و إطعام الطعام،  وَصَلُّوا بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّاسُ نِيَامٌ
That's all he says... Tears start flowing down the priests eyes... he starts crying... Recites the Shahadah right there!

Now this is amazing to me ... this representative of his religion, goes to rasul Allah, not to learn, but to argue... Rasul Allah says these few words and he accepts Islam right there... I want you to just take a moment and imagine, how powerful these words must be to that priest! And Rasul Allah didn't even say something extrememly profound or earth shattering... He just gave a very simple answer...

When asked what is Islam, all he said was... طيب الكلام  Islam is saying kind words, وَأَفْشُوا السَّلامَ  and Spreading peace, و إطعام الطعام feeding the hungry, and وَصَلُّوا بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّاسُ نِيَامٌ  and praying in the night when the word is sleeping. He summarizes the entire essence, the entire beauty of Islam in 4 things.

He didn't give the priest a lecture on Islam for an hour... Look we're having these weekly sessions on the same topic of "Islam", which will probably go on for months InshaAllah... But Rasul Allah didnt say that to the priest... he didn't schedule a weekly or daily class with him. He just answered with 4 simple things and the priest accepted Islam! Based on just this one sentence, he gives up his religion, and changes his entire lifestyle...  Can you imagine the power of Rasul Allah's words, and the power of this message? SubhanAllah!

I also told you the story of Tufail Ibn Amar AlDausi... And I keep coming back to it, because it is just SO incredible... and so relevant to the linguistic miracle of Quran. Every time I tell you this story I get goosebumps... This man, Tufail Ibn Amar... not any ordinary man... an excellent excellent poet, leader of his tribe... already biased based on what the mushrikeen and kuffar told him about Rasul Allah....  He challenges Rasool Allah : یا محمد! ما عندک؟ What do you have? Show me what you got? And all Rasool Allah (SA) responds with one single ayah... not a long speech... just one ayah...
أِنَّ الحَمدَ لِی اللہَ أحمَدَہُ وَ أستَعِینَہُ وَ أُومِنُ بِہِ وَ أتَوَکَّلُ عَلَیہِ ۔ مَن یَّھدِہِ اللہُ فَلاَ مُضِلَّ لَہ وَ مَن یَّضِلَّ لَہُ فَلاَ ھَادِیَ لَہ
And Tufail Ibn Amar starts crying, and begs Rasul Allah to convert him to Islam. He doesn't go back home to think about it! He didnt say...  Wait wait wait! ...  You know what .. this is pretty heavy stuff! let me just take this back home , let me think about it... you know... speak with the wife, discuss with my tribe members... let me think about its pros and cons... Let me get back to you on this....  NO! He accepts Islam instantly!

If somebody gives me a million dollars, no strings attached... I'm not going to ponder over it... I'll be like Thank you ! See Ya! before he gets a chance to change his mind.... Right? Or will I be like... oh you know what let me think about it, discuss it with my wife... Hell No! I'll take the money and split! Because the guy just made me an offer I can't refuse.... 

So the million dollar quesiton is, what was in these ayats that were so miraculous to the Arabs? that brought these disbelievers down to their knees instantly. What was in these ayahs that made them an offer they simply could not refuse...? And that's the linguistic dimension of these miracles. And that's why we spent so much time building up to this point... understanding the mindset of the ancient Arab, so that we can really understand what they saw, and heard when Rasul Allah recited these ayaats to them...
 

So this dimension, the linguistic dimension of Quran, lies in several things... I'll only talk about 2 of them today InshaAllah Ta'ala
1. The first is the precision in the order of words
2. The second, is the precision in the choice of words

First is the order of words, second is the choice of words. Because Arabic is a very rich language... Can someone tell me What do we mean, when we say a certain language is very rich? or poor! What does that mean?


5/6/15, 11:08:46 AM: Nabila Lateef: Arabic Dictionary has many names for Lion as follows - Lion when sitting in his Den with folded legs is called Asad, and the same Asad, when it comes out of his den & walks around he is called Zargham, and when Zargham while walking around focusses on any place he is called Ghazanfar, and when Ghazanfar while roaring walks away from his den he is called Zaigham, and when Zaigham pounces on his prey he is called Hamza, and when Hamza catches the prey in his control in such way that the prey is unable to breathe properly he is called Abbas, and when Abbas disintegrates his prey into pieces he is called Haider...
5/6/15, 11:14:24 AM: Salma Shafique: Very interesting ..thank you Nabila




In Arabic, for any given word you have dozens of synonyms. Dozens of synonyms. Each with a slight differnce, like a gradient. For example if you're planning to paint your room and you go to Home Depot to get a color swatch. For the color red, you have so many gradients, each with a slight difference in shade, and with its on unique name right? Each word in arabic is the same way.
For exmaple there are 8 words for falling asleep.
نوم، سُبات، سکون، رقاد، کری، ھجوع، ضجع
And the differences between them is, one is deep sleep, one is almost asleep, one is for when your eyes are open but your mind is sleeping, one is for you're sleeping but you can still hear people, the other is sleep when you're not lying down flat, one is for sleep with a dream in it, one is for sleep without a dream in it.

Notice I needed to use a phrase to describe some of these words, but Arabic has just one word for each of them. And this is what I mean that ...when the Arabs battled in competition, they would notice the placement of the wrong word in the context, and then they would use that against the opponent. Even we do this to some extent. When you listen to a friend speak, if you see the wrong word used in a sentence, you'll correct him. But they would do this at the nth degree. Because in Arabic there is a word for every emotion, and every intensity of that emotion

They have so many words just to describe a farmer. A word for when he's planting the seed. a word when has planted the seed, A word when he's taking out the seed, when he's watering the plant. And all those words are used, just to say farmer.
مزارع، کافر، فلاح، ملتزم
All these words mean farmer... Yes a farmer is called "Kaafir" in Arabic because he hides the seed in the ground.. A seed was apparent, visible to the eye, but the farmer hides it in the ground.... just like a disbeliever hides the truth even though it's apparent... So the word for disbeliever (Kaafir) is actually the word used for "farmer."

So we'll see that the miracle of islam is in the choice of words used to describe things which are just uttered in passing. These words are recited casually, like a flow, without actually planning the placement of each word. But the surgical precision it contains cannot be duplicated, even when you attempt to write them, let alone in speech. Even ordinary sentences are delivered using words, that turn it into a miracle. It's just the way in which he talks about it, in itself becomes uniquely miraculous.

And another thing that's so incredible... is how consistent the quran is in the choice... and order of these words. It's not like this order exists in SOME ayats... The more you study this book, the more you will consistently find this precision...

So let's set the stage for this discussion, before we discuss the miracle....

Has anyone ever given a speech before? A speech that you had to give in school or in your profession, where you needed to speak before an audience and convey an important point across....

Tell me very quickly about this speech ... what was it about?
Tell me about the preparation you had to go through to put that speech together...
Did you write it down?
Did you go back and edit your original text? Why did you edit it? Eloquence, better choice of words?
Did you read it again and again, and everytime you read it, did you make changes to it.
When did you feel satisified? When you read it and couldn't think of changes to make.
If today you look at the material again, do you think you would still make changes to it?

Now you said you wrote this speech down and edited it multiple times, right? If you had to give this speech in promptu, without preparation, without editing , would it be the exact same speech that you delivered after preparation? Would it have the same influence? Of course not! Why is that?

It's because when you and I speak, we organize our thoughts. When I came to this session, I organized my thoughts before hand. I said first I'm going to talk about this, then I'm going to talk about this and then I'm going to talk about that. I even have these points written before me. But you and I are incapable of organizing our words based on what we're going to say later.

We can't organize our words based on what we're going to say later. We can organize our thoughts, but not our words when we're speaking extemporaneously. We can do that in writing... If I'm preparing for a speech on paper, I can put down my thoughts first, and then organize my words. I can have a whole editorial process in place. I can keep going back to it and keep rephrasing my sentences. But I cannot do this when I'm spekaing. Do you understand? Do you agree?

Now is the Quran a written tradition, or an oral tradition? Oral Tradition. Now we know that when the wahi would come on Rasool Allah, he would immediately start reciting these Ayaats, in a flow.... one ayah after another until the entire passage was delivered. It's not like he would wait for the entire wahi to come upon him, then ask for a piece of paper... put his thoughts down, then organize his words, go over the material over and over again to make sure it's eloquent and the right words have been used etc. Examining history, we see there was absolutely no editorial process. As the noor of wahi was revealed to him, he converted it into words. Right? And there was not even a scheduled time for the Wahi... It would just get revealed anytime of the day... He had no way to prepare in advance for this revelation...

Have you ever heard that he said an ayah and then changed it... Did he ever say...  You know what? that didn't sound right let me change that... or you know what, that's not exactly what I meant to say, scratch that. Oh I could have said that one better, let me try again... No! We see none of that. Not a single instance where he ever said that. 23 years of Quran revelation, no editorial process, never revoked an ayah unless Allah Himself revoked it by another ayah. 23 years! We cannot do this for 23 days!

If I had these sessions written before me, I would edit and ammend and rearrange the hell out of the words that I use. Because I want them to have an impact! I want them to influence my audience... But notice at the end of the session when we have our QA, how many times have I said, "That's not what I meant"... Well if you that's not what I meant, why did i say it? Because I didn't have an editorial process in place. And speaking spontaneously, the best I can do is organize my thoughts, but not my words. You all understand?

Any questions?

6:151

وَلَا تَقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَوْلَدَكُم مِّنْ إِمْلَقٍۢ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ

17:31
وَلَا تَقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَوْلَدَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَقٍۢ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۚ

In the old days (دور جاھلیۃ), and actually even today, parents used to kill their unborn children because of poverty.... They didnt have enough food to feed themselves, and therefore could not afford to feed an additional mouth, so they would kill their children because of poverty. In both these ayaats, Allah talks to such parents, and commands them not to kill their children because of poverty, with a subtle difference...

In the first ayat He say: Do not kill your children because of poverty, we provide you all (parents) and them (children)
In the second ayat He say: Do not kill your children out of fear of poverty, we provide them (children) and you all (parents)

In the first ayah, who does Allah talk about providing first? Parents! And then who? children
In the second ayah, who does Allah talk about providing first? Children! And then about who? parents

Now what difference does it make right? parents first, children later... or children first, parents later... same thing right?

Now let's see why that is...

When Allah says do not kill your children because of poverty, does poverty already exist? Yes... it does... They don't have enough food to feed themselves, so they're killing the children....  so Allah addresses the parents first... That don't kill your children because of your current state... I will provide you first, and then I will also provide for your children.

In the second ayah, Allah talks about the fear of poverty! Does fear exist in the present or in the future? In the future! This means that the parents have their provision for the now, what they're worried about is the what? future! And who's going to join the family in the future? Children! So when he talks about fear of poverty, future provisions, related to future children, he talks about children first and then the parents. He says don't be fearful or worry about the children that are coming in the future, we will provide them and not just them, but you as well... SubhanAllah!!!

Do you see how the words that are about to be said in the future, have an influence on how words are organized in the beginning, even before they are delivered. We as humans do not have that capability when we speak. Because we can only organize our thoughts, not our words...
To us I worked really hard,  and I really worked hard, are the same thing. There is no difference. Even when we write, we are not that sensitive! But Quran was not revealed as a written word was it? It was revealed as a spoken word! These ayats had no editorial process... yet we find this amazing perfection in the way words are organized in relation to what's coming ahead in the sentence. And this kind perfection, this kind of precision, exists consistently all across the Quran.

And the Arabs would notice this as soon as they heard it, they would detect this precision, because after all they themselves were masters of this language. They relaized, that as human beings they were simply not capable of this kind of perfection, when it came to extermporaneous conversations. So they new this just could not be the word of a man.

Let's look at another one...

17:96
قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًۢا بَصِيرًۭا
29:52
كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ شَهِيدًۭا ۖ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ بِٱلْبَطِلِ وَكَفَرُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَسِرُونَ

The word  شَهِيدًۭا! means "witness"; And it is also one of the 99 attributes of Allah... so keep that in mind.
Now let's first go over the highlighted part...
The first ayah says... Allah is enough as a witness between me and you.... Who is saying this ayah? Rasul Allah! So the "ME" refers to who? RasulAllah and "YOU" refers to us or whoever is listening it. Correct?
The second ayah says... Allah is enough between me and you as a witness. Again "ME" refers to Rasul Allah and "YOU" refers to us... correct?

So the placement of which word is different in the two verses... just the higlighted part... The word Shaheedan! If you take out this word, both ayahs would read the same, correct? So in the first ayah, Rasul Allah uses the word Shaheedan first, and then say "Me and You", but in the second ayah he says "Me and You" first and then uses the word "Shaheedan".  Is that clear to everyone?

Now what difference does it make? They both mean the same thing right?

The thing is Quran is hyper-sensitive, super-sensitive to context... When Rasul Allah spoke, he organized the words like a beautiful architecture. You cannot move a single brick from its place, or its perfection, its perfection will be lost forever...

Now I want you to examine the latter part of these ayats. The text after the highlighted text.
