You'll see that in the first ayah Allah ends the ayah talking about himself... He says إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًۢا بَصِيرًۭا . Innahoo refers to Allah himself, he says Allah has complete knowledge of his slaves... Who has complete knowledge of his slaves? Allah!
You'll see in the latter part of the second ayah, He talks about the people أُو۟لَٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَسِرُونَ. They are the ones who are losers... who are the losers? the people.


So the first ayah ends talking about Allah, and the second ayah ends talking about people..... So the ayah that ends talking about Allah himself, Rasul Allah uses the attribute "Shaheedan" first, and the refers to people. But in the second ayah that ends talking about people, Rasul ALlah refers to the people first and then uses Allah's attribute Shaheedan! Subhan Allah!!!

Again words that are about to be said in the future, have an influence on how words are organized in the beginning of the sentence. This kind of perfection is just not possible with spontaneous conversations. And Quran is no doubt a spontaneous conversation that Allah has through his messenger (AS) - AllahuAkbar!



Now let's look at the perfection in the CHOICE of words in a different ayah. We all know the difference between past tense and present tense. The Quran uses all three tenses. It uses the past tense, the present tense and of course the future tense. When I say "KILLED", which tense is that? When I say "KILLS" what tense is that? In Surah Nisa, Allah says

4:92
وَمَن قَتَلَ مُؤْمِنًا خَطَـًۭٔا فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍۢ مُّؤْمِنَةٍۢ وَدِيَةٌۭ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِ
And whoever killed a believer by mistake, then he should free another muslim as recompense and pay the blood money to the relatives of the deceased

Is "Killed" past tense or present tense? It is past tense. So past tense is used with mistake

4:93
وَمَن يَقْتُلْ مُؤْمِنًۭا مُّتَعَمِّدًۭا فَجَزَآؤُهُۥ جَهَنَّمُ خَلِدًۭا فِيهَا

And whoever kills a believer on purpose, his recompense is hell fire. He will live in it forever
Is "Kills" past tense or present tense? It's present  tense. And we see here that past tense is associated with intent, or deliberation.

Past tense is associated with mistake and Present tense is associated with intent.

Now note that this is in the same passage. You would think that Allah would stick to the same tense. After all what difference does it make?
وَمَن قَتَلَ مُؤْمِنًا خَطَـًۭٔا
وَمَن قَتَلَ مُؤْمِنًا مُّتَعَمِّدًۭا

And whoever killed by mistake; and whoever killed on purpose... same thing.. But No! When he mentioned killing by mistake he uses the past tense, and when he mentions killing on purpose, he uses the present tense. You know why? Because the past tense only happened once.

I WENT to the Muslim Conference in NY. What does that mean? One time!
I GO to the Microsoft Conference in NY. What does that mean? I go every year

So here in the first ayah, Allah Ta'la talks about a man who killed by mistake and used the past tense. How many times do you kill by mistake? Just once! you only commit murder once. The second time you say "Oh Mistake again" then....
so the one who kills on purpose is a perpetrator, and he should be punished, because chances are what? he'll do it again, hence the present tense. Hence the continuity. Past tense with murder because it is accidental, it does not have continuity. Present tense with murder, because it is on purpose, it has continuity. SubhanAllah!!

Even in the tense. Not just the choice of words, but even in the tense of words there is such hyper sensitivity, that does not exist in any literary form. And you see these things are completely lost when we do not become the students of what language? The Quranic Arabic and the Ancient Arabic. Quranic Arabic is the perfected form, the pinnacle of Ancient Arabic...



 



 
Ask Yasmeen to explain what a miracle is to everyone

Another powerful dimension in miracles of Quran is the dimension of "imagination"... in which the Quran challenges the powerful imaginations of the Ancient Arabs.... If you remember, we studied this in quite details in one of our early sessions. I showed you how the ancient Arabs had a very powerful imagination. So much more powerful than the imaginations we have today... Do you remember the reason for that? Why were their imaginations so powerful? Because they had nothing, so that had to imagine....

We don't have an imagination, because everything we could possible imagine, has been shown to us in Sci Fi movies. So our imagination, at best comes from something that we've either seen in a movie, or read in a book. Right?  Media has visually taken our imaginations, to great heights... but the downside is, that we have lost the power of imaginative creativity...

But to the Arabs, they were surrounded by what? Desert! They didn't have any natural resources... no waterfalls, no rainbows, no sky scrapers, no disney land, no xbox... nothing... All they had was sand and mountains...... Can you imagine two Arab friends hanging out in the desert in those days, would their conversation would be like? ...... Dude! Have you been to that spot in the desert? The sand looks so amazing there... The dust tastes sweet... And he's like Nah man! the sand in that place looks so much more awesome! :))) Because that's all they had.... sand and dust everywhere....  Absolutely nothing else... So when you don't have anything to see, you start building things up in your imagination... And because they had the gift of language... because they had the immense power of articulation, through prose and poetry, .... they would actually paint the images in their minds using what? words! Prose and Poetry!

Do you remember the story I told you of that poor man who has nothing to eat in the house, and he's reciting poetry to his wife about how generous he is... What was the image he uses to express his immense generosity? Do you remember that image? Something to do with house and rain?
السَّیلُ حَربٌ عَلی مَکانِ العَالِی
Heavy rain does not get along with a house on top of a hill!

Now to truly understand this dimension of miracles, one thing you need to understand and keep in mind about Arabic eloquence, is this saying that the Ancient Arabs had...
الخیر الکلام من قل و دل
the best speech is that.... which is short  and precise.... short and to the point.... If someone would use many words to explain something, that was considered a weakness...

So this extremely poor poet...  talking about his generousity says...
السَّیلُ حَربٌ عَلی مَکانِ العَالِی
Heavy rain does not get along with a house on top of a hill!

Now what does a house on top of a hill, have any thing to do with heavy rain? And what in the world does that have to do with generosity?

For those of you were not in that session...
In your head imagine a house on top of a hill. And what's going on? Heavy rain! When it rains, does the water stay on top of the hill, or does it collect in a puddle at the bottom of the hill? Of course at the bottom
So he says, RIZQ, or provision comes from the sky. And there are two kinds of people in the world. The lofty ones and the lowly ones. People that are closer to God and those that are worldly. If your house is on top of the hill, aren't you close to the sky? Close to God? So the water doesn't stay with me, meaning the provision doesn't stay with me, I get it first, but I don't keep it to myself.... I keep giving it to the lowly people. So the people that are rich are actually poor, and I'm the lofty one giving them my provisions! And he says all of this, by just saying what?
السَّیلُ حَربٌ عَلی مَکانِ العَالِی
5 words. You see how short an precise his words are... Heavy rain does not get along with a house on top of a hill! It is concise, it is short, it's a riddle that challenges imagination.. It's all of these things in 5 words.... 
So these Arabs actually were big on precise and to the point conversations... They were not like some people we know who will talk and talk and talk to explain the most simplest of things...... Oh last night... I used this amazing invention that lets me rise to the top of a building using one step at a time, I felt like I was walking towards the sky one step at a time.... Dude! You just took the stairs...! Move on!

So the arabs spoke in these riddles. They wanted you to imagine what is being said. They wouldn't just say it. And if you didn't get it, they would make fun of you... They would bully you... hah! Ajam (mute)!!! So this was the kind of sensitivity to language and power of imagination they enjoyed.

In those days there were these story tellers... who would narrate these amazing fictition stories.... people would find a nice scenic view, where they could enjoy the view of the sand (so pretty much anywhere in the desert) and they would gather around this guy ... who would then start telling them a story...  stop at a crucial point and then he would say... we'll continue this next week ..... Please tune in to another expisode of Jahiliya poetry.... next week same time! :)

And the people would not only come again, they would bring more people with them... because these story tellers had such amazing imaginations. Adn as you know now,  every Arab had a powerful imagination.... So they weren't easily impressed by anyone....  But when there came someone who could challenge their imagination... OMG, he would be treated like a god among mortals!

And then 40 years later, someone as ordinary, someone as unknown, someone who's pretty much a nobody among the people of Mecca, someone known to not have any poetic skills, someone known for 40 years to have no articulation, comes up with these words.... that is prose, not poetry.... but sounds like poetry....  Words that challenges them with an imagination that was so beyond their imaginations...... so powerful in its images, in its analogies, in its comparisons, in its language, in its precision and perfection in the choice of its words, in the placement of its words.... that they were completely incapacitated.... These words of Rasul Allah (SA) brought the Arabs to their knees.

So I have 2 example for you.... We'll see if we can do both today, InshaAllah

Allah says in Surah AlHajj

حُنَفَآءَ لِلَّهِ غَيْرَ مُشْرِكِينَ بِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ فَكَأَنَّمَا خَرَّ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَتَخْطَفُهُ ٱلطَّيْرُ أَوْ تَهْوِى بِهِ ٱلرِّيحُ فِى مَكَانٍۢ سَحِيقٍۢ

OK....
حُنَفَآءَ لِلَّهِ غَيْرَ مُشْرِكِينَ بِهِ
There are those who are solely dedicated to Allah, without committing any shirk with him whatsoever... Shirk in arabic means to ascribe partners to somone. So in this context, partner to Allah.....
وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ
And whoever is to commit shirk with Allah
فَكَأَنَّمَا خَرَّ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ
Then it is as though he fell from the sky. Now I want you to remember these images that Allah is using as his example..
فَكَأَنَّمَا خَرَّ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ
Then it is as though he fell from the sky.

فَتَخْطَفُهُ ٱلطَّيْرُ
Birds are snatching off of him... Birds are having lunch on this guy , they are eating him...

أَوْ تَهْوِى بِهِ ٱلرِّيحُ فِى مَكَانٍۢ سَحِيقٍۢ
A wind is then blowing him away to a far off unchartered place


Three parts of this example... First part ... a man is falling from the sky... Second part ... birds are eating him... third part... wind is blowing him away to a far off place.

So Allah gives this example of a mushrik, someone who commits shirk using this example. Now you see the whole purpose of an example is to make the subject easy... to make it simple, make it approachable... make it easy to understand. But the purpose of an example.... most of all... is to present something that the audience can relate to... Now if the example is harder to understand than the subject, then it sort of defeats the whole point, right?

And here Allah is saying that the person committing shirk is like the one falling from a sky, then birds have lunch on him, and then a wind blows him away.... what has that go to do with shirk? This is not an example we can relate to.... There were no planes in those days... how would you fall off from the sky? But then, not just that.... birds are feeding off of him... And then the winds are blowing him away to an unchartered place... The thing is.... the guy is dead!! right? Who cares if he's blown to an uncharted place or a familiar place... what difference does it make? The guy is still dead!

The reason why we have difficulty understanding this example, is because we are thinking of it in terms of proper arabic, not Ancient arabic. One thing I didn't tell you about Ancient Arabic, is that it has its own expressions... idioms...
And idiom in English is That's cool!!! If you said this to an Ancient Arab, he'd be like Really? Do you know where you are? Have you been in th sun too long? This is the freaking desert! What are you talking about? There is no such thing as cool here! This is Saudia! It's not cool!! its scorching! So he wouldn't think of the words "That's cool" the same way as you kids do! And you kids are funny... 2 kids return after watching the same basketball match... one says...  That was SO cool! The other says...  That was SO hot! and they both mean the same thing! :)))) right? So, expressions change!

The Quran in various places, uses expressions of the Ancient Arabs. Did these expressions change over time? Yes of course! You have to understand..... that if you take these expression literally, you've lost the meaning. It's not literal... If someone tells you ... no use crying over spilt milk.. you don't say....  I didn't spill milk!... I lost my job man!! If you do, you've missed the point... because that's an expression...

So Allah compares a mushrik to a man falling from the sky... these are most of the Arabs at that time who were idol worshippers. They didn't belive in an after life. They believed that the only thing they have to look forward to is death.. There's nothing after that... game over. So if the only thing before you is death, the most important thing then becomes what? Legacy! What you leave behind! How people remember you!! That's all they had to cling to. They didn't believe in the resurrection, the sun and moon colliding, They'd say... aaaah
إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا ٱلدُّنْيَا نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيَا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ
There is nothing except this worldly life... we live we die... there is nothing more.. I don't understand this aakhirah nonsense!

Allah speaks to this adamant mushrik and tries to explain to him how terrible a mushrik is in the eyes of Allah, using an expression that THEY understood. Allah is trying to relate to this particular audience.... If you studied anything about the Arab people, you would know that they were warriors. They were really big on their tribal warfare pride. Now we have sports these days and we take pride in teams... but their pride was their battles.. The worst thing you could do, the worst legacy you could leave behind, was the the death of a coward. The death of a loser. Because after all once you die all that's left of you is your legacy, your reputation.

So the Arabs used this expression "Falling from the sky" even before the Quran was revealed. They used it all the time. What they meant by it was... imagine there are 2 tribes fighting... one is at the top of a cliff, the other is at the bottom of the cliff... Which one has the advantage? The one on top! Despite being on top of the cliff.. if they still lost the battle, once it's over... what would the enemies do to their bodies? Chuck em of the cliff. They used to call this "Falling from the sky". There were 2 scenarios that this expression was used in... either they were really lousy fighters.... that they lost the battle despite having the advanatage... or they were running away from the enemies and in their retreat they trip and fall off the cliff....  and die the death of a coward. So either they die the death of a loser or a coward. These were the worst legacies that the Arabs ever wanted to leave behind. The ugliest kind of end they could imagine would be captured in the words... "Falling from the sky".

Allah speaks to these mushrik Arabs who don't have any concept of the hereafter... who don't have any concept of the humiliation on the day of judgment, he gives them an expression that can help them understand the day from their point of view....
Think of the most ugliest thing you can imagine? let me tell you shirk is like that! It's like falling from the sky.

When you die in battle, in ancient human trandiation...when two sides collide... corpses are left all over the place... once the battle is done, what happens to those corpses? Each side comes, picks up the corpse of their dead and give them a proper burrial. Isn't that what happens? Unless... you feel that your soldier was a traitor, or a humiliation or an embaressment to your tribe, in that case nobody comes for you.......... And when nobody comes for you... guess what comes for you? Birds! الطیر . which in classical Arabic, in classical poetry was used for vultures! Birds of carrion ... that eat dead meat. So now birds are eating away from the corpose of this dead guy..  the body that their own tribe members left behind. Now these disgusting birds finish their lunch... and now they too don't want anything more from it. Even they don't want anymore!

Now what comes? Now wind comes and blows away the remains, the scum of what's left of these people, into unchartered territory. They've become the decomposed trash of the earth.... The ugliest ugliest ending that the Arabs could imagined is displayed in such beautiful terms... with one single example .... just so they can get an idea of what shirk is in the eyes of Allah....
You think shirk is a small thing? you think idol worship is a small thing? Let me put things in your perspective.

Do you see how powerful the Quran is in its examples....

See! here's one of the arguments people make.... they say the Quran states that it is clear... I should be able to understand it myself. But the thing is Quran even though it does say it is clear, even though it does say it is self evident.....  it never says it is simple.... Clear and simple are two very different things.....We shouldn't confuse clear and self evident with simple.  Quran sometimes presents very clear solutions to very complex problems, but not necessarily in a simple way... Imagine there is a very hard calculus problem... And the professor writes a very clear solution to the problem. Now the solution may be clear, but does that mean it's simple? No! the professor still needs to explain its complexities no matter how clear the solution is...

So don't oversimplify the Quran... because it has a great deal of complexity... And this is why we need the true Imams (AS), their dua'at present in every time, to help us understand these complexities in simple terms. We cannot understand this book without the medium of an Imam or a Dai....  That's why the Quran says:
فسألو اھل الذکر ان کنتم لا تعلمون
Ask the people of rememberance (the Imams/ the dua'ats) if you don't understand something..

OK... Second example... 

Now every Surah presentrs such incredible imagery through dialogue... I gave you the example of Utbah Ibn Rabeeah, of how Allah comes to the defense of his beloved prophet (AS) against Utbah's accusations... Do you remember what happens to him at the end of that dialogue? He starts crying... springs ahead and places both hands on the mouth of the prophet (AS) and begs him to stop...

Allah uses images and analogies to challenge the Arab imagination in various surah... He beautifully portrays the unseen... e.g. Surah Al-Rehman, Surah AlWaqiah, right?  You may be aware of the images of jannat and jahannum that it portrays... The images of akhirat etc. .... So one such surah is Surah AlMursilaat that I want to discuss with you today.... There are a handful of Surahs in which a lot of repititions is used.... very few of them.... In Surah Al-Rehman, we all know the verse فبای آلاء ربکما تکذبان is repeated several times... 31 times in fact. Then there is Surah الشعراء , that has a repitition of ???. Similary Surah Mursilaat is of of those surah in which the verseوَيْلٌۭ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ‎is repeated several times. Which mean, There will be immense sorrow for the one's who lied on that day... What day is Allah talking about? The day of Judgment!

The purpose of these repetitions and why Allah repeats himself so many times in these surah, is a discussion for another day InshAallah, because it has a very profound purpose... Today what I want to focus on, is presenting to you the imagery of the Quran as a miracle.

This is from Surah Al-Mursilaat... What I want to show you, are the analogies and the parallels it gives, to explain abstract concepts like aakhirat, his power, and his dominion over things...
 

You see the Arabs of Quraish had arguied with Rasul Allah that you keep talking about the sun and the moon colliding. You keep talking about the mountains moving swiftly as if they were made of wool.. People out of fear are going to look drunk... The oceans are going to boil over... these big big things you keep talking about... That's not going to happen!! When is all this going to come? We've never seen anything like that... none of that makes sense... none of it is possible. So they argued that the threats in the Quran are too cataclismic... They are too enormous to be possible. They said
1. There's no purpose to creation!
2. We are not dependent on Allah for anything
3. There is no hereafter or rising from the dead
4. There is no reward or punishment..

All this is just nonsense... crazy talk of a mad man (نعوذ باللہ).

So in response to that Allah (AZ) takes some oaths in these few ayats of Surah Murlisaat... As I told you last time, Arabic is an extremely rich language. For each word, you have dozens of synonyms. One word can have 10 different meanings, and the most amazing thing about it is that all 10 different meanings can apply in the same context.... Usually when a word has many synonyms, different words are used in different contexts... But Arabis is unique that not only can each word be used in a separate context, all it's forms can be used in the same context as well. InshaAllah I'll show you many examples of this as we progress...

SO the word المرسلۃ is literally used in Arabic for that *which has been released!* So in Surah AlMursliaat, Allah is talking about the winds. The word is also used to describe a very fast horse... who moves like the wind... A very swift horse... It is also meant to describe a messenger, one who brings a warning... الرسول.....  So Allah (AWJ) swears by the winds, and he says...
وَٱلْمُرْسَلَتِ عُرْفًۭا ‎

The word عُرف in Arabic refers to the hair on the back of the neck of a wild horse.If you don't have reins or ropes on a horse, what is the only thing you can use to slow it down? The hair on the back of its neck. Allah gives the image of winds as though they are like the like wild horses that have been unleashed because its hair has been let go... What happens when you let go the hair of a wild horse? It goes crazy... It goes out of control. It marches forward full speed ahead... So what's being paralleled here is the image of a wild horse with winds.... Meaning Allah is telling Quraish that the winds are not something that randomly blow this way or that way... The soft breeze that you feel on your face, is because it's reigns are being held.... Held by who? Allah (AWJ). But it is very possible for that supreme lord to let go of that lock of hair... If he let's go, what's going to happen? They're going to turn into this wild horse, this stallion which is going to crush through anything in its path. What happens when a hurricane decides to takes a stroll through a city? It destroys anything in its path, right? So the first thing Allah is swearing by is the winds that have the potential to destroy when its reigns are let go...

Then he says...
‏فَٱلْعَصِفَتِ عَصْفًۭا ‎
The word عصف in arabic is to move in an inconsiderate fashion. What I mean by inconsiderate fashion is to not care what's in its path... If the brakes of a truck or a buldozer fail, they will just crush anything that comes in its path.. they will not care .. they will just keep moving forward....  So the movement of a bulldozer is عصف. it's inconsiderate, it doesn't care what comes in its way... It will just keep moving forward... From عصف that comes the word "عاصف". What's my name?  :))) Thank you baba, I love you too! So Allah continues his oath.. ‏فَٱلْعَصِفَتِ عَصْفًۭا ‎
These winds that have just been unleashed have now become inconsiderate.... ruthless. They don't care what's going to come in their way... They will just destroy as they move along.

So in the first 2 ayats, Allah swears by the destruction of winds... If you've seen videos of what happened after hurricane Katrina, what it did in just 40 seconds, the destruction it caused, you have an idea of the power of these winds.... In another place Allah talks about such winds upon a nation of rebels, for 7 consecutive days.... X2.... can you imagine the destruction it must have caused? If 40 seconds can do so much, what will 7 consecutive days do??
سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيَالٍۢ وَثَمَنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومًۭا فَتَرَى ٱلْقَوْمَ فِيهَا صَرْعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجَازُ نَخْلٍ خَاوِيَةٍۢ

We sent them winds for 7 nights and 8 days continuously (حُسُومًۭا ) , continuously pounding them.... we saw what 40 seconds can do... 7 nights and 8 days! Turned their skulls like seedless dates. AllahuAkbar! such powerful words!!!

Now the winds is not some cataclysmic event such as the sun and moon coming together, the sun increasing it's gravity pull... Its not a special event that needs to be prepared.... You know how special arragments are made for weapons of mass destruction... What does Allah using to destroy these people? Just the wind! The Quraish who knew about this incident, Allah is telling them, that to destroy you, I don't need to make any special arrangements for you guys... All I need to do is release my hold on the lock of the winds... That's all I need to do.

And then he turns around and gives you the other aspect of winds... After describing the winds as the source of death and destruction, he says...
‏وَٱلنَّشِرَتِ نَشْرًۭا ‎

And the winds that serve to spread.... نشر literally means to take a bunch of seeds and just fling it into the air... What happens when you do that? They fall everywhere in random directions. So Allah swears by the winds who spread and disperse the clouds all over the world. Because what do clouds bring us? Rain! And rain is the source of life on this earth. no rain, no life... drought means death! So the wind that was the source of death and destruction in the first 2 ayaats, the same wind is now a source of life in this third ayat. SubhanAllah! And now we know even better. Winds just dont' carry clouds around, what else do they carry? the pollen! The source of all vegetation... Pollen travels from one place to another through what? winds! Those of you who have bad allergies know that too well unfortunately.  These winds are actually a mercy of Allah... That's why we are reminded to say AlHamdulillah! Because why does one sneeze? Because of a foreign particle irritating the inside of the nose. How did the particle get there, through breadth... what does one take in through breadthing... air, where did the air come from? Winds! SubhanAllah!

So Allah spreads these clouds around. Now when you think of Allah spreading these clouds through winds, you may think that it's all random. That the winds are in control....  you know what science calls the random acts of mother nature.... We know as Muslims there is no such things... These are precise, very calculated acts of who? Allah (AWJ), so he says...

‏فَٱلْفَرِقَتِ فَرْقًۭا ‎
He swears by those who make very precise decisions...  Water will fall here and not there... This land will remain dry, this land will get water. ‏فَٱلْفَرِقَتِ فَرْقًۭا ‎
Two things the Quraish have been told so far using imagery with words... These  winds can bring death, and these same winds can bring life.... I control which brings which Allah say! He swears by them, and says I'm the one in control. Then he says...

‏فَٱلْمُلْقِيَتِ ذِكْرًا ‎
These winds bring people in contact with reminders! mulqiyaat comes from the root word لقی   which means to come in contact with someone.... So Allah says these winds bring people in contact with what? Reminders through reflection. What are these reminders Allah is talking about? What were the arguments of the Quraish?

They said
1. There's no purpose to creation!
2. We are not dependent on Allah for anything
3. There is no hereafter or rising from the dead
4. There is no reward or punishment..


Winds create the cooperation between the heaves and the earths... The clouds are moving in the heavens, and the rain is falling on the earth. All of it to serve who? Us! the human beings... So the single entity that is responsible for this cordination is what? Wind... Allah is showing us that there is a single chain of command,  a single leadership... it shows the purpose of creation. Think about it... If all of this, this entire creation around us.... the winds, the clouds, the rain, the vegetation it grows, the animals that eat the vegetation... the animals that we feed on.... all of this has been created to serve us... Mankind! Allah wants us to think, that I created everything with a particular purpose to serve who? You and Me! So you think you were created without a purpose? See how everything comes in cooperation with each other, all with the purpose to serve you... If Allah created all this for the purpose to serve me, then logically, I could not been created without a purpose... I must have a purpose to serve as well.... because I too am a creation. SubhanAllah! All this realization with just a wind.... So the wind is actually a reminder for my purpose in life!

The second remind that the winds carry.... Let me give you an example. If I have a pet... and I leave my pet without water all day... After all I am "Asif" :)) In the night when I returns home, at whose mercy is the animal? At my mercy! I need to put some water in a dish or bowl and give it to the animal. But the animal is not capable of helping itself at all. it is completely at my mercy. Regardless of how this animal behaves... whether is damages my property, messes my stuff, breaks things, whether it obeys me or not, in the end... at the end of the day....  it is  completely at my mercy. Isnt' it? As wild and rebellious as human beings can get, if Allah does not give us these sips of water to drink... where do we go? We have no place to go... We are more pathetic and more powerless than the pets that we think we own. Who brings the water, the clouds.. who disperses the clouds... winds. SubhanAllah! The winds are a reminder of the lordship of Allah! That no matter who you think you are... a poor farmer or the king of nations, you are completely dependent on Allah.. he is letting you have these sips... This water is not your product. You can walk as arrogantly as you want on the earth, but if I don't send this mercy from the sky... where will you be? Do you understand now, why we sit and drink water? Why we pay our respect to water when we drink it... ?

Then the winds are a reminder of resurrection... winds bring the clouds.... water falls from the sky, and what happens to the earth? It comes back to life! DEAD becomes LIVING! Now everytime rains bring life to the dead earth, we are reminded that we will also die, and we will also be placed in the earth, and just like rain brings the dead earth to life through vegetation, we too one day will be resurrected. It will be giving out human beings on the day of judgment.

And then finally we see that in the first two ayats Allah talks about death and destruction, and the next two ayats talks about life and prosperirty using the same example. Wind.... He reminds us that Allah has in place, a profound system of punishment and reward.... Something that begetts his wrath, and other things that begett his mercy. So we are reminded to not cross certain lines and pursue other things in life. We are reminded by Allah's justice... we are reminded of Allah's commandments.... just by the winds.

And now after giving the Arabs the example of winds, and asking them to reflect on it.... and the Arabs would process them. Everything I just explained to you, the Arabs just extracted all of it from these verses immediately...

And then Allah says...
‏عُذْرًا أَوْ نُذْرًا ‎

Allahs says there are two kinds of people who will feel the wind on their face. Now after being warned by the previous 5 ayats... first will be the one who will believe, who will accept Allah's majesty, submit to his glory.... And the other will be the one who will do nothing. Even after being warned.... So for the people who realized and accepted the wind, the winds served them a warner (نذرا).  And for the people who didn't care.... now these same winds are going to become a means of punishment for them (عذرا). The winds are now going to become a cause, a means, by which they will be punished. ‏عُذْرًا أَوْ نُذْرًا ‎

Then after setting this stage... After all this explanation, after all these promises.... Allah finally comes to his point...
‏إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَوَقِعٌۭ

What you have been promised, is no doubt going to become certaintly. What you have been promised will happen. Just like these winds that can be a torment, or it can be a favor, you will one day become the recepient of either Allah's extremely wrath, or Allah's extremely favour. It is bound to happen... it is going to happen!

Oaths in the Quran are a proufound, literary master piece in and of themselves.Each one of them. Surahs that start with oaths... require special specail study... just the study of why Allah takes an oath of these things... It is incredible. And I'm right now only speaking of the external surface, the ظاہر. I'm not even talking about باطن, which is SO SO SO much more powerful.

 











































 


In the last session, I showed you how beautiful and precise the words of the Quran are.... The arabs measured the eloquence and power of a conversation, by how concise and precise it was.... That's why they had the saying...
خیر الکلام من قل و دل
The best of conversations is that which is concise and precise

In Surah Mursalaat, we saw how Allah challenged the Arab imagination, and addressed the disbelief of the Arabs using winds as an example. You saw how concise and precise those ayats were, and the sea of knowledge those very few words contained... Allah explained the entire concept of akhiraah, punishment and reward just through winds... all in 5-6 simple small ayats...

Today, I want to continue down the same path and show you some more of these amazing miracles... This one is has to do with the perfection in the choice of words in the literal sense... In Surah Ehzaab, Allah talks about marrital issues. He starts by talking about men, and then He moves on to women...

33:4
مَّا جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لِرَجُلٍۢ مِّن قَلْبَيْنِ فِى جَوْفِهِ
Allah did not put 2 hearts inside of any man.
After talking about men, he moves on to talk about women: وَمَا جَعَلَ أَزْوَجَكُم ألتِّی تُظَهِرُونَ مِنْهُنَّ أُمَّهَتِكُمْ
Let me first go over the meaning of this ayah..  The word الِرَجُلٍۢ means "Man", its gender specific... The word قَلْبَيْنِ means "2 hearts".. Its the plural form of the word "قَلْب " which means a single heart. So قَلْب is singular and means one heart, and قَلْبَيْنِ  is a double plural and means 2 hearts....  In Arabic you don't just have singular and plurals, you also have something called double plurals... So for any word you have singular, double plural, meaning exactly 2,  and then the general plural meaning more than 2.. The word in جَوْفِin arabic means a hollow space. That's why a cave in Arabic is called جَوْفِ. When you read the English translations of this ayah, you'll find most translations say...  Allah did not put two hearts inside any man's chest. And that's the wrong translation of the word جَوْفِ. The arabic word for chest is صدر.  And this is why it is so important to learn the Quranic Arabic, because the filter of translation takes away so much from the meaning. English is simply not powerful enough as a language, to accomodate the richness of Arabic...


Anyway, so Allah says... We did not put 2 hearts inside of any man.... Now ofcourse this ayah is not literal, its metaphorical... What it means is that faith and disbelief cannot coexist in any man... A man will believe or disbelive... he will love or he will hate... Cannot do both at the same time... But what's really interesting about this ayah, is it's literal form....  Allah did not say that He did not put 2 hearts inside any human being. Don't men and women both have a single heart?  So then why did Allah single out men in this ayah? Cause he specifically says لِرَجُلٍۢ which means man.
Allah could have simply said...
مَّا جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لی النَّاسِ مِّن قَلْبَيْنِ فِى جَوْفِهِ
Allah did not put 2 hearts inside any human being. That would have covered both. But Allah excluded women from it... And this is perfection in it's LITERAL form, not LITERARY form, but LITERAL form. Because  you see, a woman can get pregnant! And when a woman gets pregnant, how many hearts are inside? It could be two or more... So Allah says he did not put 2 hearts inside any man! Even in it's literal form Quran is SO incredibly perfect.... nobody can play devils advocate and says Well what about women? They can get pregnant! .... No you can't. Allah said just man لِرَجُلٍۢ ! ...  SubhanAllah! Show me this kind of perfection in any other spoken conversation or even a book, anywhere in the world.


OK... this next miracle that I want to show you today, is also related to the profound nature of the imagery of the Quran. As we've seen previously... Quran is a very coherent, it is a very connected text. One part of the ayah describes the other.... one theme being recited in different words... words that are going to be said in the future having an influence on words that are coming in the beginning... right?

So in the same context, I want to present this ayah to you from Surha Saba... This miracle is in multiple dimensions. It's a miracle in the choice of words, it is also a miracle in the placement of words, and it is a miracle of Quran's imagery.... the way in which it challenges the imagination.... Until now I've shown you ayaats that were a miracle in their own specific dimension.... And now this one is among the many many ayaats that are a mircle in mutliple dimensions all at the same time! SubhanAllah!

OK.... so Allah says....

يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَاۚ وَهُوَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ

يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ
He knows what enters into the earth
وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا
and what comes out of it,
وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ
and what comes down from the sky,
وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَاۚ
and what rises to it....
وَهُوَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ
And he is the constantly merciful and exceedingly forgiving...

Now this is a very interesting ayah... If you notice the highlighted part, what do you see, the ayah ending in? وَهُوَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ . If you read the Quran regularly, you must have noticed that you don't normally see the words ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ. instead you always see the words ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ . That's the general theme in the Quran... Over 70 times he uses the words ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ in the Quran.

قالَ سَوْفَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكُمْ رَبِّىٓ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِى فَٱغْفِرْ لِى فَغَفَرَ لَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا۟ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفَاضَ ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
قُلْ إِن كُنتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ ٱللَّهَ فَٱتَّبِعُونِى يُحْبِبْكُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ

So everywhere else in the Quran, Allah says غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ . Which again, means forgiving comma merciful.... but this is the ONLY place in the entire Quran that Allah says... ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ. merciful comma forgiving...  So what up? Now this is divine speech... And our belief is that every word has to be in its proper place... So how come this is the only place in which Allah doesn't say forgiving comma merciful....  but instead says merciful comma forgiving...

First of all... I want to tell you something about these ayah.... There is a particular theme in the Quran when it comes to these ayaats... Everytime Allah uses the words غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ  at the end of the ayah, you will find the beginning of the ayah talking about human beings... one way of the other. That's important to note.... because, when it comes to human beings... they need to be forgiven first, and then they become deserving of Allah's mercy... That is the proper order as far as human beings are concerned...

And if you think about it... this applies to us also in our relationships with each other... There is a saying in Arabic
انَّ الدَّرءَ المَفْسَدَۃ مُقَدَّم عَلیٰ جَلبِ المَسْلَحَۃ
If you have a dirty glass, and you want to drink juice.... what do you have to do first? Clean the glass... so clean out the negative element... before you can add the positive element... Forgiveness cleans out the negative element and mercy is obviously the positive element...

If your friend has offended you and you are angry with him... you will need to forgive him first, before you can sincerely show him any favors.... So it's the same principle... In order to gain Allah's mercy, we first need to gain His forgiveness..

Now let's get back to our ayah...
يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَاۚ وَهُوَ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ٱلْغَفُورُ

He knows what enters into the earth and what comes out of it, and what comes down from the sky, and what rises to it.... And he is the constantly merciful and exceedingly forgiving...

I just told you that everytime in the Quran, the words غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭare used, Allah is talking about who? The human beings? In this ayah, the words are used again, in a different order, but do you see the mention of human beings in this ayah? Not directly!
The literal meaning of this ayah pertains to the "knowledge" of Allah... That Allah knows what is going in the earth, coming out of it, coming down from the sky, rising back to it... and He is the merciful comma forgiving...

So on the outer surface, this ayah is talking about Allah's knowledge... and another interesting fact for you is that wherever in the Quran, Allah talks about knowledge, you will find the mention of mercy in that passage.... Knowledge is affiliated with mercy everywhere in the Quran. Because for us to have Allah's knowledge... in the end is the greatest mercy of all! And the greatest proof of that concept from the Quran is the amazing ayah...
الرحمن علم القرآن
The intensely merciful taught the Quran....  which is knowledge and education.

Now in this ayah that we are studying, we can understand the presence of the word ٱلرَّحِيمُ because Allah is talking about knowledge, and as I said, mercy is associated with knowledge.... But why is Allah talking about forgiviness? Why did he use the word ٱلْغَفُورُ . Forgiveness is an attribute that affects human beings... If there is no mention of human beings, there is no need for forgiveness... Have you every forgiven your car? Have you ever forgiven your lunch box? You hit your foot at the corner of a door, later do you go back to it and say... I forgive you door! No... because forgiveness is associated only with human beings... You will forgive your friend, your brother, your sister...

So the presence of this word غَفُورٌۭ means that this ayah, is in fact, talking about human beings... it's just not literal, it's implied.... When a farmer hears these words that... Allah know what enters into the earth, what does he think of? Seeds!  and when he hears the words... What comes out, he thinks of what? A plant! But if we take a deeper look... we human beings will also enter the earth one day.. and we too will come out of the earth one day... on the day of judgment...

Allah says... He knows what enters into the earth and comes out of it.... Now this could refere to the seeds and plants, but it could most certainly refer to the human beings as well... Because they too will enter the earth one day and come out of it. If you look at the next part of the ayah he says...
وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا
He knows what's coming down from the sky and what rises up to it. From the farmers perspetive what is that? Rain! But from the perspective of human beings it is revelation... the word of Allah coming down from the sky. Now once the revelation comes down, and people hear it, they either act according to it.... or they don't act according to it, so what's going back up to the sky? Their deeds! Their deeds! Their actions for evaluation.... Let's do that again...

مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا
Human beings enter the earth when they die.... human beings come out of the earth on the day of judgment
وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا
Revelation coming down from the sky.... and human deeds and actions rising back up to Allah for evaluation...

Now... at the time of death, people are in need of Allah's mercy because death is a very difficult time.. But when they are raised from the ground, they will be in need of Allah's forgiveness.. On the day of judgment we will all need Allah's forgiveness.. So mercy first and forgiveness second.

What comes down from the sky again? Revelation... and revelation is Allah's mercy...  And what goes up to the sky again? Human actions.... which need forgiveness... Mercy first , forgiveness second ... Subhan Allah! The only place in the Quran where mercy is first, forigiveness is second.... SubhanAllah!

You see? Very very accurate use of words... precisely in their place... each word precisely connected to what's been said before.... precisely connected to what coming later...

I'll tell you something very interesting.... And this is as a side note... I found this very interesting, that when you look at the study of Quran done, historically..... you'll see that the initial theme of the study of Quran was اعجاز القرآن . the miracles of Quran.... based on these facts that I shared with you, they would say... So this proves that the Quran is the message of Allah, because human beings are not capable of this kind of perfect and precise conversations.... But later we see this theme change from اعجاز القرآن to simply جمال القرآن . The beauty of Quran... The scholars started saying we don't need to prove it anymore, let's just explore it's beauty... So the theme today has become جمال القرآن۔ ......

Anyway, so this is one profound example, in not just the linguistic dimension of the miracles of Quran, but also the dimension of imagination...
Now let's look at another one... I showed you placement of words, and choice of words.... at a very micro level in the previous sessions. We zoomed into a single ayah and examined the precision and accuracy of Quran's words. Now this one that I want to show you, is perfection at the MACRO level... We zoom out for this one, from the level of ayah... into the level of Surah...

Now you know that the Quran was not revealed in the order that we see in the Quran... The first ayat that was revealed was

ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ‎
and the last ayat revealed was
ٱلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِى وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ ٱلْإِسْلَمَ دِينًۭا
but this is not the order we find in the Quran. The first ayat we find in the book is
بسم اللہ الرحمن الرحیم
of surah fateha and the last ayat is
من الجنۃ و الناس
of Surah Al-Naas

So the order we see in the Quran is not the order that it was revealed in. So what happened, is that as these passages of ayats were being revealed..... Rasul Allah was also instructing and teaching Maulana Ali the order of these passages, the order in which they need to be placed... Which passage of ayaats goes in which Surah. So imagine..... that these ayaats are actually water, coming out of a hose at different times... and Rasul Allah is holding the hose. And he has 114 buckets before him which represent the 114 surah. Now as the batch of water is coming out, he is instructing Maulana Ali that which batch goes in which bucket. You understand? So over 23 years you can imaging these surahs being filled with ayaats simultaneously, not one after the other...

Now Surah AlBaqarah is the second and the largest surah in the Quran, and it is a Madani surah which means that it was revaled to the prophet when he was in Medina. History shows us that the greater part of Al-Baqarah was revealed during the first two years of the Holy Prophet's life at Madinah. The smaller part was revealed at a later period of his life. And in between many other surahs and passages were revealed.

Now another thing you have to understand is that when we think of ayats, we think in terms of numbers. You know ayat 53 of surah Baqarah, ayat 6 of surah Fateha.... WE can talk about ayats using this number scheme... But the arabs of that time, they didn't think in terms of numbers.... They didn't go to each other and be like... Bro! did you hear the ayats 2:53 to 2:75? Was that cool or what? Oh Yeah man! that was so awesome!....  No!... All they remembered, were the ayats itself... they didn't have the concept of ayat numbers... because the ayats we documented later on by Maulana Ali during the time of Hazrat Usman. Because remember, this conversation, is an oral tradition, it's not a writtent tradition. It's spoken word... that was later written. Not written word that was spoken by Rasul Allah. And you see, because of this fact, what I'm about to tell you becomes SO SO awesome....

In Surah Baqarah, Allah says..

وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَكُمْ أُمَّةًۭ وَسَطًۭا
We have made you a middle nation. the word وسط in Arabic, just like any other word, has several different meanings. One meaning of the word is something that is exactly in the middle, something right in the center. It liternally means to be in between فَوَسَطْنَ بِهِ جَمْعًا ‎....As Allah talks about the horse that penetrates right in the middle of the army legions in Surah Aadiyaat.
Another meaning of the word وسط is when you are weighing something on a scale, and both sides of the scale are equally balanced... neither side is tipped in any direction, and they meet right in the middle, it's called وسط . And that's why وسط  also means "Just". So Allah says

وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَكُمْ أُمَّةًۭ وَسَطًۭا

Who is this ayat talking about? It's talking about our ( ائمۃ علیھم السلام). Because every Imam is the most just man during his time.

So one meaning is middle, Another meaning is just...

But in ancient arabic there is another meaning of the word وسط. It serves as the root word of واسطۃ which means a "link". Something that links 2 things exactly from the center is called واسطۃ ... So Rasul Allah, was the link or the wasita between Allah's message and us humans in terms of words. And Maulana Ali , and the Imams are the wasita, the link between Rasul Allah and us in terms of meaning. You understand?

So the word أُمَّةًۭ means nation, .... and nation refers to the group of Imams that have come in this world from the beginning of time and will continue to do so till the end of time... Allah says...  that among all human beings, we have made the Imams the most just. Because they will always keep the scale right in the middle. They will never tip the scales in any direction. Do you understand?


Does anyone know how many ayats Surah Baqarah has? 286. In Surah Baqarah, Allah says...
وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَكُمْ أُمَّةًۭ وَسَطًۭا
Wast means something that is right in the middle, right in the center. Do you know what ayat number this is? It is ayat 143. How many Ayas in Surh baqarah? 286. What is the half of 286? 143 SubhanAllah! In exactly the middle ayah of Surah Baqarah Allah speaks of a middle nation. SubhanAllah!


The next one I want to give you is based on research that was done by a man named Tariq Ibn Muhammad AlSwedaan. He did a lecture series on the miracle of Quran in Arabic in Kuwait... Very interesting paper... He showed over 50 miracles of the Quran scientifically..  I want to share with you the word count in the Quran that he identified during his research... This is not something he set out to do initially, but then as we was doing his research, he was listing the number of times a word occurs in the Quran, and he started listing them... And to his amazement, he found these equals that correspond to each other perfectly....
I have personally verified every one of these... I am a software engineer by profession... So I imported the entire quran in a database, and split each ayah into its words, and then ran word count comparisons, to see if what he claims is true... and SubhanAllah, I was able to match all of these counts.... So I want to share some of these miracles with you... Again keep in mind that Quran is not a written text, it's a spoken text that was revealed, piece by piece, passage by passage, in all sorts of order over the course of 23 years....

The word used for prayers صلوات  very interestingly is used only 5 times in the entire Quran.... which of course corresponds to the 5 prayers of the day
The word یوم ،  which means day is used exactly 365 times in the Quran
The word دنیا  is mentioned 115 times, and so is the word آخرۃ which means hereafter
Angles is mentioned 99 times in the Quran, and the word devil is mentioned 99 times in the Quran
Life and death are mentioned equal number of times in the Quran. Both 145 times
People and messengers are mentioned equal number of times... Why? Because who did the messengers come for? people!
Zakat and barakah is mentioned equal number of times
Man and woman is mentioned equal number of times. 24 times each
Day and night is the same number of times
Intellect and light mentioned the same number of times
The prophet Muhammad's name is mentioned 4 times, and the word shareeah is mentioned 4 times in the Quran.

So there are these terms that are related and mentioned equal number of times... but again, this is a recited word... this was something that was spoken by Rasul Allah... and to have this kind of balance... it's just not possible, even with writtent text, let alone spoken words.

Now here is one that I find very cool
The word Iblees (which is the name of shaitaan) is mentioned 11 times, and seeking refuge from him is mentioned 11 times... And do you know there are 2 surahs that are recited to seek refuge against shaitaan. Anyone knows what they are? Naas and Falaq... Do you know how many ayaat in them collectively?
Let's count...
Iblees mentioned 11 times, seeking refuge mentioned form him 11 times and the 2 surahs that are specifically dedicated to seeking refuge from him, have 11 ayats collectively between them.... SubhanAllah!!

The next one I want to share with you is SO SO SO amazing... I selected this today also because of what Hassan asked in the last session about how come this text is not poetry... because it does have a rhyme scheme.... So like I said last time, that the Quran is prose, not poetry... It is prose that sounds like poetry because it has a rhyme scheme. But just having a rhyme scheme does not make it a poetry... There are various rules that apply to poetry, especially Arabic poetry... and rhyming is just one of them... There are 15 different meters, called وزن  for Arabic poetry, which has to do with the length of syllables. So in Arabic Sher, each line (called a bayt) is divided into two halves, and this pair has to meet those rules... They're basically comprised of long and short syllables that occur in a specific pattern. Think of it like the beats of a music, there is a rhythm, a pattern... and so there are are 15 different kinds of patterns in Arabic poetry..

Tawil, Kamil, Wafir, Rajaz, Hajaz, Basit, Khafif,

It's actually very interesting.... So any arabic poetry that you find, it'll follow one of these 15 syllabel patterns... The key is that the syllabel pattern has to be the same in the pair... It doesn't matter how many words are used in each half, the syllabel pattern in each bayt, or verse, must be exactly similar to the syllabel pattern in the second verse.
I'll give you an example...

When Hasham Ibn Abdul Malik, who was an evil king and ruler at that time, saw how much respect the Muslims were giving to Imam Ali ZainulA'abideen during the time of Hajj.... he felt jealous... because he never got this kind of respect from the people.... So he pretendes to not recognize the Imam and asks his people who is this man? Now he knew exactly who this man was... He knew it was Imam Ibnul Imam.... But pretends to not recognize him... So there was a poet with him, his name was Farazdaq... he was actually from one of Imam Ali ZainulAbideen dua'at at the time... He immediately, spontaneously, started reciting poetry glorifying the imam... Beautiful Beautiful words...



So now even though Quran has a rhyme scheme, the syllabel patterns vary between ayats... They sound like poetry, like the most eloquent, the most beautiful poetry, but it's not poetry because it does not match any of the 15 syllabel patterns of Arabic poetry.

So the next miracle I want to show you has to do with the syllabels of the Quran... We notice that each surah has a certain rhyme scheme... the last words in an ayat rhyme, but then Allah suddenly changes the rhyme scheme in the middle of the surah, and then in some cases returns back to the previous rhyme scheme... The words of the Surah are following a certain rhyme shceme
زَكَرِيَّآ، خَفِیَّا، شَقِیَّا، وَلِیَّا، رَضِيًّۭا، نَبِیَّا، سَمِيًّۭا

This rhyme scheme is from Surah Maryam...
ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبْدَهُۥ زَكَرِيَّآ
‏إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ نِدَآءً خَفِيًّۭا
‏قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى وَهَنَ ٱلْعَظْمُ مِنِّى وَٱشْتَعَلَ ٱلرَّأْسُ شَيْبًۭا وَلَمْ أَكُنۢ بِدُعَآئِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّۭا
وَإِنِّى خِفْتُ ٱلْمَوَلِىَ مِن وَرَآءِى وَكَانَتِ ٱمْرَأَتِى عَاقِرًۭا فَهَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّۭا
‏يَرِثُنِى وَيَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ ۖ وَٱجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّۭا
‏يَزَكَرِيَّآ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَمٍ ٱسْمُهُۥ يَحْيَىٰ لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّۭا

and the ayats go on and on and the words at the end of the ayaats keep rhyming and rhyming and rhyming until you reach ayah number 34

ذَلِكَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۚ قَوْلَ ٱلْحَقِّ ٱلَّذِى فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ
Everything was rhyming perfectly so far, and now from here the rhyme scheme changes... 

‏مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدٍۢ ۖ سُبْحَنَهُۥٓ ۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمْرًۭا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ
‏وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى وَرَبُّكُمْ فَٱعْبُدُوهُ ۚ هَذَا صِرَطٌۭ مُّسْتَقِيمٌۭ
فَٱخْتَلَفَ ٱلْأَحْزَابُ مِنۢ بَيْنِهِمْ ۖ فَوَيْلٌۭ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن مَّشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
‏أَسْمِعْ بِهِمْ وَأَبْصِرْ يَوْمَ يَأْتُونَنَا ۖ لَكِنِ ٱلظَّلِمُونَ ٱلْيَوْمَ فِى ضَلَلٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
‏وَأَنذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْحَسْرَةِ إِذْ قُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ وَهُمْ فِى غَفْلَةٍۢ وَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
‏إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَرِثُ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْهَا وَإِلَيْنَا يُرْجَعُونَ

So why is that? Is there any sort of logic behind this change or is it just random? As we know Quran is perfect in its form, everything is precisely in its place.... every word and its position in an ayah has a specific purpose...  so obviously it's not possible for the rhyme scheme to be just random..
Now if you later read the translation of the ayahs that had the first rhyme scheme, you'll notice that Allah is narrating the stories of the prophets.... So he uses a particular rhyme scheme for that...  As soon as the subject changes, the rhyme scheme changes...
You know how in literature, when you want to start a new thought you start it in a new paragrpah, with an indentation... Quran does not have paragraphs in its surah, it's way of expressing a change in subject is a change in the rhyme scheme...

So in this Surah Maryam, Allah is talking about the prophets using the rhyme scheme of
زَكَرِيَّآ، خَفِیَّا، شَقِیَّا، وَلِیَّا، رَضِيًّۭا، نَبِیَّا، سَمِيًّۭا
Then as soon as the subject changes, and Allah talks about himself, as the true lord and the only one worthy of worhsip, and how the disbelievers are in great loss... as soon as Allah switches to this subject the rhyme scheme changes...
يَمْتَرُونَ,   فَيَكُونُ,   مُّسْتَقِيمٌۭ,  عَظِيمٍ,  مُّبِينٍۢ,   يُؤْمِنُونَ,  يُرْجَعُونَ

Do you see that? As soon as the subject changes, the rhyme scheme changes.... so that the listner could know that now a new subject is being talked about... But whats So interesting, so amazing, is that when Allah returns back to the old subject, when he returns to talking about the prophets again, he picks up the same old rhyme scheme again... In the next ayah notice...

وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَبِ إِبْرَهِيمَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقًۭا نَّبِيًّا

Back to the old rhyme scheme, because he's talking about prophets again...

شَيْـًۭٔا ‎, سَوِيًّۭا ‎, عَصِيًّۭا ‎, وَلِيًّۭا ‎

A word that is not poetry... a word that is dealing with very very serious subjects, but yet for the listner  who is listening carefully knows that the subject is one because the rhyme scheme is one. And as soon as the rhyme scheme changes, the subject has advanced to something else... and when the rhyme returns, it was because that tangent was necessary to complete the original subject.... SubhanAllah! Show me another document that does that! Consistently through out its text...  Those of you who know surah AnNaba

عَم يَتَسَآءَلُونَ
‏عَنِ ٱلنَّبَإِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
‏ٱلَّذِى هُمْ فِيهِ مُخْتَلِفُونَ
‏كَلَّا سَيَعْلَمُونَ ‎
‏ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَيَعْلَمُونَ

Subject changes...

‏أَلَمْ نَجْعَلِ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِهَدًۭا
وَٱلْجِبَالَ أَوْتَادًۭا
‏وَخَلَقْنَكُمْ أَزْوَجًۭا
‏وَجَعَلْنَا نَوْمَكُمْ سُبَاتًۭا
‏وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلَّيْلَ لِبَاسًۭا

SubhanAllah! One subject is done, the next one has its own rhyme scheme...

And sometimes you will have long passages with the same exact rhyme scheme with something interjecting in between, that doesn't fit the rhyme scheme... You know why?  because that tangent is necessary for the rst of it and you have to pay intention.. Allah will say

لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ
‏وَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِٱلنَّفْسِ ٱللَّوَّامَةِ
‏أَيَحْسَبُ ٱلْإِنسَنُ أَلَّن نَّجْمَعَ عِظَامَهُۥ
‏بَلَىٰ قَدِرِينَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّسَوِّىَ بَنَانَهُۥ
‏بَلْ يُرِيدُ ٱلْإِنسَنُ لِيَفْجُرَ أَمَامَهُۥ
‏يَسْـَٔلُ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ

Subject changes....

‏فَإِذَا بَرِقَ ٱلْبَصَرُ
‏وَخَسَفَ ٱلْقَمَرُ
‏وَجُمِعَ ٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ
‏يَقُولُ ٱلْإِنسَنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَيْنَ ٱلْمَفَرُّ
‏كَلَّا لَا وَزَرَ
‏إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ ٱلْمُسْتَقَرُّ
‏يُنَبَّؤُا۟ ٱلْإِنسَنُ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بِمَا قَدَّمَ وَأَخَّر

Subject changes

‏بَلِ ٱلْإِنسَنُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِۦ بَصِيرَةٌۭ
‏وَلَوْ أَلْقَىٰ مَعَاذِيرَهُۥ

Then a small tangent, because Allah needs to tell us something which is important to understand what's coming ahead... So he changes the rhyme scheme for just one ayah...
‏لَا تُحَرِّكْ بِهِۦ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِۦٓ

And then returns back to the original subject at the beginning of the surah

‏إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا جَمْعَهُۥ وَقُرْآنَهُ
‏فَإِذَا قَرَأْنَهُ فَٱتَّبِعْ قُرْآنَهُ
ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا بَيَانَهُۥ

SubhanAllah! A rhyme scheme that tracks the subject matter.... Explain to me how is this possible with spoken conversation. AllahuAkbar!









 









 






















































 

Happy Mothers day to all moms!
AlHamdulillah! We've reached this final dimension of miracles of the Quran... It's amazing how time flies... today is our 10th session MashaAllah!

One of the first miracles I started with, was the structural miracle of Quran... We've understood miracle or the arabic word اعجاز  as an act that renders someone incapable of a response... And I discussed at length as to how, and why a miracle is something that you experience, and it is not something that you necessarily see... In contrast, a magic trick is something that you see... but a miracle is an experience that you have THROUGH something that you see or hear...
And I said that the ultimate purpose of a miracle has always been to challenge mankind....  In fact.... Allah Himself challenges mankind...
that if you think this Quran is not the word of Allah, then produce a surah similar to it...
وَإِن كُنتُمْ فِى رَيْبٍۢ مِّمَّا نَزَّلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍۢ مِّن مِّثْلِهِۦ
But what's truly incredible is what Allah says after that
وَٱدْعُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَدِقِينَ
Once you've come up with something that YOU think is similar to what my messenger has produced, then invite your OWN people to be the judge of it.... Now think about that for a second...  I'm a software developer by profession. If I challenged you to write a better software than me, woldn't I like to be the final judge of what you come up with? And if I cannot be the judge, wouldn't I at least want to setup a panel of unbiased judges...  who don't know me or my opponent........ That sounds logical right?

But Allah doesn't say, once you've produced something similar to what my messenger is telling you, bring it back to me so that I can judge... or even bring it to Rasul Allah so HE can judge... instead Allah says...  وَٱدْعُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَدِقِينَ
He says call your OWN people to be the judge of it... Now who is Allah speaking to in this ayah...  who is He challenging? Disbelievers right? So who would these people invite to be the judge... disbelievers like themselves, right? They will invite people that are already biased against Allah and His messenger...  Allah, says..... call them... call those disbelievers... and let them be the judge... You'll see that they too will judge against you! SubhanAllah... Now that is confidence....  But then Allah doesn't stop there... he goes a step further and says...
فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا۟ If you are not able to do it... وَلَن تَفْعَلُوا۟ and you will CERTAINLY not be able to do it.....
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلنَّارَ ٱلَّتِى وَقُودُهَا ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱلْحِجَارَةُ ۖ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكَفِرِينَ
Then be ready for the hell fire that will be fueled by disbelievers and stones, that has been especially prepared for the disbelievers...

Allah didn't just say believe in this book because I'm saying it is a miracle... He said you try to come up with something better, something more perfect... But If you can't... and I'm telling you can't, because I'm the one who created you... I know what you can do and what you cannot do... I know that you will never be able to creat any text, any prose, any poetry that can match the Quran.... Not in any of its dimension... whether its linguistic excellence, or structure, or scientific reasoning... I'm telling you that the best of the best human speech, cannot compare to the Quran in its precision, its perfection, its eloquence, its imagery, in how concise it is.. So trust me... If you don't trust me, then try your best... Have it judged by your own people.... But even after all this you don't believe... then I will fuel my hellfire with you... SubhanAllah!

What's interesting is that Allah compares such disbelievers with stones... he says ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱلْحِجَارَةُ   ... disbelievers and stones.. Why the comparison with stones? Because nothing can penetrate a stone...... Allah says that if after all the evidence I've presented to you, if you still don't believe, then you are nothing more than a dead stone... Infact Allah says in a different place that Your hearts are WORSE than stones ....

ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَلِكَ فَهِىَ كَٱلْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةًۭ
Even harder than stones...

Now we've seen many examples from all these different dimensions... and they're all mind blowing... but what I'm going to show you today... in my personal opinion.... it's simply the most incredible miracle of all..... To me personally, this particular dimension was.... like standing next to Moosa (AS) and watching him split the sea before my very two eyes.... And this is the structural miracle of the Quran....

See the thing with miracle is....  I can't tell you that this particular dimension is the ultimate miracle of Quran... Because I may experience the miracle through one dimension, but you may experience it through another.... The Quran is not a miracle because of MY understanding... it's a miracle because it is beyond my understanding... But this was definitely the one that made a believer out of me.... When I experienced this miracle, I remember, I was in a public library in NY studying and researching the Quran, and I was so overwhelmed by Allah's majesty and his glory that I needed to fall in Sajda....
I'm not someone who likes to stand out in a crowd... or draw attention to myself... I'm always the one hiding in the shadows... But I remember... when I experienced this miracle...... I was so overwhelmed... I just had to place my head on the ground in Sajda... I just couldnt' control myself... I was so overpowered by that feeling....  So I just got off my chair and fell in Sajda and started crying... It was such an incredible feeling... In that moment I didn't care who is around me, who is looking at me... is it proper, improper... I just had to submit myself to Allah... And for the first time I realized, what Namaaz is supposed to feel like...
I hope and pray that Allah blesses us all with this same feeling and experience InshaAllah ta'ala... because I have never ever felt that emotion again.. before or after that day... but I really wish for it...

So todays miracle is related to the structure of Quran... If you remember, I started off with the structure of the Quran as a miracle.... We talked about the apparent structure of the Quran previously, rather lack of structure... But I'm going to very quickly repeat it, for the benefit of those who were not present in that session....  because you can't really appreciate what I have to show you today, without first appreciating the fact that how unique the Quran is, that even in it's external appearance, it doesn't fit any existing literary structure....

So we know that the Quran is the message of Allah delivered in the words of Rasul Allah... In its final form, the Quran is a book, a kitaab, as Allah says....

ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ نَزَّلَ ٱلْكِتَبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ

Now the last time when we compared the structure of Quran, as a book, with any other book in the world.... we found some remarkable distinctions. Every book we know normally has a table of contents, chapters, paragraphs, lines, an index at the end, right?

But when we look at the Quran's structure, we see that the way the Quran organizes and categorizes its content is very unique... We see that it has something called Surahs... It has a total of 114 surahs... Now the word Surah, is translated in English as what? Chapter! So we have Chapter 1 (Fateha), Ch2 (Baqara), Ch3 (AaleImran), Ch4 (Nisaa)...  etc
Now you tell me! In every other book that we know, Ch4 is usually based on Ch 3? Is it not? 
Once you read Chapter 3...  do you find the exact same thing repeated again in Chapter 4 ? and then again in Chapter 5 and then again in Chapter 6? No it's not! There is no repetition. When reading any book we normally find, that there is some sort of logical and sequential progression across all chapters.

But does the Quran repeat itself across multiple chapters? Yes it absolutely does! ...... Even though it has stated the story of Moosa (AS), in chapter 2, it does it again in Ch 3, again in Ch 4, and again in Ch 5. There are over 70 passages in the Quran just related to Moosa (AS), and they are all over the place, in almost every single chapter...... Not only that... we also find that these stories are not in any chronological order. Quran doesn't talk about the birth of Moosa (AS) in ch 2, then his childhood in Ch3, then his adulthood his Ch4.... No! It starts with His adulthood, then his prophethood, then his birth... it's in all kinds of order, all over the place!

Then we also see that in Quran, numerous topics are covered with sudden switches from one topic to another and then back to the original subject again. But that doesn't happen in the books that we read.... Imagine reading a bed time story book to your child that goes like this....

The fairy God mother waves her magic wand over Cinderella and her clothes turn into a magnificent gown... And the fox says to the 3 little pigs, if you don't open the door, I'll huff and puff and I'll blow your house down... So red riding hood says... wow gradma such big eyes you have... and so the fairy god mother warns Cinderella, that the magic will end at midnight!

I seriuosly doubt this story book will make it to the best sellers list... right? Because it wouldn't make sense... We don't have different stories, within the same chapter, organized this way... So then even though we call these surahs "chapters"..... they're not really "chapters", are they? Because what WE call a chapter in a story book is something else, and what Quran calls a Surah is something else. In a story book chapter, what always happens is that the chronology moves forward, in a particular order, but not in the Surah. So you cannot compare the surah to a chapter. SURAH is Quran's own proprietary term. There is no such thing as a Surah in any other book.

Now let's dig deeper... What does a Surah contain? Ayaats! What is the word "Ayah" translated as in English? Verse! Now where do we hear the word verse outside of Islam? What comes to mind when we hear the word verse. Poetry, Songs, even the bible. Now the Quran is very adamant in letting us know that this content is definitely not poetry:
وما علمنہ الشعر و ما ھو بقول شاعر
وما علمنہ الشعر و ما ینبغیلۃ۔ ان ھو الا ذکر و قرآن مبین
We did not teach you poetry! This Quran is not poetry. So the last thing we as Muslims should be thinking, when we heart the word "verse", is that this is poetry. And the last thing we should be thinking is that this content of the Quran has been plagurized from the bible. But the 2 things that come to mind when you hear the word verse, is poem and bible.

So, can we think of an ayah as a sentence? No! Because a sentence is defined as
a grammatical unit of one or more words that expresses an independent statement, and that typically has a subject as well as a predicate

Now in the Quran, we find there is one single word that is a complete ayah? Al-Rehman! YaSeen! Just one word that is a complete Ayah! Have you every read a sentence that just said "Magnificent" or just "Beneficient"... No! That can be a title or a heading... but cannot be considered a sentence...  But in the Quran, a single word can be a complete ayah.

Then, we also find that multiple ayats can join together to form one complete sentence.
الحمد للہ رب العالمین، الرحمن الرحیم مالک یوم الدین
Technically, these are three ayats.... but grammatically just one sentence. In the previous example an ayah was just one word... Now we see  3 separate ayats ... but grammatically one single sentence. You don't have this in any other language. And then, there are entire passages, entire paragraphs dedicated to explaining an entire subject, but it is just one single ayah...



9 different sentences, but one single ayah...

So you cannot confine an ayah to any existing literary form. It is completely unique. No other book in the world has anything similar to an ayah. Just like the word Surah, the word Ayah is a proprietary term too.... Copyrights of Allah (AWJ)! There is ONLY one word you can use to describe an ayah and that is the word "Ayah".

So you see how unique the structure of Quran is? It is unlike any other book in the world.

Then let's take a look at the categorization of Quran's content... Is there any order in the way it's content is structured? Is there any chronological order? Are the Meccan surahs first and then the Madani Surahs? No! They're all mixed in together. Is the shortest Surah first and the longest surah last? Or is the longest Surah first and the shortest Surah last? No! So we see that it's not going by size order, it's not going by subject order, it's not going by chronological order, stories are not going in any sequence..... Every other book in the world has some order, a sequence, a progression, but not the Quran...... This led the orientalist to conclude, after researching the Quran in every possible way, that the Quran has no order... it's chaos (NA)

But we know that can't be true... because we see that there is nothing chaotic about the Quran... So there has to be a structure... The reason the orientalist could not identify an order for the Quran is because,  just like the surah, and the ayah, it has its own unique order....  unlike the order we find in any other book. And it's an order that cannot ever be replicated with this kind of perfection....

So let's start looking at the order now...
 


Now before I get into it, I want to remind you all.... that the Qur’an was originally delivered to its audience, in the form of speech through recitation.... there was absolutely no editorial process in place... many of the verses were even revealed on the spot.... sometimes as a response to unexpected questions and challenges that were brought forward to Rasul Allah from both believers and non-believers. I want you to also remember that the Quran is made up of 6,236 ayaats, that were gradually revealed over a period of 23 years.... sometimes ascomplete surahs, and sometimes in large passages..  So for a completely spontaneous, spoken text, comprising of over 6000 ayaats, spread over a period of 23 years, you would expect its structure to be extremely incoherent. Wouldn't you? But what we will soon see InshaALlah, is that the Quran, and the Surahs in the Quran have a miraculously coherent strucutre..... a structure that is completely and entirely beyond any human possibility...

I also want to say that is not something very easy to understand, because we are not conditioned to think in this way... I'm going to try my best to make it as simple and clear as possible, but this will require some reflection on your part as well..... So, when I pause and ask if you have any question... please don't hesiteate... You shouldn't feel bad if you don't understand... this is not an easy topic... I'll be happy to repeat it as many times as you need me to... I've also created lots of diagrams for you to make it easy... I hope and pray to Allah (AWJ) that He makes it easy for us all to understand this today...


So then what is this remarkable structure of the Quran??
The Quran's structure is actually symmetrical... It has a circular composition.... also called a "Ring Composition"....  What that means is,  that there is a very clear correspondence, a very clear corelation, between the beginning of a surah and the end of the surah....


So imagine you cut out a perfect circle from a sheet of paper... and then you fold it in half on its center....  You'll see that every single point on the right side will meet the exact same point on the left side... Or in other words I can say point A on one side will mirror point A on the opposite side... Point B will mirror point B and so on...
