I'll give you a very simple example to make this clear... In the English language, or any language for that matter... we have a stucture that fits this formula... It's called a palindrome... A palindrome is a word that reads the same, whether you read it forward or you read it backwards.. like BOB, MADAM,  RACECAR... my khala AFIFA, ... A palandrome has the same symmetrical structure..

You can see, the word RACECAR spells the same whether you spell it forwards or backwards... And this is the structure of the Quran... The entire Quran is structured as a palandrome...

So if you take any surah, and split it in half, you'll see that the first half, will exactly mirror the second half...... This is how every single Surah in the Quran is organized.... SubhanAllah!!
Now this mirroring in certain surahs are in the literal senese... in others it is by meaning... but they're all structured the same way... in a ring composition... Isn't that amazing??


Let's assume a surah has 7 ayaats. Not lets assign an alphabet to each individual ayah... So the first ayah is "A" , second is "B", third is "C" , fourth is "D".... Now in this case, which alphabet will be the middle ayah? or the center of this circle? It would be "D", right? Because you have 3 ayaats before D and 3 ayaats after D...  So what we'll find is that the ayaats before D in any surah, will always mirror the ayats after D in perfect symmetry....



Now we can replace these variables with Ayats or we can replace them with topics...
If we replace them with ayaats, then ayah A on the left will mirror Ayah A on the right.. Ayah B on the left will mirror Ayah B on the right and so on....
If we replace these variables with topics, then topic A on the left will mirror topic A on the right.. topic B on the left will mirror topic B on the right and so on....


So now let's take a look at one of the surahs of Quran.... We'll start from the outer most level... which is the TOPIC level... I've selected Surah Al-Baqarah for our case study... The reason I chose this surah is because it has a large surface area... that makes it easy to analyse....

Hassan...

We know Surah Baqarah is made up of 286 verses. When you study this surah, you'll find that the entire content of the surah can be divided into 9 distinct topics... I'm not going to go into the details of each of these topics because that's going to become a very long discussion.... All I want to do for our discusion today, is show you this circular pattern....




I've listed the ayats for you in paranthesis, and I encourage everyone to actually check it out when you get time...

But if you look at this image... what is the first topic? What's the last topic? What's the second topic? What's the second last topic?.... so you see what I'm talking about? The structure of Quran is circular.... If I rearranged this list to fit into a ring composition, it will look something like this....


Do you see how the first half aligns perfectly with the second half? SubhanAllah! How amazing is that??

So let's discuss for a bit, why Allah chose this circular structure... A circle is a very special geometric shape in our religion.... especially in the creation of this universe... we all know that a circle exists because of its center.... If there is no center... there is no circle... All of Allah's creation is based on this one shape... You see how everything in nature is circular.... The earth is circular.... it's rotation is circular...  all processes within nature are cyclic... seasons repeat in a cycle.. all planets are circular, their orbits in which they rotate is circular, and as they rotate, they rotate on their axis, which is also circular... day and night repeat constantly in a cycle...  namaaz is cyclic... fasts repeat in a cycle... tawaaf around the kaaba is circular... right?

There are actually taveel and haqaiq behind all of these geometric shapes.... But I don't have permission to get into that... So I'll just try to explain it from its apparent form...

So why did Allah choose this shape? Well one reason.... is that one of the attributes of a circle is that it is never ending... it's a loop....  It starts at a point, reaches its end and then starts all over again....

Now listent to this carefully.... talking about the 4 seasons... If you think of all 4 seasons as one entity.... I can say that season begins as an infact with spring, then becoms young with summer, then starts getting old with fall, then finally dies in winter. And then a new spring begins and goes through the same cycle again... In us hmans, a child is born, becomes young, grows old and dies, and then another generation takes its place... the legacy of the previous generation continues in future generations... So each period can be considered as an independent circle... So a child completes the circle of childhood... once that circle ends, the circle of youth begins, that culminates into the beginning of manhood, then the cycle of old age... So the advantage of this circular structure is that it produces growth... It moves one from weakness to strength and then back from strength to weakness so that a next cycle can begin...

So continuity is one of the attributes of a circle...

The word Quran literally means something that is read over and over again... So Quran also follows this cyclic pattern... Why? Because the Quran doesn't stop after reciting it once... Once you've completed its recitation, it starts all over again... It's recitation is constant... Why? Because each cycle is supposed to produce growth... and at its end, evolve into something better....
In the first iteration of Quran, we're supposed to understand it's language, in the next iteration, we're supposed to understand its taveel, in the next, it's haqaiq, in the next, its daqaiq... that's how it's supposed to proceed... That's how our deeni knowledge system works as well... it's all circular...

That's why the word "daira" is used which means circle... right? Momineen ka daira, mafaseeh ka daira, manaseeb ka daira... taveel ka daira, haqaiq ka daira...


So as I said, the entire existance of a circle is based on it's center. Now here's the most interesting part....
What did I tell you in the example of a child.... What's the center point of human life... his or her youth!!!! That's when a child is at its peek!! Every childs potential is actualized at his youth... Ideally speaking, that's when he's the strongest, that's when he's the healthiest, that's when he's the most active... Everything he accomplishes at that point, he will now utilize in his remaining life... he will carry that, and refine it through the remaining stages of his life.... So man and woman were actually created for their youth... not for their childhood, not for their old age, but for their youth.. why? So they can get married and start the next generation... give birth to a new circle... a new cycle of life... Does that make sense?

So when we study this surah... and every other surah in fact, it's almost like Allah placed the protractor on the central topic and then drew the circle... We see that the central idea of the Surah is the declaration of the new Qibla... which are ayats 142-152..... In these 10 ayats Allah basically declares that the Ka'aba is now the new Qibla for Muslims... and you can see that all remaining topics are related to this central theme.... You take out the topic of Ka'ba from this surah.... you take out the center,....  everything around it falls apart.... Why? Because everything before the center, leads to the establishment of the Ka'aba... and everything after the center is based on the existence of Ka'aba.. and there is a systematic progression in this that is so mind blowing...

It starts by talking about faith and disbelief, and it ends by talking about faith and disbelief... But when it talks about faith and disbelief the first time,  it is in the abstract sense... as a concept

ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ
وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِٱلْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ
أُو۟لَٓئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًۭى مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأُو۟لَٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ
These ayat talk about faith in the present tense. Allah says... ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ... "Those who believe" ... present tense... who are these people of the present time when the Quran is being revealed, before the establishment of the Ka'aba... the bani israeel ....
but when it ends in faith and disbelief .... after the establishment of the Ka'aba, it changes it tense to past tense..
it's no longer ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ...... وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ  أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ
instead it now says
آمَنَ ٱلرَّسُولُ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِۦ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَلَٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِ
The prophets have believed... Because the Ka'aba has now been established... and the establishment of Ka'aba means that the people have accepted faith... so the tense changes to the past tense...
Before, it was "Those who believe"... but after the establishment of Ka'aba,  now it's "the prophets who believed" آمَنَ ٱلرَّسُولُ he no longer says یؤمن ٱلرَّسُولُ
It's now آمَنَ ٱلرَّسُولُ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ
He no longer says ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ
He now says  ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَلَٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِ

Before it is deliverance of Sharia to Bani Israeel... because the Ka'ab was not yet established... but now after the establishment of ka'aba it is deliverance of Sharia to Muslims... Who are Muslims? the ones that face Ka'aba, right?
Before the establishment of Ka'aba, Ibrahim AS....  the constructor of Kaba is tested ..... after the establishment of Kaba, Muslims... the ones Ka'ba was constructed for, and the followers of Ka'ba are tested...

So we see this incredible evolution, this growth, this maturity in religion as the surah passes its mid point... SubhanAllah.... How do you do this with Spoken literature?? How do you do this? And Wallahi, I'm only sharing the tip of the iceberg with you today... There is so much depth just in this one surah... I'll need 20 more sessions to go through this... And this is just what little I have learnt.... I really believe there is no end to the depth of this ocean... just this one surah.... AllahuAkbar..
 

Another example.... those who have read and understand Surah Yousuf, they can appreciate this...



The surah begins with Hazrat Yusuf AS having a dream that 11 starts and the sun and moon are prostrating to him; The surah ends with his dream being actualized
Second topic.... his brothers plot against him.... Second last topic: his brothers learn their lesson
Third topic... the ministers wife tries to seduce him.... Third last topic: the ministers wife confesses her act
Fourth topic... Elite women of the city try to seduce him.... Fourth last topic: they confess their crime
Fifth topic.... Yousuf AS goes to prison.... fifth topic from the last.... Yousuf AS is freed from prison
Sixth topic.... The king has a dream... Sixth topic from the last... The kings dream is interpretted
 

So this was about Surah Baqarah and Yousuf... but I very quickly want to talk about a few short surahs that everyone knows and has memorized... and I feel you'll be able to appreciate this incredible symmetry so much more InshaAllah...



What does unique mean?
One who doesn't need anyone, does he need a father or a son?




What is the history of this surah? What is this surah about?

Last one


 


The topics of all surah's are structured this way... in a circular composition... And this is spoken text...
Now if you find this incredible... get this... not only are the surahs in a ring composition, there are passages within each surah that are in a ring composition.. There are these outer rings... and within it are various inner rings... Then not only are surah's in a ring composition, various ayaats are in a ring composition.... Not just ayats are in a ring composition, sentences within ayaats are in a ring composition.... Then not just one single surah is in a ring composition.... two surahs come together to form a ring composition. Then not jut two surahs come together to create a ring... two consequitive ayats come together to form a ring.... Allahu Akbar!
فبای آلاء ربکما تکذبان
 

Hamdulillah... We've finally reached the last session on this topic of Quranic miracles... In the last session, I showed you the incredible symmetry in the structure of Quran... How each surah is structured as a palindrome.... Today I want to take this a step even further... and show you this perfect symmetry at a more micro level...

I'm a bit concerned about time... because I have a lot to cover... but I don't want to rush through this because I want us all to really enjoy this incredible beauty of Quran's symmetry...

Yameena
Allah says in Surah AlMuddassir... It is the 74th surah of the Quran... He begins the surah by saying...

یا أیھا المدثر  Oh You who covers himself, قم فأنذر  Rise and warn the people,  و ربک فکبر and constantly glorify your lord.

What I want to focus everyone's attention today is on the sentence...
و ربک فکبر
this means constantly glorify your lord.

You can see it begins with the letter و.... think of this letter in this context as a Capital letter. When you start a new sentence, you begin with a capital letter... و in Arabic works the same way.... there are many ways in which the letter و is used... over 21 ways... But here let's just think of it as a capital letter used in the beginning of a new sentence. And the rest of the ayah says...

ربک فکبر
ALWAYS state the glory of your lord!

Let me tell you something about the Arabic language... In english you have a singular and you have a plural... What is the plural of house? What is the plural of mouse? :)  In Arabic you don't just have plural... you have a singular, you have a plural and then a super plural.... The plural of house is houses in english.... but you don't have a super plural like housesssss.... You have car , and then you have cars, but you don't have carsessss..... But in Arabic you do...

So the word کَبِّر  in the ayah is a SUPER plural... . It comes from the root word کِبرor   کبیر, which means big, or immense or glorious.... Also the word اکبر as in اللہ اکبر. The superlitive form of the word کبیر is کَبِّر ! which means something that is glorious, or magnificent beyond measure... And here it is used as an imperative verb (فعل أمر)... meaning Allah is actually commanding his prophet (SAS) to CONSTANTLY glorify his lord!

Now in the previous session, what was one of the attributes of a circle that I mentioned? What does circle represent? Continuity right? And this sentence, ربک فکبر  commands the  prophet (SAS) to CONSTANTLY glorify your lord! Is this clear so far?

So now let's break down these two words into its letters

ر ب ک ف ک ب ر

What do you notice about the structure of this sentence? It's a palandrome! But think about the content. Constantly state the glory of your lord! Doesn't something have to be in a loop to be constant? And when something reads forward the same way, as it reads backwards, isn't that in an endless loop?

So the words used to represent the continuous, the never ending, the constant, the ever lasting glory of Allah is stated as a palandrome... in a circular composition... SubhanAllah! How do you do that with spoken text? Allah is commanding his prophet to remind us .....that no matter where we look, no matter in which direction we look.... North to South, South to North, East to West, West to East, every where we will find Allah is being constantly glorified by all his creation! And he states this never ending cycle of Allahs glory, in a circular form! SubhanAllah!

Spoken text... no editorial process in place... revealed in passages over 23 years... and such incredible symmetry! SubhanAllah!
 

Faizah

You know Surah Yaseen right? ... Allah ta'ala says in Surah Yaseen

لَا ٱلشَّمْسُ يَنۢبَغِى لَهَآ أَن تُدْرِكَ ٱلْقَمَرَ وَلَا ٱلَّيْلُ سَابِقُ ٱلنَّهَارِ ۚ وَكُلٌّۭ فِى فَلَكٍۢ يَسْبَحُونَ

Allah speaks of all heavenly bodies in this ayah. He says...  It is not permissable for the sun to reach the moon, nor does the night overtake the day, but each, in an orbit, is rotating. Each have been assigned an orbit, and they are rotating in their orbits! What is he speaking about? Planets in space? Revolving and Rotating? When something rotates or revolves... what geometric shape are they making? Circular... Now when something is moving in a circle, does it repeat itself? Meaning, will it reach the same spot again during its motion? Yes it will, and it will continue to do so over and over and over again! That's why we see that the planets are in a loop? Right?

The first thing I want to present to you about this ayah, is that it is a miracle also in the scientific dimension. For the longest time it was believed that the sun was the center of the universe. But Allah said 1400 years ago that the sun too has an orbit. And if it has an orbit, it cannot be the center, because by definition, a center cannot move.

Now the sentence in which Allah states this circular motion is where He says.....

كُلٌّۭ فِى فَلَكٍۢ يَسْبَحُونَ
All heavenly bodies are rotating in their orbits. فَلَكٍۢ means Orbit, and  يَسْبَحُونَ means "ROTATION".  I want you to keep that in mind, OK? It's really important...

Now let's break كُلٌّۭ فِى فَلَكٍۢ into its letters
ک ل ف ی ف ل ک     +    یسبحون

First of all do you see that this is a palindrome? Now the palindrome is a circular composition, which means it must have a center..... What's the center alphabet.... So can I say that all the other alphabets are rotating around the letter Ya? Now check this out... what word did Allah use for rotation? یسبحون... what does that word start with? SubhanAllah!
The letters are rotating around the very word that Allah uses to say rotation یسبحون!!! SubhanAllah!!! How do you do that? How can human beings come up with this kind of symmetry..... in spoken text? You see... when we create palandromes, what we have to take into consideration is "spelling". And when we take spelling into consideration, we have to compromise our content! But this ayat, even though a palandrome, does not compromise on it's content one bit!

Not only Allah tells the Arabs about a reality which is being proven today.... 1400 years later...... not only is he telling us about heavenly bodies, rotating in a circle in a loop, but He does it using words that are in a loop... He tells us with letters that are themselves in rotation, and they are rotating around the very letter, that He uses to say the word rotation...... AllahuAkbar!

This kalaam, this conversation, just cannot get any more miraculous than this!

Is the most intelligent, most eloquent human being in the world capable of doing this in speech? Can the smartest man on earth spontaneously spit out palandromes with such perfection? not only without compromising the content, but actually revealing its significance?  - SubhanAllah!!! This content was recited to the people..... We are baffled even in the way it is spelled! Absolutely mesmerizing ; absolutely stunning in the perfection of its form.


Combination Palendromes

Nadia, Saleem, Imran....
In the last session we experienced this symmetrical miracle by seeing the entire surhas as a palandrome (Surah Baqara, Yousuf, Ikhlaas)... And today we've just seen it at the sentence level... So we've seen the symmetry at the macro level as well as the micro level.... Now let's see two completely different Surahs coming together to create perfect palindromes... 

There is a very beautiful Surah in the Quran called "Surah AlRehman" which is the 55th Surah of the Quran... Do you know which Surah comes immediately after it? The 56th Surah? Let's check your google skills... Let's see how fast you can find out... GO! 
Surah AlWaqiah! Surah AlRehman is the 55th Surah and Surah AlWaqiah is the 56th Surah in the Quran... 


We'll go in reverse order.... I'm going to talk about Surah AlWaqiah first.... then Surah AlRehman... OK?
This surah has 5 main subjects that I'm going to list for you in order... I want you to remember them because I'm going to ask you to say it back to me...  You can write it down if you like...OK?

So 5 topics of Surah Al-Waqiah...

The first main topic of Surah Al-Waqiah.... It describes a group of people that Allah considers as the Elite...
وَٱلسَّبِقُونَ ٱلسَّبِقُونَ أُو۟لَٓئِكَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ فِى جَنَّتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
The first of the foremost... these are the higest ranking people in the jannah... that's the first topic of this Surah... Allah talks about these poeple right in the beginning of the surah... Let's call this topic... "First and the Foremost people or Highest ranking people in Jannah"

The second topic of Surah AlWaqiah... Allah mentions another group of people وَأَصْحَبُ ٱلْيَمِينِ  people of the right hand. ... And where are these people? They are in Jannah too.... and there is a description of what they will find there...
وَأَصْحَبُ ٱلْيَمِينِ مَآ أَصْحَبُ ٱلْيَمِينِ.  فِى سِدْرٍۢ مَّخْضُودٍۢ.  وَطَلْحٍۢ مَّنضُودٍۢ.  وَظِلٍّۢ مَّمْدُودٍۢ . وَمَآءٍۢ مَّسْكُوبٍۢ
Various descriptions of what they will find in Jannah.... Now the people of the right hand are also people of Jannah, YES? So Allah first talks about  ٱلسَّبِقُونَ ٱلسَّبِقُونَ , meaning the premium package... the highest level, the best people... then he talks about the rest of the poeple who also made it onboard... So there is the first class jannah and then there is the economy class janaah as well... So 2 classes of jannah are mentioned.. but they're both good... they're both making it... they're both saved...
OK!

The third topic of Surah Waqiah.... people of the left hand... وَأَصْحَبُ ٱلشِّمَالِ . Now where are these people located? In Jahannam.. hell fire... and Allah gives a detailed description of what all is included in the hell package...
وَأَصْحَبُ ٱلشِّمَالِ مَآ أَصْحَبُ ٱلشِّمَالِ.  فِى سَمُومٍۢ وَحَمِيمٍۢ . وَظِلٍّۢ مِّن يَحْمُومٍۢ . لَّا بَارِدٍۢ وَلَا كَرِيمٍ
Allah describes the various torments these people of hell will face....  These are people of the left hand... Are the people of the left hand safe, or are they in danger? They are in danger...

How many subjects did I say the surah has? 5 subjects... So what 3 subjects have we talked about so far? We have 2 groups of people who are saved... ٱلسَّبِقُونَ ٱلسَّبِقُونَ the first and the foremost, the أَصْحَبُ ٱلْيَمِينِ people of the right hand, and then there are the أَصْحَبُ ٱلشِّمَالِ people of the left hand, who are people of the hell fire... So two good and one bad so far....


Fourth topic  ... Allah states a list of things that ONLY HE can do...... you and I cannot do... And he asks these as questions...
أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تُمْنُونَ?  ءَأَنتُمْ تَخْلُقُونَهُۥ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْخَلِقُون؟
Do you not see the seeds? Did you create the seeds or do we create them?
‏أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تَحْرُثُونَ ؟
‎ ءَأَنتُمْ تَزْرَعُونَهُۥ؟  أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلزَّرِعُونَ
Do you not see what you sow in the ground? Do you grow the plants out of the soil or do we grow them?
أَفَرَءَيْتُمُ ٱلْمَآءَ ٱلَّذِى تَشْرَبُونَ؟ ءَأَنتُمْ أَنزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنزِلُونَ؟
Don't you see the water that you drink? Do you bring it down from the skies or do we bring it down?

So the fourth subject are a list of things that ONLY Allah can do and we cannot do...  So let's give this topic a simple title... The creations of Allah that only Allah can do...

Let's start from the beginning... we have 4 topics now.... what was the first one?
First and the foremost... what was the second one
People of right hand... what was the third one
People of left hand.. what was the fourth one..
the creations of Allah that can only be Allahs creation


The fifth and final subject of this surah is....
فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِمَوَقِعِ ٱلنُّجُومِ.  وَإِنَّهُۥ لَقَسَمٌۭ لَّوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عَظِيمٌ.  إِنَّهُۥ لَقُرْآنٌۭ كَرِيمٌۭ
It is a passage about the greatness and the glory of the Quran.. It is about the Quran itself.. It is all about how the Quran is AWESOME!  It is actually one of the most beautiful ayaats that describe the power of the Quran....

So now we have 5 main subjects of Surah AlWaqiah... Repeate them to me
1. first and the foremost
2. People of right hand
3. People of left hand
4. creations of Allah
5. the greatness of the Quran..


Now what was the surah before Surah AlWaqiah... it's Surah Rehman... Now Surah Al-Rehman also has 5 subjects!
Subject Number 1:
ٱلرَّحْمَنُ عَلَّمَ ٱلْقُرْآنَ . خَلَقَ ٱلْإِنسَنَ . عَلَّمَهُ ٱلْبَيَانَ
The greatness of the Quran... for his creation Al-Insaan, taught by the intensely merciful AlRehman himself....
What was the last subject of Surah AlWaqiah?

Subject Number 2 in Surah Al-Rehman,
ٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ بِحُسْبَانٍۢ . وَٱلنَّجْمُ وَٱلشَّجَرُ يَسْجُدَانِ . وَٱلسَّمَآءَ رَفَعَهَا وَوَضَعَ ٱلْمِيزَانَ
Oh my goodness... Subject number 2 of Surah Rehman.... the creations of Allah that only Allah can create... the sun, the moon, the stars....
What was the second last subject of Surah AlWaqiah?

The first subject of Surah Rehman is the greatness of the Quran, the last subject of Surah AlWaqiah is greatness of the quran.
The second subject of Surah Rehman is creations of Allah that only he can create, the second last subject of Surah AlWaqiah is creations of Allah that only he can create


Subject Number 3 of Surah Rehman... it is about judment day and criminals that will end up in the hell fire...
سَنَفْرُغُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَ ٱلثَّقَلَانِ.  يُرْسَلُ عَلَيْكُمَا شُوَاظٌۭ مِّن نَّارٍۢ.  وَنُحَاسٌۭ فَلَا تَنتَصِرَانِ . فَإِذَا ٱنشَقَّتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ فَكَانَتْ وَرْدَةًۭ كَٱلدِّهَانِ .
فَيَوْمَئِذٍۢ لَّا يُسْـَٔلُ عَن ذَنۢبِهِ إِنسٌۭ وَلَا جَآنٌّۭ.  يُعْرَفُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ بِسِيمَهُمْ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِٱلنَّوَصِى وَٱلْأَقْدَامِ.
  هَذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِى يُكَذِّبُ بِهَا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ.  يَطُوفُونَ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَ حَمِيمٍ آنٍۢ
Detailed description of the people of hell fire...
What was the third last subject in Surah AlWaqiah? People of the left hand... people of the hell..


Subject Number 4 ...
وَلِمَنْ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِۦ جَنَّتَانِ
There are 2 jannah for people who fear Allah...  and then he describes those 2 jannah ... explains what all is included in this package...
ذَوَاتَآ أَفْنَانٍۢ . فِيهِمَا عَيْنَانِ تَجْرِيَانِ.  فِيهِمَا مِن كُلِّ فَكِهَةٍۢ زَوْجَانِ . مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ فُرُشٍۭ بَطَآئِنُهَا مِنْ إِسْتَبْرَقٍۢ .
وَجَنَى ٱلْجَنَّتَيْنِ دَانٍۢ.  فِيهِنَّ قَصِرَتُ ٱلطَّرْفِ لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنسٌۭ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَآنٌّۭ . كَأَنَّهُنَّ ٱلْيَاقُوتُ وَٱلْمَرْجَانُ . هَلْ جَزَآءُ ٱلْإِحْسَنِ إِلَّا ٱلْإِحْسَنُ
He describes these jannah at length... And then immediatel after that....

Subject Number 5...
وَمِن دُونِهِمَا جَنَّتَانِ
And in addition to that, and above and beyond that...... I have two more awesome jannah for you.. What's included in this special package?
مُدْهَآمَّتَانِ . فِيهِمَا عَيْنَانِ نَضَّاخَتَانِ . فِيهِمَا فَكِهَةٌۭ وَنَخْلٌۭ وَرُمَّانٌۭ . فِيهِنَّ خَيْرَتٌ حِسَانٌۭ.  حُورٌۭ مَّقْصُورَتٌۭ فِى ٱلْخِيَامِ.
  لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنسٌۭ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَآنٌّۭ . مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ رَفْرَفٍ خُضْرٍۢ وَعَبْقَرِىٍّ حِسَانٍۢ
BEAUTIFUL BEAUTIFUL Ayaats. InshaAllah I'd love to study Surah Rehman in detail with you in one of the future sessions

But anyway, so So he talks about jannah 2 times... the economy package first and the delux package last.... Subjects four and five...
What were the second and first subjets of Surah Waqiah... in reverse order
اصحاب الیمنand السابقون السابقون.
Subject number 1 and subject number 5, both about the higher level of jannah... Subject number 2 and subject number 4, both about a lower level of jannah... Subhan Allah!
He talks about the higest janna first in Surah Waqiah, he talks about the higest janna last in Surah Rehman
He talks about the lower janna second in Surah Waqiah, he talks about the lower janna second last in Surah Rehman...
Perfect Perfect symmetry! How do you do that with spoken text? Two completely different surahs... coming together in perfect symmetry... AllahuAkbar!

Yasmeen Hussain

My personal favorite .... Ayat Al-Kursi... This one ayah is made up of 9 sentences... OK? 9 independent sentences coming together to form one single ayah...

ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ
لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌۭ وَلَا نَوْمٌۭ
لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ
مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ
يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُم
وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍۢ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ
وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ
وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا
وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ

So 9 sentences... Now of course you've already guessed... this too is a plaindrome... So we'll just go over the translation of each one of these symmetrically.... So we'll compare the first and the last, the second and the second last, the third and the seventh and so on...

So let's start with the first and the last...

The first one...
ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ
This beautiful sentence ends with 2 of Allah's names... What are those 2 names? alHayee The living, and AlQayyoom.. the source of all establishment or the source of all maintenance, the ever watchful. Qayyoom has many meanings... Basically, everything stands, and exists and is maintained because of Allah... So not only does he create... He also keeps any eye on it! If he only created, and didn't maintain, creations would get destroyed... And you obviously first create and then maintain... So the living first and the source of all maintenance second........ ٱلْحَىُّ  first and ٱلْقَيُّومُsecond... So because He is the ultimate creator and He is the ultimate maintainer.. what does that make Him? Now join it to the 9th sentence

وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
And He is the lofty and the mighty... Lofty by virtue of creation! Mighty by virtue of maintenance....  Perfect Symmetry!

********************************************************************************************************


Now let's look at the second and 8th one......

لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌۭ وَلَا نَوْمٌۭ
Drowsiness and sleep do not even touch him...
You kids know what drowsiness means, right? You know that strange feeling you have during lectures in your classroom... that's drowsiness ! Your eyes start closing a little bit.... You're not actually sleeping but you're just about ready to enter dreamland...... that is what's called  "Sinah" in Arabic ..... and the next step after that is "Naum" which means deep sleep... First comes Sinah... then comes Naum... So these are things that creations have... .Creation gets tired, they start getting drowsy and then eventually they fall asleep ...

Before we talk about its pair sentence.. I want to tell you something about security.... Security guards have the hardest job in the world...Why?  because it's the most boring job on the planet... What do you always find the security guard doing in movies? Either staring at the CC TV monitors,  And it's the same damn picture for 12 hours.... They're staring at the blue third hand beat up toyota parked in a spot... not even a proshe or a lamburginin... not even a Mercedeze Benz......
OR they're sitting on a chair staring into space for hours and hours... It's truly the most exciting job you can ever do in your life.... Especially if you're a college student....  So what tends to happen when you're guarding something? You tend to get sleepy... You tend to get tired... That's why eventually what you always find every security guard doing is what? Sleeping!

But Allah says in the 8the sentence...
وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا
Guarding the skies and the earth, does not exhaust Allah... What does guarding bring? Drowsiness and Sleep... Sinah and Naum! But Allah says not me! I guard all my creation, but I never get exhausted... Perfect symmetry


********************************************************************************************************
 
What's the third ayah?
لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ
He owns whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth... Before I can explain this connection to you, I need you to understand the difference between 2 arabic words... مالک and مَلِک... Because it'll make you appreciate these ayats like nothing else.... مالک means an owner... مَلِک means a king... is there a difference between a king and an owner? Yes! You are the owner of you pen ... but you do not say... I am the royal sovereign king of this pen! right? That wouldn't t make a lot of sense... You understand? People can be owners of small things... you can own a car, you can own a pen, you can own a phone, a computer... but you cannot own a kingdom.. A king would have a kingdom... here's another problem...  ownership is about property! but kingdom is not specifically about property .. you're not a king over a tree, or the king over a piece of land... You are a king over all the people that live in that land..  Kingdom is about controlling people... ownership is about controlling objects ... there's a big difference...  Ownership is at the micro level where as kingdom is at the Macro level...
Allah says..  لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ
I have the ownership of everything thats in the heavens and everything thats in the earth

What is the ayah about? Ownership or kingdom? It's about ownership!   So every single thing is owned by Allah....

Now let's look at the third last sentence?
وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ
His throne extends to the skies and the earths... Allah talks about his throne.. Who has a throne? A king has a throne... So when he's talking about his throne, is he talking about ownership or kingdom?  He's talking about his kingdom! 
So you see, the previous sentence was about ownership... this sentence is about kingdom! Both together completing the picture! Because if you're just an owner, you're not a king... if you're just a king, you're not necessarily an owner... You can be the king of an island... but does that mean you own every house on that island... No! You might own your house... but are you the king of the house? No! But Allah is not only the owner of all things... He is also the king of all things... ! He is مالک and he is also مَلِک ... The مالک attribute of Allah's stated by... لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ and the مَلِک attribute of Allah stated by وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ.


********************************************************************************************************
Next one....

مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ
Who will come and make شفاعۃ with Allah unless he gives permission... Let's begin with what does the word شفاعۃ  mean? The translation I sent has a big English words Intercession! Let's make it simple ... شفاعۃ means... you got connections! You got people! It means you are about to lose your job but your uncle is the manager of the company and he comes and says... No No No... he's with me!... He's good! That's شفاعۃ .... Your dad is the chief of staff of a hospital, especially if you're in Pakistan... and you get to go in the Emergency or Casualty unit even if you just have a headache.... you get called in first! That's شفاعۃ ..... The guy with the broken ribs can wait... After all, it'll still be broken half an hour later right?

So Allah says... On the day of judgment who would dares make شفاعۃ ... Who would dare Intercede except without MY permission! So that means ONLY the people he selects, can make شفاعۃ  . Not everyone.... You understand? Allah is about to throw someone in hell... I can't just step forward and say... woa woa woa... Allah mian... .. He's with me! :))))) No... He'll throw me along with him...

So nobody will have the right to make شفاعۃ  on behalf of anyone else except the one he chooses.... And the one He has chosen is his beloved messenger Muhammad Mustafa (SA). We beg Allah to let us all qualify for his intercession on that day... because Allah says nobody else will come for you on judgment day... Not going to happen...

So this ayah is nobody having any authority unless Allah gives it to them... And the only exception is by Allah's permission... IF Allah gives permission, someone can interceed otherwise nobody can say anything on that day... Everyone clear about that?

Now let's look at its pair...

وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍۢ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ
They know nothing.. they have no power, they encompass nothing of his knowledge إِلَّا , except... بِمَا شَآءَ  of what HE wills. Both statements are about the exceptions that ONLY Allah has... In the first one, nobody can intercede EXCEPT the one who has the permission of Allah... The second statement, nobody can gain anything from his knowledge EXCEPT the one who He chooses. SubhanAllah!

Now what's left? The center statement..

يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُم

OMG! He says He knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them... He knows that everything that is coming before this statement is in perfect correlation, perfect symmetry with what's coming ahead of His statement.... AllahuAkbar!!
Right in the middle He is saying... I know what's before and I know what's coming after... He makes this statement the center of the circle... The radius of one end.... will be equal to the radius of the other end.... SubhanAllah! Who can speak like that??  The way in which Rasul Allah delivers this message... who can do that???

Again, remember, we read teh Quran as a book.. but did Rasul Allah recite like that? Did he say that look sentence number 1 and 9 and 2 and 8 and 3 and 7, and 4 and 6 are in perfect correlation to the 5th sentence? No! This is spoken word! He was just speaking the Quran... We are able to see thtese things when they were put in writing... The Quran was SO perfect even when it was delivered in speech!
When is there a greater chance of mistake... when you write something or when you say something? When you say something! Once you say it, it is very hard to take it back... especially if it's on youtube!  AllahuAkbar!
But if you're writing a nasty email... before you hit send, you can think about it and tone it down... you can rephrase things.. and finaly just say Eid Mubarak and send it...  you have a chance to take it back.. But you can't do that in spoken word... when you say something.... as soon as you've said it.... you're done! No room to edit... no room to fix it... Allah did not reveal this quran in writing... he revealed it in speech!
 

 SO this AlHamdulillah concludes our topic of the miracles of Quran... This symmetric miracle was the last one I wanted to show you.... there are SO many more ayats structured this way.... but InshaAllah, I hope I was able to deliver the most relevant ones... And I REALLY REALLY enjoyed sharing these miracles with you! AlHamdulillah!

One thing I quickly want to mention about this symmetry of Quran... Why did Allah choose a circle for his entire creation... The universe, processes, the cycles of prophets, the cycle of years, months, weeks... why did Allah choose this shape....

Well, like I said before, one of the attributes of a circle is continuitiy.... So it was to indicate the immense consistency of His nature.... Can you imagine if we had a moody Allah (نعوذ باللہ). We don't think about these things... but imagine for a second if your day depended on how Allah was feeling today.... What if he was angry today... You know His powers right? Imagine what he can do in a split second... see these are things we completely take for granted... We'll talk about this a lot more InshaAllah.... but One reason for this shape is to show the consistency of his beautiful nature...

Another reason is to indicate "LOVE".... Because a circle is encompassing.... What does a mother do, when a child runs in her arms... She wraps her arms around the child... right? Encompasses the child in her love.... The child feels safe and peaceful in the mothers embrace.... So safety is another reason for this shape... You notice how the circumfrence of a circle has no holes in it.... Once inside the circle, the child is completely protected by the love and mercy of Allah.... That's why in my first session I mentioned that if Islam could be summarized in one single word... it would be LOVE! Everything is designed to reveal Allah's love for His creation.... Yet we are made to believe that all he wants us to do is burn us in hell...

But the circle also indicates justice... You see how no matter where you draw the radius it will always be equal? So even though He is a loving Allah, his love is based on justice... He loves with justice, and is just with love... We don't subscribe to the Christian theology, that God is nothing but love... In fact even this belief of the Christians is a deviation from their own principles.... The bible clearly states that Allah is loving and merciful but he is also just.... He will never do injustice... So even we as Mulsims should never forget that... Yes he is nothing but Love... but that love includes justice.... so we should not take undue advantage of his love.... Just becausae he is all love doesn't mean He will not punish.... He most certainly will to establish his justice... but his mercy and love superceeds everything....  Even when he excutes justice, he does it with love... His love is at the root of EVERY action....

There are many other attributes of a circle... for our discussion we will InshaAllah just focus on these...



























 


Now we looked at 2 consequiteve Surahs coming together to form a perfect palandrome.. now let's look at 2 consequtive ayats that come together to form a palindrome...


Allah (سبحانہ و تعالی) says in Surah Al-Jumuah... These are the first 2 ayats

يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْمَلِكِ ٱلْقُدُّوسِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى بَعَثَ فِى ٱلْأُمِّيِّۦنَ رَسُولًۭا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَتِهِۦ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَإِن كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ لَفِى ضَلَلٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ

Those of you who read the Quran regularly must have noticed that in most Ayats Allah ends the ayah by referring to 2 of his names
غفور الرحیم، عزیزٌ حکیم ، عزیزٌ انْتقام،
You'll see that Allah mentiones 2 of his names many many times in the Quran. Always a name pair is used...  But if you notice in the first ayah of Surah Jumuah, there is something unique... Tell me what's unique about this ayah...

يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْمَلِكِ ٱلْقُدُّوسِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ

Do you notice something different here? Allah uses 4 names in the end of this ayah.... It's unique isn't it, compared to all other ayats. I want to first go over the meanings of these names with you.. I want you to take notes, as I tell you these ... I'm going to ask you to repeat these for me...

The first is Malik.... Malik means the king... means what?
Then is Al-Quddoos... It means the perfectly pure, and you can also say the source of all purity
Then Al-Azeez ... It means the Ultimate Authority
Finally there is AlHakeem .... AlHakeem has lots of meaning...  I'll just share one here, for our purpose it means the wise...

Repeat

Now who is the first Ayah talking about?
يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْمَلِكِ ٱلْقُدُّوسِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ

Everything in the heavens and the earth glorify Allah. He is the King, the Pure, The Authority, the Wise
Who is this ayat talking about? Allah!

Who is the second ayah talking about?
هُوَ ٱلَّذِى بَعَثَ فِى ٱلْأُمِّيِّۦنَ رَسُولًۭا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَتِهِۦ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ
It is He who has sent among the unlettered, a Messenger from themselves reciting to them His verses and purifying them and teaching them the Book and wisdom -
Who is this ayat talking about? Rasul Allah

This is also one of the most beautiful ayats of the Quran, because in this one ayah, Allah has summarized the entire Seerah of the prophet... (SA). The entire seerah... In this ayah Allah talks about the 4 steps his beloved messenger Rasul Allah (SA) took in order to transform society... He took 4 steps...

يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَتِهِۦ
وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ
وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ
وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ

Hmmm... where did we hear 4 before?? How many names of Allah were mentioned? And His messenger follows how many steps? Now let's take a step back and let me give you something to think about....

Imagine you live in a time let's say 4000 of years ago...  You just bought your brand new donkey and you're taking it out for a spin... and as you're travelling, you see that you are entering into a brand new kingdom. How do you know you're entering a new kingdom? At the border, how would you know this? Through signs of the kingdom! Flags? Statues! Monuments! right? You notice soldiers that bear the mark of the king... you might notice a castle...
You will see certain signs that this is a kingdom or that this place has a king! You will notice the signs... You understand? A king is known by his signs and symbols... You will not know a king unless he's wearing a crown.. If he's wearing a T-Shirt, you will not know he's a king... He has to be wearing his robe and a crown. If a king is living in a 3rd floor apratment, he's not a king ... He can call himself royalty all he wants, but he's not really a king ... He has to have a castle.... a throne... These are all signs that you are dealing with a ...? King! So the first point I want to make is that a king is known by his signs...

What was the first name of Allah mentioned? AlMalik! What does it mean? King!
Allah says in the next ayah that his messenger does 4 things... what is the first one? The first one is

يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَتِهِۦ
He reads on to the people the king's signs! How is a king known? By his signs! And the messenger tells the people his... signs! Notice his kingdom? You see those mountains over there? it's part of his kingdom! You see that sun over there? it's part of his kingdom! You seen that moon in the night? it's part of his kingdom! That's how you know you're dealing with a king.... You see the stars at night? that's the kingdom of Allah... he is known by his signs.



What was the second name of Allah in the first ayah? AlQudoos! What did it mean? I forgot! Source of all purity...
And what's the second thing the messenger does...?
وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ
He purifies them! He reads the kings signs to them, and he what? Purifies them.. You see a connection?


Now what was the third name of Allah? AlAzeez! What did it mean? Ultimate authority!
Where do laws come from? Laws come from an authority! In a house....  rules come from the parents... it does not come from the child .... because the parents have what? Authority! In a class room the rules come from the teacher ... they don't come from the students, because the teacher has what? Authority! In a country, the rules come from the government! It comes from the poeple making the laws ... It comes from the courts... not the common people... because who has the autirity? The courts do! Laws come from the autority.. Allah says..

وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ
He teaches them the book... And the word book in Arabic literature is used for "law" ... So it's like saying that he teaches them the... law! And law can only be given by the proper authority... AlAzeez is the only one who can make alKitaab. You cannot make kitaab without Al-Izza... You cannot have law without an authority... And BTW even in English literature we say .. the judge threw the book at him... that does not mean that the judge got angry and opend his skull by thrwoing a 15lb book at the plaintiff... it means that the judge used the full extent of the law against this person. He threw the book at him! Or if someone says I'm going to do this by the book... what does that mean? I'm going to do this according to the law.... that's by the book. So AlAzeez is the authority and the messenger teaches the law dictated by the authority... وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ

What was the last name of Allah mentioned in this sequence? AlHakeem! The Wise! Allahs says
وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ
He teaches them wisdom! Where does wisdome come from? It comes from the wise! 4 names of Allah in the first ayah... 4 activities of the messenger in the next ayah and they're each corelated perfectly ... and it took me 20 minutes to explain this to you... but Rasul Allah just says it....  He just says it... What human being is able to think this way... Hmmmm I just said 4 names of Allah, I should now back them up with 4 steps, 4 verbs that correlate with the previous names in the same order... Who can do that? It's beyond human capability... and RasulAllah just creates these miracles spontaneously... SubhanAllah!

We as Muslims just read through these miracles without appreciating them, we just read through these powerful observations without realizing what we just read... And wallahee if you do realize, you are just left mesmerized... AllahuAkbar! You're left overwhelmed...
